eng: When Abram was years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, I am El Shaddai. Walk with me and be trustworthy.
local: Jemanso Abram n daa ti niŋi yuma pisiwai ni awai, Duuma ta o saani, a ti yeli u, Mani n niŋi Naawunni so n niŋi kpeeŋu zaa daana. Wumi n tira ma jemani zaa, ka niŋina yaseesi n veela.

eng: I will make a covenant between us and I will give you many, many descendants.
local: M ni digili i noori, ka ti i yaŋdamta.

eng: Abram fell on his face, and God said to him.
local: Abram gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa, Naawunni yeli u.

eng: But me, my covenant is with you; you will be the ancestor of many nations.
local: M digilini i ni naŋgbanaa, maa e ni niŋi balidamta yaadaba.

eng: And because I have made you the ancestor of many nations, your name will no longer be Abram but Abraham.
local: E yuuri bi lee n niŋi Abram, ama Ibrahim, a kpali m loobira i ni balidamta yaadaba.

eng: I will make you very fertile. I will produce nations from you, and kings will come from you.
local: M ni ti i yaŋdamta, ba seesi ni niŋi nadima, ka ba seesi ba loobi balisi.

eng: I will set up my covenant with you and your descendants after you in every generation as an enduring covenant. I will be your God and your descendants God after you.
local: M ki tami naŋgbanso ni ti e ni e soosa zu yaŋseesi n ya n ti loobi balisi la, a wara saŋa ni saŋa zaa. M ni niŋi e ni e yaŋsi zaa Naawunni.

eng: I will give you and your descendants the land in which you are immigrants, the whole land of Canaan, as an enduring possession. And I will be their God.
local: Keenan tiŋgbaŋaa zaa, seela e n niŋi saanaa, m ni naag u ti e ni e yaŋsi, o niŋi ba toori saŋa ni saŋa zaa, ka m niŋi ba Naawunni.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for you, you must keep my covenant, you and your descendants in every generation.
local: Naawunni yeli Ibrahim, E gba daga e sagi n pa m naŋgbandigiligu a, e ni e soosa zu yaŋsi zaa.

eng: This is my covenant that you and your descendants must keep: Circumcise every male.
local: E ni e yaŋsi zaa daga ya sagi, a ŋmaara ya biduusi zaa yoya.

eng: You must circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it will be a symbol of the covenant between us.
local: Za zinaa wara, a daga e ŋmaara biduusi zaa ba n dogi e laanta yoya, o dabaanii daari. Gurunseesi ba n dogi e laanta, a ni seesi ya n da. N ŋa ni wusi maa naŋgbandigiligu ba m nini sunsuuniŋi.

eng: Be sure you circumcise those born in your household and those purchased with your silver. Your flesh will embody my covenant as an enduring covenant.
local: A daga ba ŋma so zaa yoori. N ŋa ni wusi niŋgbaŋaa niŋi maa, m naŋgbandigiligu maa doni wara saŋa ni saŋa zaa.

eng: Any uncircumcised male whose flesh of his foreskin remains uncircumcised will be cut off from his people. He has broken my covenant.
local: Daba zaa yoori ni bi ŋma, lala daana, ba nyisi u yi m niriba puwa, a kpali o bi pa m naŋgbandigiligu maa zu.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for your wife Sarai, you will no longer call her Sarai. Her name will now be Sarah.
local: Naawunni yeli Ibrahim, A bi daga e n lee n boona e poga yuuri Sarai, za zinaa wara, o yuuri n niŋi Saara .

eng: I will bless her and even give you a son from her. I will bless her so that she will become nations, and kings of peoples will come from her.
local: M ni nafa u, o dogi biduu ti i, ka m loobi u balidamta ma, nadima ni yi o yaŋsi puwa.

eng: Abraham fell on his face and laughed. He said to himself, Can a -year-old man become a father, or Sarah, a -year-old woman, have a child?.
local: Ibrahim gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa, ka laara, ka feera o sufu niŋi, Daba n tee niŋi yuma koowa, ka dogi biya? Saara n tee dogi, jemanso o n niŋi yuma pisiwai ŋa.

eng: To God Abraham said, If only you would accept Ishmael.
local: Ŋama zaa nyaaŋa, o boosi Naawunni, E ki ti Isimeel o niŋi m kapete dira.

eng: But God said, No, your wife Sarah will give birth to a son for you, and you will name him Isaac. I will set up my covenant with him and with his descendants after him as an enduring covenant.
local: Naawunni yeli u, Akai, e poga Saara ni dogi biduwa, e digili o yuuri Yisaaka. M ni ti naŋgbandigiligu maa ba m ninu sunsuuniŋi, a ni o yaŋsi zaa, a wara. O niŋini naŋgbandigiligu so n ka baasigu.

eng: As for Ishmael, Ive heard your request. I will bless him and make him fertile and give him many, many descendants. He will be the ancestor of twelve tribal leaders, and I will make a great nation of him.
local: M wumi e suusigu, a yi Isimeel pelli, a zu, m ni nafa u, a ti u biisi ni yaŋdamta. Ni naagi u loobi nabiisi pii ni bayi ba, ka m naa o yaŋsi maa baligu loobi nivutintaasi.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with Isaac, who will be born to Sarah at this time next year.
local: Ama m naŋgbandigiligu maa ni pa e bii Yisaaka niŋi, so Saara n ya n dogi yumini jemanaa zu.

eng: When God finished speaking to him, God ascended, leaving Abraham alone.
local: Jemanso Naawunni n togsi Ibrahim saani baasi, o gaari.

eng: Abraham took his son Ishmael, all those born in his household, and all those purchased with his silver—that is, every male in Abrahams household—and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that same day, just as God had told him to do.
local: A dindaari zaa, Ibrahim wumi n ti Naawunni, ka ŋma o bii Isimeel yoori, a ni daba zaa ni ba o laanta, a naa o gurunsi, seesi ba n dogi o laanta, a ni seesi zaa o n da.

eng: Abraham was years old when he circumcised the flesh of his foreskin.
local: Ibrahim ni ŋma o yoori, ka o yuma pisiwai ni awai.

eng: and his son Ishmael was years old when the flesh of his foreskin was circumcised.
local: Ka Isimeel mi niŋi yuma pii ni ata.

eng: That same day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised.
local: O ni o ba zaa daa ŋmani ba yoya dindaluŋko.

eng: All the men of his household, those born in his household and those purchased with silver from foreigners, were circumcised with him.
local: Daba zaa n daa n ba Ibrahim laanta daa n ŋma ba yoya, a ni o guruŋseesi ba n dogi o laanta, a ni seesi ba n da.

eng: While Amraphel was king of Shinar, Ellasars King Arioch, Elams King Chedorlaomer, and Goiims King Tidal.
local: Jemanso nadimseesi n daa n ya n tuusi taaba yi toowa niŋi. Nadima banaasi yuya n ŋa: Babilon naaba Amafel, Elasa naaba Ariyok, Elam naaba Kedolaama, a ni Goyiim naaba Tidal. Nadima banu mi yuya n ŋa: Sodom naaba Bera, Gomora naaba Besa, Adima naaba Sinaaba, Zeboyiim naaba Semeba, a ni Bela naaba. O yuyinni n niŋi Zowa.

eng: These latter kings formed an alliance in the Siddim Valley .
local: Nadima banu maa daa n laŋsi taaba, ba wa ti zabi Sidim nantaŋŋu puwa, seela n niŋi yaarim teeku la. Yuma pii ni ayi puwa, ba daa n bani naaba Kadilaama suurisi niŋi. Yuma pii ni ata daana, ba zagsi maa ba n di n ba o suurisi niŋi. Yuma piini anaasi daana, Kedolaama ni nadima seesi n ba o saani la niŋi naŋgbanluŋko wa ti zabi n nyaŋŋi Rifam dima, a yi Asiteraf Kanayim, Zuzim dima, a yi Ham, Emim dima, a yi Kiriatam paaligu niŋi, Horiti dima, a yi Idom zoori zu, a kari ba lala wa ti paa El-Paran n niŋi tambisigu paaligu la torigu. Niŋi n na, ba pori nyaaŋa ta En-Misipa, Kadesi n na la. Ba nyaŋŋi Amaleki dima, a yi ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa niŋi, a nyaŋŋi Amori dima seesi n ba Hazazon Tama la. Niŋi n na, nadima banu la niŋi naŋgbanluŋko, ba ni ba toowa zabiriba, wa ti laŋsi Sidim falimini niŋi, a leeli yaarim teeku la. Barima n niŋi Sodom, Gomora, Adima, Zeboyiim, a ni Bela nadimaa ni ba toozabiriba niŋi siriya nisaaniŋi ba zabi nadima banaasi la.

eng: against King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar, four kings against five.
local: seesi n niŋi, Elam, Goyiim, Babilon dima naaba ni Elasa, nadima banu ya n tuusi ba banaasi.

eng: Now the Siddim Valley was filled with tar pits. When the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah retreated, they fell into them; and the rest fled to the mountains.
local: Sidim falimini maa zaa daa niŋini kootaali boga, jemanso Sodom ni Gomora nadima n zora maa, niriba maa seesi li kootaali boga maa puusi, ka ba seesi zo n du zoori zu.

eng: They took everything from Sodom and Gomorrah, including its food supplies, and left.
local: Ka nadima banaasi maa ti kaasi Sodom ni Gomora dima bunso zaa nyem, haali ba bindira zaa.

eng: They also took Lot, Abrams nephew who lived in Sodom, and everything he owned, and took off.
local: Ka ba gbaa Loti, Abram sunzo biya, a ni o bunso zaa nyem gaari.

eng: When a survivor arrived, he told Abram the Hebrew, who lived near the oaks of the Amorite Mamre, who was the brother of Eshcol and Aner, Abrams treaty partners.
local: Daba yindaana, daa n chee n wa ti yeli Abram, Hiburu daba la, bunso zaa n niŋi. A jemani maa Abram daa n zinni leeli daba so ba n boona Mamiri, Amori daba, titintaasi see. Daba maa mabiisi n daa n niŋi Esikol ni Aneri, ba ni Abram daa n mari ni naŋgbanluŋko.

eng: When Abram heard that his relative had been captured, he took all of the loyal men born in his household, three hundred eighteen, and went after them as far as Dan.
local: Jemanso Abram n wumi maa ba gbaa o sunzo bii gaari, o laŋsi o laanta puu toozabiriba zaa, ba daa n ba niriba kobsita ni pii ni banii, o mari ba kari nadima banaasi maa bobiri, a wa ti paa ba Dan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

eng: During the night, he and his servants divided themselves up against them, attacked, and chased them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
local: Nisaaniŋi n na, o barigi niriba maa gbulagbula, yiiŋu, ba ŋme ba dindima maa niŋi, a nyaŋŋi ba. O kari ba lala haali, a wa ti paa Hooba, Damasika kiŋkagili sagbinni.

eng: He brought back all of the looted property, together with his relative Lot and Lots property, wives, and people.
local: ba va binseesi zaa ba n naa maa. Ka mari Loti ni o bunso zaa, a ni pogiba ni nivuseesi zaa ba n ma n gaari la pori n ta.

eng: After Abram returned from his attack on Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom came out to the Shaveh Valley to meet him.
local: Jemanso Abram n daa n zabi n nyaŋŋi naaba Kedolaama ni o nataaba la, ka pori n ta Sodom naaba tuusi u falimini so ba n boona Sava la niŋi, ka ba boona u naaba falimini.

eng: Now Melchizedek the king of Salem and the priest of El Elyon had brought bread and wine.
local: Ka Mekizade, so n niŋi Salem naaba, ka na kpari n niŋi Duuma Naawunni nakarigu la sandaantumina, o mari paanusi ni daam ti ti Abram.

eng: and he blessed him, Bless Abram by El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth;.
local: a nafa u yeli, Naawunni nakarigu la, so n maali saazu ni tiŋŋa la, o nafa Abram.

eng: bless El Elyon, who gave you the victory over your enemies. Abram gave Melchizedek one-tenth of everything.
local: Kpeesigu niŋini Naawunni nakarigu, so n ti e nyaŋŋi e dindima la toori. Abram nyisi binseesi zaa o ma n yi toowa puu ta la bu piipii zu ti Mekizade.

eng: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, Give me the people and take the property for yourself.
local: Sodom naaba yeli Abram, Mari binseesi zaa e n ma n ta la, ka naa niriba la wune ti ma.

eng: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, I promised the LORD , El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth.
local: Abram yeli u, M peya, nimbirimootiri, a yi Duuma nufu niŋi, so n niŋi Naawunni nakarigu saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa maana.

eng: that I wouldnt take even a thread or a sandal strap from anything that was yours so that you couldnt say, Im the one who made Abram rich.
local: a maa m ki naa bunso zaa n niŋi e toori, haali borabi koo sabitaa mii gba. E da ti yeli dindaso maa, mani n naa Abram loobi bindaana.

eng: The only exception is that the young men may keep whatever they have taken to eat, and the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre—may keep their share.
local: M ki naa sen zaa, ama binseesi m toozabiriba n di la ko. Ama, tori n ti seesi n paasi n niŋi la Aneri, Esikol, a ni Mamiri.

eng: When God began to create the heavens and the earth—.
local: Bunso zaa piiligu, Naawunni n daa n maalini saazu ni tiŋŋa.

eng: the earth was without shape or form, it was dark over the deep sea, and Gods wind swept over the waters—.
local: Tiŋŋa maa daa n niŋini binyoo, ka ligu ni koom paali u, Naawunni sii poli n jinna koom maa.

eng: God said, Let there be light. And so light appeared.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawun yeli, Cheli paaligu ta. Lala mi niŋi.

eng: God saw how good the light was. God separated the light from the darkness.
local: O nye paaligu maa, ka o veela. O barigi paaligu maa yi ligu la niŋi.

eng: God named the light Day and the darkness Night. There was evening and there was morning: the first day.
local: O booli paaligu maa bee, ka booli ligu la mi yiiŋu. Zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi o dapiiligu.

eng: God said, Let there be a dome in the middle of the waters to separate the waters from each other.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli bobiri barigi koom maa buyi.

eng: God made the dome and separated the waters under the dome from the waters above the dome. And it happened in that way.
local: Bobiri maa ti seesi du saa, ka seesi ba tiŋŋa.

eng: God named the dome Sky. There was evening and there was morning: the second day.
local: Ka Naawunni booli bobiri maa sagbaŋŋu. O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ayi daari.

eng: God said, Let the waters under the sky come together into one place so that the dry land can appear. And thats what happened.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli koseesi n ba tiŋŋa laŋsi ziluŋko, ka tiŋgbaŋŋu kuuŋu o ta. Lala mi niŋi.

eng: God named the dry land Earth, and he named the gathered waters Seas. God saw how good it was.
local: O booli tiŋgbaŋŋu kuuŋu la tiŋŋa, ka kolaŋsira la mini teeku. O nyaasi a, ka a veela.

eng: God said, Let the earth grow plant life: plants yielding seeds and fruit trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind throughout the earth. And thats what happened.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli tiŋgbaŋŋu buli bumbula, seesi a niŋi chi baligu, ka seesi a niŋi tiisi baligu, ka a wanna. A mi niŋi.

eng: The earth produced plant life: plants yielding seeds, each according to its kind, and trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Lala n na tiŋgbaŋŋu maa buli bumbula balisi. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the third day.
local: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ata daari.

eng: God said, Let there be lights in the dome of the sky to separate the day from the night. They will mark events, sacred seasons, days, and years.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli paaligusi ta sagbana niŋi, a barigi bee ni yiiŋu, jemani, daasi, a ni yuma.

eng: They will be lights in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth. And thats what happened.
local: A zaa ni ba sagbana niŋi, a tira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa paaligu. A mi niŋi.

eng: God made the stars and two great lights: the larger light to rule over the day and the smaller light to rule over the night.
local: Lala n na, Naawunni maali paaligu karigu ni paaligu bila, ŋa la o tira bee paaligu, ŋa la o tira yiiŋu paaligu. Ka o maali ŋmeribiisi gba.

eng: God put them in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth.
local: A zaa ba sagbana niŋi tira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa paaligu.

eng: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw how good it was.
local: a nyaasira bee ni yiiŋu, ka barigi paaligu yi ligu niŋi. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fourth day.
local: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba anaasi daari.

eng: God said, Let the waters swarm with living things, and let birds fly above the earth up in the dome of the sky.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli koom puu bunvuusira baligu zaa, a zoo n ba niŋi chaŋina. Ka cheli binyigira zaa, a ba niŋi yigira sagbana zu.

eng: God created the great sea animals and all the tiny living things that swarm in the waters, each according to its kind, and all the winged birds, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Naawunni daa n maali binnyaara kara niŋi koom puwa, a ni koom puu binnyaara zaa, a zoo n chaŋina, a ni niinsi balisi zaa. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

eng: Then God blessed them: Be fertile and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.
local: Naawunni nafa a, ka yeli, Ya wasi n paali koom maa niŋi. Ka niinsi gba, a wasi.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fifth day.
local: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba anu daari.

eng: God said, Let the earth produce every kind of living thing: livestock, crawling things, and wildlife. And thats what happened.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli tiŋgbaŋŋu binnyaara ni a balisi zaa ba niŋi, yiri buŋkobiri, moo puwa buŋkobiri, a ni binnyaara zaa. A mi niŋi.

eng: God made every kind of wildlife, every kind of livestock, and every kind of creature that crawls on the ground. God saw how good it was.
local: Naawunni maali a zaa, ka nyaasi a, ka a veela.

eng: Then God said, Let us make humanity in our image to resemble us so that they may take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, the livestock, all the earth, and all the crawling things on earth.
local: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.

eng: God created humanity in Gods own image, in the divine image God created them, male and female God created them.
local: Naawunni maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani u. O maali ba daba ni poga.

eng: God blessed them and said to them, Be fertile and multiply; fill the earth and master it. Take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Naawunni nafa ba, ka yeli, Ya dogira biisi, a paali tiŋgbaŋŋu maa, ka nyaasira u, a mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, saazu niinsi, a ni bini zaa n chaŋina tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: Then God said, I now give to you all the plants on the earth that yield seeds and all the trees whose fruit produces its seeds within it. These will be your food.
local: Ya nyaasi, m ti ya ni chi baligu, a ni tiisi wala, a niŋi ya bindira.

eng: To all wildlife, to all the birds in the sky, and to everything crawling on the ground—to everything that breathes—I give all the green grasses for food. And thats what happened.
local: M naani moori ni vaari ti buŋkobiri, niinsi a ni tiŋvuurigusi, a niŋi a bindira. Lala mi niŋi.

eng: God saw everything he had made: it was supremely good. There was evening and there was morning: the sixth day.
local: Ka Naawunni nyaasi bunso zaa o n maali maa, ka a veela ga. O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ayoobu daari.

eng: This is the record of Adams descendants. On the day God created humanity, he made them to resemble God.
local: Gbaŋŋu a yetirini Adam baligu yela, Naawunni daa maali ni nisaala, o ŋmaani u.

eng: God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, Be fertile, multiply, and fill the earth.
local: Naawunni nafa Nuhu ni o biisi, ka yeli ba, Ya dogi biisi, ba wasi n paali tiŋgbaŋaa zaa.

eng: and created them male and female. He blessed them and called them humanity on the day they were created.
local: O maali bani daba ni poga, ka nafa ba, a booli ba nisaaladima.

eng: All of the animals on the earth will fear you and dread you—all the birds in the skies, everything crawling on the ground, and all of the seas fish. They are in your power.
local: Buŋkobiri, niinsi a ni korotosi zaa ni zora ya, ka ba ya nuusi niŋi.

eng: The man Adam knew his wife Eve intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Cain, and said, I have given life to a man with the LORD s help.
local: Adam ni o poga lagimi, o mari puwa, a dogi daba. Niŋi n na Hawa yeli, Naawunni n suŋŋi ma, ma dogi daba, ka digili o yuuri Keen.

eng: When Adam was years old, he became the father of a son in his image, resembling him, and named him Seth.
local: Adam di yuma koowa ni pisita, ka dogi biya, o ŋmaani u, o digili o yuuri Sefi.

eng: Abram went up from Egypt toward the arid southern plain with his wife, with everything he had, and with Lot.
local: A zu, Abram ni o poga nyisigi Yijipiti paaligu kiŋkagili sagbinni, a pori n kpe Keenan paaligu kiŋkagili sazu, a ni o bindaalim bini zaa, ka Loti gba paag u gaari.

eng: Everything that lives and moves will be your food. Just as I gave you the green grasses, I now give you everything.
local: Pimparaŋa, ya ni dira a zaa, a ni bumbutira, m naani a zaa ti ya, a niŋi ya bindira.

eng: She gave birth a second time to Cains brother Abel. Abel cared for the flocks, and Cain farmed the fertile land.
local: A nyaaŋa, Hawa lee n dogi daba, Ebel. Ebel daa niŋini buŋkobguuna, ka Keen mi niŋi kokpaara.

eng: After Seths birth, Adam lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Sefi dogim nyaaŋa, o di yuma kobsinii, ka lee n dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: Abram was very wealthy in livestock, silver, and gold.
local: Abram daa niŋini bindaana, a mari peeri, buusi, naagi, gbitisi a ni sugasi.

eng: However, you must not eat meat with its life, its blood, in it.
local: Bunluŋkoso n bi daga ya n di n di niŋi, valiso zeem n bi yi o niŋi, a kpali bo, zeem maa niŋi n na nyevuri be.

eng: Some time later, Cain presented an offering to the LORD from the lands crops.
local: A n daa n ti paa jemani yinni, Keen mari o pu bindira, a ti puusi Naawunni.

eng: In all, Adam lived years, and he died.
local: Adam yuma kobsiwai ni pisita nyaaŋa n na o kpi.

eng: Abram traveled, making and breaking camp, from the arid southern plain to Bethel and to the sacred place there, where he had first pitched his tent between Bethel and Ai.
local: Niŋi n na, o nyisigi seela o n zi maa, a poli n jili n ti paa Betel ni Aii sunsuuniŋi, seela o n daa daŋŋi n zi la, ka zi niŋi.

eng: I will surely demand your blood for a human life, from every living thing I will demand it. From humans, from a man for his brother, I will demand something for a human life.
local: Ka nira nyisi o todaana nyevuri, o ni nye tubidarigirisim, ka ba ku buŋkoowa zaa ni nyisi nisaala nyevuri.

eng: while Abel presented his flocks oldest offspring with their fat. The LORD looked favorably on Abel and his sacrifice.
local: Ka Ebel mi ku o pee bipiiligu, ka naa o zimaŋa ziya puusi Naawunni, a peri Naawunni ga, o deeg o puusigu.

eng: When Seth was years old, he became the father of Enosh.
local: Sefi yuma koowa ni anu poin ka dogi Inosi.

eng: that is, to the place at which he had earlier built the altar. There he worshipped in the LORD s name.
local: Seela o n daa n me sariga daaŋu ti Duuma la, ka nyisi sariga nisaaniŋi.

eng: Whoever sheds human blood, by a human his blood will be shed; for in the divine image God made human beings.
local: Niso zaa ni ku nisaala, niso gba ni ku u, a kpali Naawunni meni nisaala maa o ŋmaani u.

eng: but didnt look favorably on Cain and his sacrifice. Cain became very angry and looked resentful.
local: Ama, Keen ni o puusigu daa n bi peri Naawunni, suuri gbaa Keen.

eng: After the birth of Enosh, Seth lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Inosi nyaaŋa, O yuma kobsinii ni ayapoin, ka o n lee n dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: Now Lot, who traveled with Abram, also had flocks, cattle, and tents.
local: Loti, so ninu n daa n chaŋina maa gba, daa marini peeri, buusi, naagi ni o laanta dima.

eng: As for you, be fertile and multiply. Populate the earth and multiply in it.
local: Ama nyiwune, dogi bidamta, lala jemani ya yaŋsi ni paali tiŋgbaŋaa zaa niŋi.

eng: The LORD said to Noah, Go into the ark with your whole household, because among this generation Ive seen that you are a moral man.
local: Lala jemani n ti paa, Duuma yeli Nuhu, E ni e baligu ya kpe ŋarintintaa la puwa, a kpali bo, m nyeya maa e ko n niŋi nivusuŋŋu a yi durinye ŋa niŋi.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Why are you angry, and why do you look so resentful?.
local: Duuma boosi Keen, Bo zu n na e je suuri, a dugu nufu?.

eng: In all, Seth lived years, and he died.
local: Sefi yuma kobsiwai ni pii ni ayi, ka o kpi.

eng: They had so many possessions between them that the land couldnt support both of them. They could no longer live together.
local: Ama, buŋkobiri moŋobira zii daa bi zoo, ba niriba bayi maa zaa di n lagimi n zi, a kpali bo, buŋkobiri maa daa n niŋi aligaaya.

eng: God said to Noah and to his sons with him.
local: Naawunni yeli Nuhu ni o biisi.

eng: From every clean animal, take seven pairs, a male and his mate; and from every unclean animal, take one pair, a male and his mate;.
local: Mari buŋkobseesi n daga sariga nyisigu tulum buyapoinyapoin daba ni poga, a ni buŋkobseesi n bi daga sariga nyisigu daba ni poga ayaayi ko.

eng: If you do the right thing, wont you be accepted? But if you dont do the right thing, sin will be waiting at the door ready to strike! It will entice you, but you must rule over it.
local: Ka e di niŋi bunso n daga, m di ki deeg e puusigu? Ama, e n bi niŋi nene la zu, yabeeri kpabirini o nyaŋŋi i, ama, a daga e nyaŋŋi u.

eng: When Enosh was years old, he became the father of Kenan.
local: Inosi yuma kobsiwai, ka o dogi Keenan.

eng: After these events, the LORD s word came to Abram in a vision, Dont be afraid, Abram. I am your protector. Your reward will be very great.
local: N ŋama nyaaŋa, Naawunni nyisi n wusi Abram, ka yeli u, Da zora dabeem. M ni kogi i yi nimbirimoo zaa niŋi, ka yo i samkarigu.

eng: Conflicts broke out between those herding Abrams livestock and those herding Lots livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites lived in the land.
local: Lala zu, zabiri daa li Abram buŋkokpagiriba ni Loti buŋkokpagiriba niŋi. Lala jemani maa ka Keenan nivusi ni niseesi ba n boona Periziti dima la n daa n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: I am now setting up my covenant with you, with your descendants.
local: M digilini naŋgbanni tira ya ni e yaŋsi.

eng: and from the birds in the sky as well, take seven pairs, male and female, so that their offspring will survive throughout the earth.
local: a ni niinsi balisi tulum buyapoinyapoin daba ni poga, ka nyaasira a, a wasira durinye ŋa puwa.

eng: Cain said to his brother Abel, Lets go out to the field. When they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
local: Dindaso, Keen yeli o sunzo Ebel, Cheli ti gaa laŋgaraa. Ba n we nisaaniŋi, Keen ŋme u ku.

eng: The heavens and the earth and all who live in them were completed.
local: N ŋa n na saazu ni tiŋŋa ni bunso zaa n ba a niŋi daa n maali.

eng: After Kenans birth, Enosh lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Keenan dogirisim nyaaŋa, Inosi yuma kobsinii ni pii ni anu, a nyaaŋa n na o lee n dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: But Abram said, LORD God, what can you possibly give me, since I still have no children? The head of my household is Eliezer, a man from Damascus.
local: Abram yeli, Duuma Nakarigu, bo n na e sanyoobu maa ya maali ma, jemanso ni ka biisi ŋa? Eliyeza n niŋi Damasika nira la n si n niŋi m kapete dira.

eng: Abram said to Lot, Lets not have disputes between me and you and between our herders since we are relatives.
local: Niŋi n na, Abram yeli Loti, Ti niŋini dogim luŋko, a bi daga e buŋkokpagiriba ni m buŋkokpagiriba ba zabira.

eng: and with every living being with you—with the birds, with the large animals, and with all the animals of the earth, leaving the ark with you.
local: a ni binvuusira zaa, niinsi, buŋkobiri a ni bunso zaa n paasi ya niŋi yi ŋariŋu la puwa.

eng: In seven days from now I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights. I will wipe off from the fertile land every living thing that I have made.
local: Daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, m ni ti saa ta dapisinaasi, wuntoona ni yiina, a muri bunvuusigu zaa ni maali durinye ŋa puwa.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Where is your brother Abel? Cain said, I dont know. Am I my brothers guardian.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma boosi Keen, E sunzo ba ya? Keen yeli, M bi baŋŋi. Mani n nyaasira u.

eng: On the sixth day God completed all the work that he had done, and on the seventh day God rested from all the work that he had done.
local: Daba ayapoin daari Naawunni tuma maa baasi, ka o naa dindaari maa vuusi.

eng: In all, Enosh lived years, and he died.
local: Inosi dini yuma kobsiwai ni anu ka kpi.

eng: He continued, Since you havent given me any children, the head of my household will be my heir.
local: E bi ti ma biisi, m gurunsi ŋa yindaana n ni di m kapete.

eng: Isnt the whole land in front of you? Lets separate. If you go north, I will go south; and if you go south, I will go north.
local: Ka tiŋgbaŋŋu maa n do ŋa? Ti barigi taaba. Ka e gaa nuzaa, m ni gaa nudiya, ka e mi gaa nudiya, m ni gaa nuzaa.

eng: I will set up my covenant with you so that never again will all life be cut off by floodwaters. There will never again be a flood to destroy the earth.
local: Naŋgbandigiligu maa n ŋa, m digilini noori maa m ki lee n naa koom muri bunvuusira zaa, koom bi lee n chena o ti muri tiŋgbaŋaa zaa.

eng: Noah did everything the LORD commanded him.
local: Nuhu niŋi bunso zaa Naawunni n yeli o niŋi la.

eng: The LORD said, What did you do? The voice of your brothers blood is crying to me from the ground.
local: Duuma boosi u, Bo tumtumbee n na e tumi a? E sunzo zeem do tiŋŋa chaari n tira ma, a maa, m darigi e tubiri.

eng: God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it God rested from all the work of creation.
local: Naawunni nafa daba ayapoin daari maa, ka loobi u vuusim daa, a kpali bo, o tuma maa zaa baasiya.

eng: When Kenan was years old, he became the father of Mahalalel.
local: Keenan yuma pisiyapoin ka dogi Mahalali.

eng: The LORD s word came immediately to him, This man will not be your heir. Your heir will definitely be your very own biological child.
local: Niŋi n na, o lee n wumi Duuma ni togsira o saani. E guruŋu maa ki di e kapete, e gbagba biduwa n ni niŋi e kapete dira.

eng: Lot looked up and saw the entire Jordan Valley. All of it was well irrigated, like the garden of the LORD , like the land of Egypt, as far as Zoar .
local: Loti nyaasi n poli n jili, ka nye maa Joodan falimini, a wa ti paa Zowa, ka niŋi n mari koom nene, a maa Duuma pu la, koo Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu. Ŋama zaa nyaaŋa poin, ka Naawunni daa muri Sodom ni Gomora.

eng: God said, This is the symbol of the covenant that I am drawing up between me and you and every living thing with you, on behalf of every future generation.
local: Naawunni yeli, Buŋaa n ya n niŋi naŋgbandigiligu maa teesa, a ti e ni bunvuusira, a wa ti paa e yaŋsi zaa.

eng: Noah was years old when the floodwaters arrived on earth.
local: Jemanso koom maa n daa n paali maa, Nuhu daa n niŋini yuma kobsiyoobu.

eng: You are now cursed from the ground that opened its mouth to take your brothers blood from your hand.
local: A zu, naŋgbansiibu niŋini e toori, e ki lee n tee n ko o niŋi, e sunzo zeem la zu.

eng: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created. On the day the LORD God made earth and sky—.
local: Seema Naawunni n daa n maali saazu ni tiŋŋa n na la. Duuma Naawunni n daa maali saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa la.

eng: After the birth of Mahalalel, Kenan lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Mahalali nyaaŋa, o di yuma kobsinii, a ni pisinaasi, ka lee n dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: Then he brought Abram outside and said, Look up at the sky and count the stars if you think you can count them. He continued, This is how many children you will have.
local: Duuma mari u yi yaŋŋa, ka yeli u, Nyaasi sagbana zu, ka niŋi aniya kaali ŋmeribiisi a, lala n na e yaŋsi ya n sagi.

eng: So Lot chose for himself the entire Jordan Valley. Lot set out toward the east, and they separated from each other.
local: A zu, Loti nyisi Joodan falimini maa, a kaasi n naag o zu to wuluŋpusigu. Seema a n di niriba bayi maa barigi taaba niŋi n na la.

eng: I have placed my bow in the clouds; it will be the symbol of the covenant between me and the earth.
local: M naani m sasuwa niŋi sagbana niŋi, wune n ya n niŋi m naŋgbandigiligu maa teesa ti durinye.

eng: Noah, his sons, his wife, and his sons wives with him entered the ark to escape the floodwaters.
local: O ni o poga, a ni ba biisi, ni ba deempogiba kpe ŋarintintaa maa puwa, kopaali maa da muri ba.

eng: When you farm the fertile land, it will no longer grow anything for you, and you will become a roving nomad on the earth.
local: Ka e buri bunso zaa, a ki niŋi. E ki mari yiri, ka gaa n chaŋina.

eng: before any wild plants appeared on the earth, and before any field crops grew, because the LORD God hadnt yet sent rain on the earth and there was still no human being to farm the fertile land.
local: vatuya ni bumbula daa na ka niŋi, a kpali Duuma Naawunni na bi ti saa ta, ka nira zaa daa n na ka, o ko tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: In all, Kenan lived years, and he died.
local: Keenan daa n dini yuma kobsiwai ni piiya, ka kpi.

eng: Abram trusted the LORD , and the LORD recognized Abrams high moral character.
local: Abram yeride seema Duuma ni yeli u maa, Duuma deeg u, ka yeli, ni o niŋini yamonsi daana.

eng: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, and Lot settled near the cities of the valley and pitched his tent close to Sodom.
local: Abram zi Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ka Loti mi zi tiŋsi sunsuuniŋi, a yi falimini maa niŋi, a leeli Sodom.

eng: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow appears in the clouds.
local: Jemanso zaa ka sagbana ti pili, sasuwa maa ni yi.

eng: From the clean and unclean animals, from the birds and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Niŋi n na, Nuhu mari buŋkobseesi n daga sariga nyisigu, a ni seesi n bi daga, niinsi a ni bunvuurigusi zaa kpe ŋarintintaa maa puwa, daba ni poga, a maa seema Naawunni n yeli u la.

eng: Cain said to the LORD , My punishment is more than I can bear.
local: Keen yeli Duuma, M tubidarigirisimaa ba toom ga ti ma.

eng: though a stream rose from the earth and watered all of the fertile land—.
local: Ama koom daa n bulisini yi tiŋŋa, a du n maagira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa.

eng: When Mahalalel was years old, he became the father of Jared.
local: Mahalali yuma pisiyoobu ni anu, ka dogi Jaadi.

eng: He said to Abram, I am the LORD , who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land as your possession.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli u, Mani n niŋi Duuma so n pa i yiya, a mari i yi Waa n ba Babilon paaligu, a ti naa tiŋgbaŋaa ti i, o niŋi e buŋ.

eng: The citizens of Sodom were very evil and sinful against the LORD .
local: Nisaaniŋi niriba maa daa niŋini ferebeeri dima, ka niŋi yabeeri ga kpe Duuma niŋi.

eng: I will remember the covenant between me and you and every living being among all the creatures. Floodwaters will never again destroy all creatures.
local: m ni teesi m naŋgbandigiligu yi ya ni buŋkobiri zaa maa, koom ki lee n di bunvuusira zaa.

eng: After seven days, the floodwaters arrived on the earth.
local: Daba ayapoin maa nyaaŋa, komutugu maa piili.

eng: Now that youve driven me away from the fertile land and I am hidden from your presence, Im about to become a roving nomad on the earth, and anyone who finds me will kill me.
local: Zinni, e kari ma ni yi e saani, a ni tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi. M ki mari yiri, ka gaa n chaŋina. Ka nivu zaa paa ma, o n ku ma.

eng: the LORD God formed the human from the topsoil of the fertile land and blew lifes breath into his nostrils. The human came to life.
local: A jemani maa Duuma Naawunni naa tanni me daba, ka vuusi nyevuri vuusim niŋi o ŋoya niŋi, o loobi nyevuri daana.

eng: After Jareds birth, Mahalalel lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Jaadi nyaaŋa, o dini yuma kobsinii a ni pisita, ka dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: But Abram said, LORD God, how do I know that I will actually possess it.
local: Abram boosi u, Duuma Nakarigu, wula ma ya n niŋi baŋŋi maa o niŋini m buŋ.

eng: After Lot separated from him, the LORD said to Abram, From the place where you are standing, look up and gaze to the north, south, east, and west.
local: Loti ni gaa maa nyaaŋa, Duuma yeli Abram, Za seela e n be ŋa, ka lalli e nufu nyaasi kiŋkagili sagbinni, sazu, a ni wuluŋpusigu ni wulunliya.

eng: The bow will be in the clouds, and upon seeing it I will remember the enduring covenant between God and every living being of all the earths creatures.
local: Ka sasuwa maa si n yi sagbana maa niŋi, m ni nye u, ka teesi m naŋgbandigiliguso n ka nyisigirisim koo baasigu, a yi m ni ya ni bunvuusira zaa sunsuuniŋi tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi.

eng: In the six hundredth year of Noahs life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day—on that day all the springs of the deep sea erupted, and the windows in the skies opened.
local: Nuhu yuma kobsiyoobu, o dapii ni ayapoin, ŋmerisi ayi yumini maa niŋi, tiŋkoom ni kosoosi zaa chibi, sakoom kpaa n yi sagbana zu.

eng: The LORD said to him, It wont happen; anyone who kills Cain will be paid back seven times. The LORD put a sign on Cain so that no one who found him would assault him.
local: Duuma yeli u, Akai, ka nira zaa ku i, ni ku o niriba bayapoin yo samini. Lala zu, Naawunni duligi Keen, a kpaasi niriba tuba, a maa ba da ku u.

eng: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden in the east and put there the human he had formed.
local: Ka ti Duuma Naawunni digili pu, a yi Idin paaligu n ba wuluŋu pusigu, ka naa daba maa niŋi nisaaniŋi.

eng: In all, Mahalalel lived years, and he died.
local: Mahalali yuma kobsinii a ni pisiwai ni anu, ka o kpi.

eng: He said, Bring me a three-year-old female calf, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.
local: Duuma yeli u, Mari nagisaa, busaa, pedaa, a zaa a niŋi yuma ataata, a ni kili ni ŋmenni ti ti ma.

eng: because all the land that you see I give you and your descendants forever.
local: M ya n naani tiŋgbaŋaa, e n nyaasi a ti e ni e yaŋsi, o niŋi ya toori, a ma n wara.

eng: God said to Noah, This is the symbol of the covenant that I have set up between me and all creatures on earth.
local: N ŋa naŋgbandigiligu teesa ŋa n na ma naa n tira bunvuusira zaa.

eng: It rained on the earth forty days and forty nights.
local: Saa maa ta dapisinaasi wuntoona ni yiina.

eng: Cain left the LORD s presence, and he settled down in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
local: A zu, Keen yi Naawunni saani, a we paaliguso ba n boona Nodi, o gbinni n niŋi, gaa n chaŋina, a yi Idin wuluŋpusigu.

eng: In the fertile land, the LORD God grew every beautiful tree with edible fruit, and also he grew the tree of life in the middle of the garden and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni ti tisuma zaa baligu buli, seesi wala n veela buli. Tiisi ayi za pu maa sunsuuniŋi, ŋala tira nyevuri, ka ŋala mi tira yasuma ni yabeeri baŋinisim.

eng: When Jared was years old, he became the father of Enoch.
local: Jaadi yuma koowa ni pisiyoobu, ni ayi, ka dogi Inoki.

eng: He took all of these animals, split them in half, and laid the halves facing each other, but he didnt split the birds.
local: Abram mari lala buŋkobiri maa ti ti Naawunni, ka kasigi a, ayaayi, a digili a bobaayi a tooli taaba, ama niinsi la wune, o bi kasigi a.

eng: I will make your descendants like the dust of the earth. If someone could count the bits of dust on the earth, then they could also count your descendants.
local: M ya n ti i ni yaŋdamta, seesi nisaala zaa di n ki tee n ba kaali, ka nira yi tee n kaali tiŋgbaŋŋu a niŋi tanni, lala wune, ba ni tee kaali e yaŋsi gba.

eng: Noahs sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth came out of the ark. Now Ham was Canaans father.
local: Nuhu biisi maa n daa n paasi o niŋi yi ŋariŋu maa pu la n ŋa, Sem, Ham ni Jafeti. Ham bii n daa n niŋi Keenan.

eng: That same day Noah, with his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, Noahs wife, and his sons three wives, went into the ark.
local: Dindaso saa maa n piili n chena n na, Nuhu ni o poga, a ni o biduusi ba boona Sem, Ham ni Jafeti ni ba pogiba kpe ŋariŋu maa.

eng: Cain knew his wife intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Enoch. Cain built a city and named the city after his son Enoch.
local: Niŋi n na, Keen naa poga, a dogi biya, ka digili o yuuri Inoki. Ka me tiŋtintaa, ka naa bii maa yuuri digili tiŋŋa maa.

eng: A river flows from Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides into four headwaters.
local: Mogiri mi daa n bani Idin paaligu, a zo n ti maagira pu maa, ka barigi bunaasi.

eng: After Enochs birth, Jared lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Inoki nyaaŋa, o yuma kobsinii, ka o lee n dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: When vultures swooped down on the carcasses, Abram waved them off.
local: Zugusi ta a diibu, Abram kari a basi.

eng: Stand up and walk around through the length and breadth of the land because I am giving it to you.
local: Pimparaŋa, nyisigi n poli n jili tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa, a nyaasi u, a kpali bo, m ya naagi u ni ti i.

eng: These were Noahs three sons, and from them the whole earth was populated.
local: Nuhu biisi bata maa yaŋsi n niŋi nisaaladima zaa n ba tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi la.

eng: They and every kind of animal—every kind of livestock, every kind that crawls on the ground, every kind of bird —.
local: Ka ba mari buŋkobiri balisi zaa gba paasi n kpe ŋariŋu maa.

eng: Adam knew his wife intimately again, and she gave birth to a son. She named him Seth because God has given me another child in place of Abel, whom Cain killed.
local: A jemani maa, Adam ni o poga lee n dogi daba. Poga maa yeli, Naawunni lee n ti ma ni biduwa, o za Ebel noba niŋi, so Keen n daa n ku la. O digili o yuuri Sefi.

eng: The name of the first river is the Pishon. It flows around the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
local: Piiligu mogiri maa ba boona Pison, o zo n wara Havila paaligu zaa.

eng: In all, Jared lived years, and he died.
local: Jaadi yuma kobsiwai ni pisiyoobu ni ayi, ka o kpi.

eng: After the sun set, Abram slept deeply. A terrifying and deep darkness settled over him.
local: Wuluŋu n ti liira, gbeem gbaa Abram ga, ligu ti pili u birib, dabeem gbaa u.

eng: So Abram packed his tent and went and settled by the oaks of Mamre in Hebron. There he built an altar to the LORD .
local: Niŋi n na, Abram nyisigi seela o n daa zi la, a wa ti zi n begi Mamiri titintaasi, a yi Hebiron, nisaaniŋi n na o me sariga daaŋu ti Duuma.

eng: Noah, a farmer, made a new start and planted a vineyard.
local: Nuhu daa n niŋini kokpaara, wune n daa n piili n buri binseesi wala ba naa n maana daam.

eng: they came to Noah and entered the ark, two of every creature that breathes.
local: Binnyaara zaa baligu ayaayi gba paasi Nuhu niŋi kpe ŋariŋu maa.

eng: Seth also fathered a son and named him Enosh. At that time, people began to worship in the LORD s name.
local: Niŋi n na, Sefi gba naa poga, a dogi daba, ka digili o yuuri Inosi. A jemani maa n na niriba piili n naa Duuma yuuri kpeesira u.

eng: That lands gold is pure, and the land also has sweet-smelling resins and gemstones.
local: Sugasuma mi ba niŋi, a ni tulalesuma, a ni kuguso n mari tono.

eng: When Enoch was years old, he became the father of Methuselah.
local: Inoki yuma pisiyoobu ni anu poin ka o dogi Matuusala.

eng: Then the LORD said to Abram, Have no doubt that your descendants will live as immigrants in a land that isnt their own, where they will be oppressed slaves for four hundred years.
local: Duuma yeli, E yaŋsi ni niŋi saamba yi paaligu yindaana niŋi, ba ni niŋi gurunsi, ka ba namsi ba ga yuma kobsinaasi.

eng: He drank some of the wine, became drunk, and took off his clothes in his tent.
local: O n daa n ŋu daam maa seesi, a kuuli, a pirigi alibana do o gbana sugiri puwa.

eng: Male and female of every creature went in, just as God had commanded him. Then the LORD closed the door behind them.
local: Bunvuusira maa daa niŋini daba ni poga, a maa seema Duuma Naawunni n daa yeli la. Nuhu ni bini maa zaa n kpe n baasi, Duuma gbagba yo ŋarintintaa maa noori.

eng: The name of the second river is the Gihon. It flows around the entire land of Cush.
local: Moga ayi daana maa n niŋi Gihon, o mi zo n wara Kusi paaligu zaa.

eng: Enoch walked with God. After Methuselahs birth, Enoch lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Matuusala nyaaŋa, Inoki daa n niŋi petira Naawunni, yuma kobsita poin ka dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: But after I punish the nation they serve, they will leave it with great wealth.
local: Ama, m ni darigi niseesi n naa ba loobi gurunsi maa tuba, ka jemanso ba n yiira paaligu maa niŋi, ba ni naa ba bindaalim bini yi ba saani.

eng: Ham, Canaans father, saw his father naked and told his two brothers who were outside.
local: Ham, Keenan ba, n ti kpe, ka nye o ba n pirigi alibana do, o yi n wa ti yeli o mabiisi bayi la.

eng: The flood remained on the earth for forty days. The waters rose, lifted the ark, and it rode high above the earth.
local: Saa maa n ta dapisinaasi la, seela zaa paali koom, a mari ŋariŋu maa du n pa kozu.

eng: The name of the third river is the Tigris, flowing east of Assyria; and the name of the fourth river is the Euphrates.
local: Moga atadaana maa n niŋi Tigirisi, a zo n yiira Asiriya paaligu n ba wuluŋu pusigu. Moga anaasidaana maa n niŋi Yufireeti.

eng: As for you, you will join your ancestors in peace and be buried after a good long life.
local: Nyiwune, ni kurigi poin, ka kpi, ba pi i, a ni niŋgbirimaasim.

eng: In all, Enoch lived years.
local: Inoki yuma daa n niŋi kobsita ni pisiyoobu ni anu.

eng: Shem and Japheth took a robe, threw it over their shoulders, walked backward, and covered their naked father without looking at him because they turned away.
local: Ba bayi maa naa waje pili ba bogiri niŋi, a lugi gbinni kpe sugiri maa puwa, a naa waje la pili ba ba, ba da nye o alibana maa.

eng: The waters rose and spread out over the earth. The ark floated on the surface of the waters.
local: Koom maa paali ga, ŋariŋu maa laŋina.

eng: The LORD God took the human and settled him in the garden of Eden to farm it and to take care of it.
local: Duuma Naawunni n naa daba maa niŋi pu la n ba Idin paaligu la o doosira, ka nyaasira u.

eng: The fourth generation will return here since the Amorites wrongdoing wont have reached its peak until then.
local: E yaŋsi ni paa balisi tulumbunaasi poin, ka pa pori n ta kpe, a kpali Amori dima yabeeri na bi paa tubidarigirisim.

eng: Enoch walked with God and disappeared because God took him.
local: Inoki ni Naawunni n daa chaŋina, o naag u du saa, ka o bi kpi.

eng: When Noah woke up from his wine, he discovered what his youngest son had done to him.
local: Daam maa n ti yi Nuhu niŋi, o wumi seema o biisi maa bibila daana n niŋi u.

eng: The waters rose even higher over the earth; they covered all of the highest mountains under the sky.
local: Koom maa paali n muni zoori zaa.

eng: The LORD God commanded the human, Eat your fill from all of the gardens trees;.
local: Ka ti u zaaligu yeli, E ni tee n di pu maa tiisi zaa wala.

eng: After the sun had set and darkness had deepened, a smoking vessel with a fiery flame passed between the split-open animals.
local: Wuluŋu n ti li, zii gbaa, duguso wusi ni yiira o niŋi, a ni miinkpalli ni bugum yi n ti mari buŋkoŋmaasira la sunsuuniŋi gaari.

eng: When Methuselah was years old, he became the father of Lamech.
local: Matuusala yuma koowa ni pisinii ni ayapoin, ka o dogi Lameki.

eng: He said, Cursed be Canaan: the lowest servant he will be for his brothers.
local: ka yeli, Naŋgbansiibu niŋini Keenan toori, o ni niŋi guruŋu ti o beya la.

eng: The waters rose twenty-three feet high, covering the mountains.
local: A muni zoori maa noba limsim pisiyi.

eng: but dont eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, because on the day you eat from it, you will die.
local: ama, da pa ti di tiso n tira yasuma ni yabeeri baŋinisim la wala. A kpali bo, dindaso zaa e n di u, e ni kpi.

eng: That day the LORD cut a covenant with Abram: To your descendants I give this land, from Egypts river to the great Euphrates.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni ti Abram noori yeli, M ti i ni noori maa tiŋgbaŋaa, a za Yijipiti torigu, a wa ti paa mogiso ba n boona Yufireeti la zaa ni loobi e yaŋsi toori.

eng: After Lamechs birth, Methuselah lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Lameki nyaaŋa, o yuma kobsiyapoin ni pisinii ni ayi, ka dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: He also said, Bless the LORD , the God of Shem; Canaan will be his servant.
local: O lee n yeli, nafa o niŋi Sem Duuma Naawunni toori, Naawunni n ti Keenan niŋi Sem guruŋu.

eng: Every creature took its last breath: the things crawling on the ground, birds, livestock, wild animals, everything swarming on the ground, and every human being.
local: binnyaara zaa ni ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa muri, niinsi, buŋkobiri balisi ni nisaaladima zaa.

eng: Then the LORD God said, Its not good that the human is alone. I will make him a helper that is perfect for him.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli, O bi daga daba ko n ba niŋi, m ni maali suŋina, o niŋi o todaana.

eng: In all, Methuselah lived years, and he died.
local: Matuusala daa niŋini yuma kobsiwai ni pisiyoobu ni awai, ka kpi.

eng: May God give space to Japheth; he will live in Shems tents, and Canaan will be his servant.
local: Naawunni n ti Jafeti o wasi, ka ti o yaŋsi ni Sem ba ba niŋi, ama Keenan ni niŋi Jafeti guruŋu.

eng: So the LORD God formed from the fertile land all the wild animals and all the birds in the sky and brought them to the human to see what he would name them. The human gave each living being its name.
local: A jemani maa o naa tanni me buŋkobiri ni niinsi zaa, ka naag a ta daba maa saani, o digili a yuya.

eng: God wiped away every living thing that was on the fertile land—from human beings to livestock to crawling things to birds in the sky. They were wiped off the earth. Only Noah and those with him in the ark were left.
local: Seema Duuma daa n muri binnyaara zaa n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi n na la, nisaaladima, buŋkobiri a ni niinsi zaa. Ama Nuhu ni o niriba, a ni binseesi n daa ba ŋariŋu la puu ko daa n bi muri.

eng: When Lamech was years old, he became the father of a son.
local: Lameki dini yuma koowa ni pisinii ni ayi, ka dogi biduwa.

eng: After the flood, Noah lived years.
local: Kopaali maa nyaaŋa, Nuhu daa lee n dini yuma kobsita ni pisinu.

eng: The human named all the livestock, all the birds in the sky, and all the wild animals. But a helper perfect for him was nowhere to be found.
local: Daba maa n digili a yuya maa baasi, o bi nye o suŋina zaa yi a puwa.

eng: The waters rose over the earth for one hundred fifty days.
local: Koom maa n daa n paali n za, daasi koowa ni pisinu.

eng: and named him Noah, saying, This one will give us relief from our hard work, from the pain in our hands, because of the fertile land that the LORD cursed.
local: Ka digili o yuuri Nuhu, ka yeli, ŋala n ni maa ti sufusi, a yi ti tuma pelli, a kpali Naawunni n si tiŋgbaŋŋu a noori la zu.

eng: In all, Noah lived years; then he died.
local: O daa dini yuma kobsiwai ni pisinu poin ka kpi.

eng: So the LORD God put the human into a deep and heavy sleep, and took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh over it.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni ti daba maa gbisi ga. O n gbisira maa, Duuma Naawunni nyisi o nyekobiri, ka ti nisaaniŋi ti yo.

eng: After Noahs birth, Lamech lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: O n dogi Nuhu nyaaŋa, Lameki dini yuma kobsinu ni pisiwai ni anu, ka dogi biduusi ni bipunsi.

eng: With the rib taken from the human, the LORD God fashioned a woman and brought her to the human being.
local: O naa nyekobiri maa maali poga, a ti daba maa.

eng: In all, Lamech lived years, and he died.
local: Lameki daa n dini yuma kobsiyoobu ni pisiyapoin ni ayapoin, ka kpi.

eng: The human said, This one finally is bone from my bones and flesh from my flesh. She will be called a woman because from a man she was taken.
local: A nyaaŋa daba maa yeli, Pimparaŋa wune m pa nye m gbagba ŋmaani, m koba niŋi kobiri, m valiga niŋi valiga. O n yi daba niŋi zu, o yuuri n niŋi poga.

eng: When Noah was years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: Nuhu daa n dini yuma kobsinu, ka dogi Sem ni Ham a ni Jafeti.

eng: This is the reason that a man leaves his father and mother and embraces his wife, and they become one flesh.
local: Lala zu, daba yi cheli o ba ni o ma, ka naa poga, ba loobi luŋko.

eng: God remembered Noah, all those alive, and all the animals with him in the ark. God sent a wind over the earth so that the waters receded.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni daa n bi tami Nuhu ni buŋkobiri la zaa n daa n ba ŋariŋu maa puu la yela, o ti sauwa vaara, koom maa piili n sigira.

eng: The two of them were naked, the man and his wife, but they werent embarrassed.
local: Daba maa ni poga maa daa n marini alibana, ka vi bi mari ba.

eng: The springs of the deep sea and the skies closed up. The skies held back the rain.
local: O ti konimbiisi zaa n daa n ba tiŋŋa bulisira la zaa nyeri, ka yo saa dinaŋgbana, saa maa kasigi.

eng: The waters receded gradually from the earth. After one hundred fifty days, the waters decreased;.
local: Koom maa naa daasi koowa ni pisinu a sigi.

eng: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day, the ark came to rest on the Ararat mountains.
local: Dapii ni ayapoin daari, ŋmerisi ayapoin daana niŋi, ŋariŋu maa sigi n ti za zoso ba n boona Ararat la zukpaa niŋi.

eng: The waters decreased gradually until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month the mountain peaks appeared.
local: Koom maa sigira, a ti paa ŋmerisi pii daana dayinni, zoori yi n turi.

eng: After forty days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.
local: Dapisinaasi nyaaŋa, Nuhu yoo sugulugu.

eng: He sent out a raven, and it flew back and forth until the waters over the entire earth had dried up.
local: Ka tumi nyiŋgbuŋ, o bi lee n pori n ta, ka ba niŋi n yigi n poli n jinna, a ti paa koom maa n ŋu.

eng: Then he sent out a dove to see if the waters on all of the fertile land had subsided.
local: Niŋi n na, Nuhu lee n tumi ŋmenni ni o yi n nyaasi n nyee koom maa sigiya a.

eng: but the dove found no place to set its foot. It returned to him in the ark since waters still covered the entire earth. Noah stretched out his hand, took it, and brought it back into the ark.
local: Ŋmenni maa daa n bi nye ziya o dobi, a kpali koom maa daa n na n paali maa zu, o pori n ta ŋariŋu maa ziya, Nuhu teeg o nuu gbaag u niŋi.

eng: All people on the earth had one language and the same words.
local: Piiligu maa, durinye puu niriba zaa daa n togsirini yatogiluŋko.

eng: He waited seven more days and sent the dove out from the ark again.
local: O guri daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, o lee n tumi ŋmenni.

eng: When they traveled east, they found a valley in the land of Shinar and settled there.
local: Ba daa n chaŋŋi n kpabira ziya, ba mari wuluŋpusigu, a ti paa paaligu yinni ba boona Babilon paaligu, ka zi nisaaniŋi.

eng: The LORD said to Abram, Leave your land, your family, and your fathers household for the land that I will show you.
local: Dindaso, Duuma daa yeli Abram, Yi e paaligaa, e niriba puwa, a ni e ba laanta, a gaa tiŋgbanso ni ya n wusi i ŋa.

eng: The dove came back to him in the evening, grasping a torn olive leaf in its beak. Then Noah knew that the waters were subsiding from the earth.
local: Dindaari maa zaanoori, ŋmenni maa pori n ta ka tiso ba n boona olivi la vapaali kagini o noori. Niŋi n na, Nuhu baŋŋi maa koom maa sigira.

eng: Sarai, Abrams wife, had not been able to have children. Since she had an Egyptian servant named Hagar.
local: Abram poga Sarai daa n bi dogi bii zaa ti u, ama o mari Yijipiti guruŋpoga Heega.

eng: They said to each other, Come, lets make bricks and bake them hard. They used bricks for stones and asphalt for mortar.
local: Niŋi n na, ba to jina, Ya cheli ti ŋma birikisisi, ka ŋo a, a mari kpeeŋu. Lala ba niŋi birikisisi maa, ka naa kootaali me a.

eng: I will make of you a great nation and will bless you. I will make your name respected, and you will be a blessing.
local: Ni ti e yaŋsi zoo, ba ni niŋi baliso n mari zaaziya. M ni nafa i, ka ti e yuuri du, e ni niŋi nafa ti niriba.

eng: He waited seven more days and sent out the dove, but it didnt come back to him again.
local: O lee n guri daba ayapoin, ka lee tumi ŋmenni maa gbagba, ŋala wune ŋmenni maa bi lee n pori n ta.

eng: Sarai said to Abram, The LORD has kept me from giving birth, so go to my servant. Maybe she will provide me with children. Abram did just as Sarai said.
local: Lala zu, o yeli Abram, Duuma gbaa m dogim, bo n na e bi mari m guruŋaa doora? Saŋa, o ni dogi bii ti ma. Abram sagi n pa bunso Sarai n yeli maa.

eng: They said, Come, lets build for ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the sky, and lets make a name for ourselves so that we wont be dispersed over all the earth.
local: Ba lee n yeli, Ya cheli ti me tiŋkarigu, ka me kpaa o niŋi, o ba wogilim tisi sagbana, lala jemani o ni ti ti yuuri du, ka ti da pisimi n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: I will bless those who bless you, those who curse you I will curse; all the families of the earth will be blessed because of you.
local: M ni nafa niseesi ni nafara i, ama ni si niseesi ni siira i noori, e niŋi n na ma ni dolli n nafa balisi zaa.

eng: In Noahs six hundred first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters dried up from the earth. Noah removed the arks hatch and saw that the surface of the fertile land had dried up.
local: Nuhu yuma kobsiyoobu ni yinni, o ŋmeripiiligu dayinni n na koom maa sigi nyem. Niŋi n na, Nuhu yoo ŋariŋu maa dinoori, ka nye maa tiŋŋa pa kuugira.

eng: When the number of people started to increase throughout the fertile land, daughters were born to them.
local: Jemanso niriba n daa wasi n paali durinye ŋa, ba daa n dogini bipunsi.

eng: After Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Abrams wife Sarai took her Egyptian servant Hagar and gave her to her husband Abram as his wife.
local: A zu, Sarai naa Heega ti u, o loobi o poga. A jemani Abram n daa n wa ti zi Keenan, o yuma pii nyaaŋa n na lala daa n niŋi.

eng: Then the LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the humans built.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma sigi n ti nyaasi tiŋkarigu la, a ni kpaa maa ba n me la.

eng: The snake was the most intelligent of all the wild animals that the LORD God had made. He said to the woman, Did God really say that you shouldnt eat from any tree in the garden.
local: A jemani maa, waafu n daa n baŋŋi yomsigu gaari buŋkobiri zaa Naawunni n daa n maali la. Dindaso o daa n ta poga maa saani, a ti boosi u, Yamonsi n na Naawunni yeli ya da dira tii zaa wala pu ŋa niŋi.

eng: Abram left just as the LORD told him, and Lot went with him. Now Abram was years old when he left Haran.
local: Lala jemani, ka Abram daa niŋini yuma pisiyapoin ni anu, o nyisigi n yi Haran, seema Duuma n yeli u o niŋi la, ka Loti pa o zu gaari.

eng: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day, the earth was dry.
local: O ŋmerisi ayi daana dapisiyi ni ayapoin n na tiŋŋa daa kuu nyem.

eng: The divine beings saw how beautiful these human women were, so they married the ones they chose.
local: Naawunni biduusi nyaasi bipunsi maa veelim, ka naa seesi n peri ba.

eng: He slept with Hagar, and she became pregnant. But when she realized that she was pregnant, she no longer respected her mistress.
local: Abram ni Heega lagimi, o mari puwa. Jemanso o n ti baŋŋi maa o mari ni puwa, zunyaabu ta, o bi lee booli Sarai sen.

eng: And the LORD said, There is now one people and they all have one language. This is what they have begun to do, and now all that they plan to do will be possible for them.
local: Ka yeli, Pimparaŋa, niribaa niŋini baligu luŋko, a togsira yatogiluŋko, ka piili n niŋi bunso ba n niŋina ŋa. Bo n na ba ni kpabira ba niŋi, ka niŋi u je?.

eng: The woman said to the snake, We may eat the fruit of the gardens trees.
local: Poga maa yeli u, Ti n tee n di tii zaa wala n ba pu ŋa niŋi.

eng: Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, all of their possessions, and those who became members of their household in Haran; and they set out for the land of Canaan. When they arrived in Canaan.
local: Abram mari o poga Sarai ni o sunzo bii Loti ni ba bindaalim bini zaa, a ni ba gurunsi zaa ba n mari n yi Haran gbaa Keenan soori. Ba n wa ti paa Keenan paaligu maa.

eng: God spoke to Noah.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli Nuhu.

eng: The LORD said, My breath will not remain in humans forever, because they are flesh. They will live one hundred twenty years.
local: Duuma yeli, M sisuŋŋu ki ba nisaala niŋi, o ba niŋi wara. O n niŋi valiga la zu, o yuugirisim zaa o niŋi yuma koowa ni pisiyi ko.

eng: Sarai said to Abram, This harassment is your fault. I allowed you to embrace my servant, but when she realized she was pregnant, I lost her respect. Let the LORD decide who is right, you or me.
local: Niŋi n na, Sarai yeli Abram, Nyini n ti Heega nyaasira ma jaga. Mani n naagi u ti i, ama o n baŋŋi maa o n mari puu la, o pa nyaasira mani jaga. Naawunni ni di m nini zaa seriya, a baŋŋi so toori n ba niŋi.

eng: Come, lets go down and mix up their language there so they wont understand each others language.
local: Ya cheli ti sigi n wa ti jebi ba yatoga maa, lala jemani ba ki lee n wumini taaba yatoga.

eng: but not the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden. God said, Dont eat from it, and dont touch it, or you will die.
local: Ama tiso n za sunsuuniŋi la, Naawunni yeli ti da pa ti di o wala, koo a siisi u gba, ka pa lala, ti ni kpi.

eng: Abram traveled through the land as far as the sacred place at Shechem, at the oak of Moreh. The Canaanites lived in the land at that time.
local: Abram ni o niriba chaŋŋi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi ti paa More titintaa see, a yi Sekem tiŋŋa. A jemani maa Keenan dima daa na ba nisaaniŋi.

eng: Go out of the ark, you and your wife, your sons, and your sons wives with you.
local: Pimparaŋa, e ni e poga ni ya biduusi ni ba pogiba ya yi ŋariŋu maa puwa.

eng: In those days, giants lived on the earth and also afterward, when divine beings and human daughters had sexual relations and gave birth to children. These were the ancient heroes, famous men.
local: A jemani maa, Naawunni biduusi n daa n naa bipunsi maa, ba dogi biisi ba boona ba Nifilim. Barima n daa niŋi nivutintaasi, a mari kpeeŋu, yumdamta n gaari.

eng: Abram said to Sarai, Since shes your servant, do whatever you wish to her. So Sarai treated her harshly, and she ran away from Sarai.
local: Abram yeli u, Nyaasi, e guruŋu n na, nyini n su o kpeeŋu, niŋi u seema zaa e n kpabira. Sarai namsi u ga, o yi n zo.

eng: Then the LORD dispersed them from there over all of the earth, and they stopped building the city.
local: Lala n na Naawunni pisimi ba zaa yi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ba cheli tiŋkarigu maa meebu.

eng: The snake said to the woman, You wont die!.
local: Waafu maa yeli, Akai, ya ki kpi.

eng: The LORD appeared to Abram and said, I give this land to your descendants, so Abram built an altar there to the LORD who appeared to him.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma ta Abram saani ti yeli u, Paaliguso ni naa tira e yaŋsi maa n na la. Nisaaniŋi Abram me sariga nyisigu daaŋu ti Duuma, so n ta o saani maa.

eng: Bring out with you all the animals of every kind—birds, livestock, everything crawling on the ground—so that they may populate the earth, be fertile, and multiply on the earth.
local: Nyisi niinsi ni bunvuusira zaa n ba ŋariŋu la puu la paasi n yi, a ba niŋi dogi n wasi n paali tiŋŋa a.

eng: The LORD saw that humanity had become thoroughly evil on the earth and that every idea their minds thought up was always completely evil.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma daa n nye seema nisaaladima n niŋi ferebeeri dima, a niŋina yabeeri ga, jemani zaa.

eng: The LORD s messenger found Hagar at a spring in the desert, the spring on the road to Shur.
local: Duuma malaaka wa ti paa Heega, a yi bulugu n ba Sora soori, tambisigu tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

eng: Therefore, it is named Babel, because there the LORD mixed up the language of all the earth; and from there the LORD dispersed them over all the earth.
local: Tiŋkarigu maa yuuri n daa n niŋi Babilon, a kpali bo, nisaaniŋi Naawunni daa jebi niriba maa yatoga, ka za niŋi pisimi ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: God knows that on the day you eat from it, you will see clearly and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.
local: Naawunni baŋiya maa, ka ya di u, ya nufu ni yoo, ya ba maa Naawunni, a baŋŋi yasuma ni yabeeri.

eng: From there he traveled toward the mountains east of Bethel, and pitched his tent with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. There he built an altar to the LORD and worshipped in the LORD s name.
local: A nyaaŋa, o nyisigi n naa o zu to kiŋkagili sazu, a wara tiŋso n zi gunguntiri zu, ka niŋi tiŋkarigu Betel n ba wulunliya, ka Aii mi ba wuluŋpusigu. Ba sabi ba gbana suga, ka me sariga daaŋu, a kpeesi Duuma.

eng: So Noah went out of the ark with his sons, his wife, and his sons wives.
local: Niŋi Nuhu ni o poga ni ba biduusi ni ba pogiba yi ŋariŋu maa puwa.

eng: The LORD regretted making human beings on the earth, and he was heartbroken.
local: A daa n niŋi u sufusaaŋu ni beerim ga, o n maali nisaaladima niŋi durinye puwa, o yeli, ka n di baŋŋi ya.

eng: and said, Hagar! Sarais servant! Where did you come from and where are you going? She said, From Sarai my mistress. Im running away.
local: Ka boosi u, Heega, Sarai guruŋu, ya n na e yi, ka ya n na e wara? O yeli u, M zoni wara, ka che m diima.

eng: These are Terahs descendants. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Haran became the father of Lot.
local: Yumdamta nyaaŋa, daba yindaana n daa n ba niŋi, ba boona u Tera. O biisi n niŋi Abram, Naho ni Haran. Haran bii n daa niŋi Loti.

eng: The woman saw that the tree was beautiful with delicious food and that the tree would provide wisdom, so she took some of its fruit and ate it, and also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.
local: Niŋi n na, poga maa nyaasi, ka tii maa wala veela diibu, a niŋi u naŋgbaŋkuubu maa o n di n di u, ka mari yam. A zu, o po u di, ka naa n ti o sira gba o di.

eng: Then Abram set out toward the arid southern plain, making and breaking camp as he went.
local: Niŋi n na, o cherigi ziiziisi poli n jina, a naa o zu kpa Keenan kiŋkagili sazu.

eng: All the animals, all the livestock, all the birds, and everything crawling on the ground, came out of the ark by their families.
local: Lala n na ba nyisi bunvuusira ni niinsi la gba yi ŋariŋu maa puwa, a ni a peerigusi.

eng: So the LORD said, I will wipe off of the land the human race that Ive created: from human beings to livestock to the crawling things to the birds in the skies, because I regret I ever made them.
local: Ka yeli, M ni muri nisaaladima, buŋkobiri, bunvuurigusi, a ni niinsi. A kpali ba beebu ti m sufu saami.

eng: The LORD s messenger said to her, Go back to your mistress. Put up with her harsh treatment of you.
local: O yeli u, Pori n gaa o saani, a wa ti niŋi o guruŋu.

eng: Haran died while with his father Terah in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
local: Haran daa n kpini o yiri gbagba, ba boona Waa, a yi Babilon paaligu, ka o ba na ba niŋi.

eng: Then they both saw clearly and knew that they were naked. So they sewed fig leaves together and made garments for themselves.
local: Noba maa niŋi, ba nufu yoo, ba nye maa ba bi so sen, a zu ba naa tii vayelima se n tomi taaba, a so.

eng: When a famine struck the land, Abram went down toward Egypt to live as an immigrant since the famine was so severe in the land.
local: A jemani, kom n daa n lini Keenan, bindira yela daa ba toom, Abram nyisigi n gaa Yijipiti paaligu, o wa ti ba niŋi daba ayi.

eng: Noah built an altar to the LORD . He took some of the clean large animals and some of the clean birds, and placed entirely burned offerings on the altar.
local: Niŋi n na, Nuhu laŋsi kuga a maali sariga nyisigu ziya, ka nyisi buŋkobiri la ni niinsi la seesi n daga sariga nyisigu, a nyisi sariga, a kusi a ti Naawunni.

eng: But as for Noah, the LORD approved of him.
local: Ama, Nuhu wune yela daa n peri Duuma.

eng: The LORD s messenger also said to her, I will give you many children, so many they cant be counted.
local: Ka o lee n yeli u, M ni ti i yaŋdamta seesi nira zaa di n ki tee n kaali.

eng: Abram and Nahor both married; Abrams wife was Sarai, and Nahors wife was Milcah the daughter of Haran, father of both Milcah and Iscah.
local: Abram naa Sarai, ka Naho mi naa Milika, Haran bipuŋŋu, o biisi maa yinni n niŋi Isika.

eng: During that days cool evening breeze, they heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden; and the man and his wife hid themselves from the LORD God in the middle of the gardens trees.
local: Dindaari maa zaanomaasiri, ba wumi Duuma Naawunni n chaŋina pu maa niŋi, ba zo n sogira tiisi maa puwa.

eng: Just before he arrived in Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, I know you are a good-looking woman.
local: Jemanso ba n ya n loo n du Yijipiti paaligu, o yeli o poga Sarai, E veela ga.

eng: The LORD smelled the pleasing scent, and the LORD thought to himself, I will not curse the fertile land anymore because of human beings since the ideas of the human mind are evil from their youth. I will never again destroy every living thing as I have done.
local: Naawunni ni wumi bunvuusira la sariga ŋuwa, o yeli o maŋŋa, M ki lee n si tiŋŋa a noori, a kpali niriba yaniŋisa zu. M baŋiya maa nisaala bibiilim niŋi zaa, fere feera niŋini yabeeri, m ki lee n muri bunvuusira seema ni daa n niŋi la zaa.

eng: These are Noahs descendants. In his generation, Noah was a moral and exemplary man; he walked with God.
local: Nuhu yela n ŋa. O ko n daa n zora Naawunni, ka dagiri bi tabi u.

eng: The LORD s messenger said to her, You are now pregnant and will give birth to a son. You will name him Ishmael because the LORD has heard about your harsh treatment.
local: E ni dogi biduwa, ka digili o yuuri Isimeel, a kpali Duuma wumi e namsigu kumsi.

eng: Sarai was unable to have children.
local: Sarai daa niŋini poginyee.

eng: The LORD God called to the man and said to him, Where are you.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni booli daba maa, Ya n na e be.

eng: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, This is his wife, and they will kill me but let you live.
local: Ka Yijipiti dima nye i, ba ni baŋŋi maa m poga n niŋi i. A kpali lala zu, ba ni ku ma, ka cheli i.

eng: As long as the earth exists, seedtime and harvest, cold and hot, summer and autumn, day and night will not cease.
local: Za jemani a wa ti paa durinye nyisigirisim, jemani ni ba niŋi ti n ŋa binaa, bumbutigu ni a naagirisim, waari ni zibiiŋu, wuuni ni seeniŋi, bee ni yiiŋu, a zaa ni mari a jemani.

eng: Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: O daa n mari biduusi bata, Sem, Ham ni Jafeti.

eng: He will be a wild mule of a man; he will fight everyone, and they will fight him. He will live at odds with all his relatives.
local: Ama, e bii maa ni ba maa buŋŋu n galimi, o ni zabira niriba, ka niriba gba ba zabira u, meesim ki ba o ni o mabiisi puwa.

eng: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot , and his son Abrams wife, Sarai his daughter-in-law. They left Ur of the Chaldeans for the land of Canaan, and arriving at Haran, they settled there.
local: Niŋi n na, Tera mari o bii Abram ni o yaŋŋu Loti, Haran biya, o deempoga Sarai, Abram poga, a nyisigi n yi Waa n ba Babilon paaligu, a wara Keenan paaligu. Ba n wara maa, ba wa ti paa seela ba n boona Haran, ka zi niŋi.

eng: The man replied, I heard your sound in the garden; I was afraid because I was naked, and I hid myself.
local: Daba maa yeli, M wumini e n chaŋina pu la niŋi, dabeem gbaa ma, a kpali ni mari alibana zu, ma sogi.

eng: So tell them you are my sister so that they will treat me well for your sake, and I will survive because of you.
local: Yeli ba maa m sunzopoga n niŋi i, lala wune, ba ki ku ma, ka mari ma nene, a kpali e zu.

eng: In Gods sight, the earth had become corrupt and was filled with violence.
local: Ama durinye niriba zaa daa n niŋina yabeeri Naawunni nufu niŋi, a wusira kpeeŋu.

eng: Hagar named the LORD who spoke to her, You are El Roi because she said, Can I still see after he saw me.
local: Niŋi n na, Heega boosi o maŋŋa, Yamonsi n na ma nye Naawunni, ka na ba nyevuri puwa? Lala zu, o booli Duuma, so n togsi o saani la, Naawunni so n nyaara ma.

eng: He said, Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat from the tree, which I commanded you not to eat.
local: Naawunni boosi u, Ŋani n yeli i ni e marini alibana? E dini tiso ni yeli e da di la wala la.

eng: Terah lived years, and he died in Haran.
local: Haran tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi n na Tera daa n kpi, ka o niŋi yuma kobsiyi ni anu.

eng: When Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw how beautiful his wife was.
local: Jemanso ba n loo n kpe Yijipiti paaligu, Yijipiti dima maa nye maa o poga maa veela ga.

eng: God saw that the earth was corrupt, because all creatures behaved corruptly on the earth.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni nyaasi durinye niriba maa, ka ba zaa niŋini yabeniŋiniba.

eng: Therefore, that well is called Beer-lahai-roi; its the well between Kadesh and Bered.
local: A zu n na niriba boona bulugu so n do Kadesi ni Bered sunsuuniŋi la, Nyevuri daana so n nyaara ma bulugu.

eng: The man said, The woman you gave me, she gave me some fruit from the tree, and I ate.
local: Daba maa yeli u, Poga la e n naa ti ma la n ti ma tii maa walli ma di.

eng: When Pharaohs princes saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh; and the woman was taken into Pharaohs household.
local: Yijipiti paaligu naaba maa tumtuminiba seesi n nye poga maa wa ti yeli u seema o n veela. A kpali lala zu, ba mari u gaa naaba maa yiri.

eng: The LORD God said to the woman, What have you done?! And the woman said, The snake tricked me, and I ate.
local: Duuma Naawunni boosi poga maa, Bo n na e niŋi lala? Poga maa yeli u, Waafu la n yomsi ma, ma di u.

eng: Hagar gave birth to a son for Abram, and Abram named him Ishmael.
local: A nyaaŋa, Heega dogi biduu ti Abram, o digili u Isimeel.

eng: God said to Noah, The end has come for all creatures, since they have filled the earth with violence. I am now about to destroy them along with the earth.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli Nuhu, A kpali durinye ŋa yabeeri ni kpeeŋu wusigu a zu, m ni muri u. Bunso zu ni ya n muri nisaaladima ni tiŋgbaŋaa zaa n na la.

eng: Things went well for Abram because of her: he acquired flocks, cattle, male donkeys, men servants, women servants, female donkeys, and camels.
local: Poga maa zu, naaba maa mari Abram nene, ka ti u buŋkobiri, peeri, buusi, naagi, buŋsi, gurunsi a ni nyogimasi.

eng: The LORD God said to the snake, Because you did this, you are the one cursed out of all the farm animals, out of all the wild animals. On your belly you will crawl, and dust you will eat every day of your life.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli waafu maa A kpali bunso e n niŋi a zu, a yi buŋkobiri zaa puwa, e ko ni nye n ŋa naŋgbansiibu, za zinaa wara e ni naa e puu vuura tiŋŋa, ka e dira tanni, a yi e nyevuri niŋi.

eng: Abram was years old when Hagar gave birth to Ishmael for Abram.
local: Lala jemani, ka Abram yuma pisinii ni ayoobu.

eng: so make a wooden ark. Make the ark with nesting places and cover it inside and out with tar.
local: A zu, kpabi dasuma kpaasi ŋarintintaa, ka maali diisi yi o puwa, ka naa kootaali taari o puu ni o nyaaŋa zaa.

eng: Then the LORD struck Pharaoh and his household with severe plagues because of Abrams wife Sarai.
local: Ama, a kpali naaba maa n naa Abram poga Sarai gaari o laanta la zu, Duuma ti toorabeeri gbaa o ni o laanta dima zaa.

eng: I will put contempt between you and the woman, between your offspring and hers. They will strike your head, but you will strike at their heels.
local: M ni ti dintiri o ba e ni poga maa sunsuuniŋi, ka e biisi ni o biisi gba ni niŋi dindima. O bii ni zaasi e zu, ka e mi dumi o nosiŋkpaari.

eng: This is how you should make it: four hundred fifty feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high.
local: N ŋa n na ma kpabira o zoogirisim, o wogilim, noba kobsinaasi ni pisinu, o yelim, noba pisiyapoin ni anu, o duubu mini, noba pisinaasi ni anu.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Abram and said, Whats this youve done to me? Why didnt you tell me she was your wife?.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa booli Abram, a ti boosi u, Bo n na e niŋi lala, wula n na e bi yeli ma maa e poga n na?.

eng: To the woman he said, I will make your pregnancy very painful; in pain you will bear children. You will desire your husband, but he will rule over you.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli poga maa, M ni ti e mari sufusaaŋu, ka nye beerim, dindaso e n ya n dogi. Ŋama nyaaŋa, e sira yela ni ba e sufu niŋi ga, ka o mini n ni niŋi e zudaana.

eng: Make a roof for the ark and complete it one foot from the top. Put a door in its side. In the hold below, make the second and third decks.
local: Pili ŋariŋu maa, ka niŋi o saazu sugulugu, o yelim o niŋi maa noba ayi, ka niŋi dinaŋgbanluŋko, ka maali diisi ata tomi taaba zu, a maa kpaa, ka a mari gbinniŋi, sunsuuniŋi, a ni saazu.

eng: Why did you say, Shes my sister, so that I made her my wife? Now, heres your wife. Take her and go.
local: Wula zu n na e yeli maa e sunzopoga n na, ka ti ma naag u loobi m poga? E poga n na la, mari u kunna.

eng: To the man he said, Because you listened to your wifes voice and you ate from the tree that I commanded, Dont eat from it, cursed is the fertile land because of you; in pain you will eat from it every day of your life.
local: Niŋi n na, o yeli daba maa gba, A kpali e ni wumi n ti e poga, ka di tiwaliso ni yeli ya da di la zu, m si tiŋgbaŋaa noori, ka pa e tumi n beligi ga, ka nye bindirigu, a yi e beebu puwa.

eng: I am now bringing the floodwaters over the earth to destroy everything under the sky that breathes. Everything on earth is about to take its last breath.
local: A kpali bo, m ya n ti ni koom paali durinye zaa, a muri bunvuusira zaa nyem.

eng: Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning Abram, and they expelled him with his wife and everything he had.
local: Naaba maa ti o nivusi mari Abram yi paaligu maa niŋi, a ni o poga ni binseesi zaa ba n mari.

eng: Weeds and thistles will grow for you, even as you eat the fields plants;.
local: Tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni buli matimari ni goosi, ka ya dira moo niŋi tiisi wala.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with you. You will go into the ark together with your sons, your wife, and your sons wives.
local: Ama nyiwune, m ya n ti i ni noori. Lala jemani maa ti paa, mari e poga, e biisi, a ni ba pogiba kpe ŋariŋu maa.

eng: by the sweat of your face you will eat bread— until you return to the fertile land, since from it you were taken; you are soil, to the soil you will return.
local: E ni tumi ga, a nyee zibiiŋu, e bindirigu zu, a yi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ka e lee n pori n kpe tanni maa niŋi. Tanni ba naa n me i, e ni lee n pori n loobi tanni.

eng: From all living things—from all creatures—you are to bring a pair, male and female, into the ark with you to keep them alive.
local: A lee n naa n paasi, mari buŋkobiri ni niinsi zaa baligu ayaayi, daba ni poga, a kpe ŋariŋu maa. Ka nyaasira a.

eng: The man named his wife Eve because she is the mother of everyone who lives.
local: Adam digili o poga maa yuuri Hawa, o gbinni n niŋi nisaaladima zaa ma.

eng: Take some from every kind of food and stow it as food for you and for the animals.
local: Ka e laŋsi bindira baligu zaa niŋi ŋariŋu maa puwa, a niŋi ya ni buŋkobiri maa bindira.

eng: The LORD God made the man and his wife leather clothes and dressed them.
local: Ka Duuma Naawunni naa buŋkobiri gbana maali bimpinna pili Adam ni o poga.

eng: Noah did everything exactly as God commanded him.
local: Niŋi n na, Nuhu niŋi bunso zaa Naawunni n yeli u la.
