eng: The LORD God said, The human being has now become like one of us, knowing good and evil. Now, so he doesnt stretch out his hand and take also from the tree of life and eat and live forever.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli, Pimparaŋa nisaala pa loobini maa tirima, a baŋŋi yasuma ni yabeeri. A bi daga ti n cheli o ba kpe, ka tee n nye soori n lee n di tiso n tira nyevuri la walli, ka ki lee n kpi, ka ba niŋi n wara.

eng: the LORD God sent him out of the garden of Eden to farm the fertile land from which he was taken.
local: A zu, Duuma Naawunni nyisi u yi pu maa n ba Idin paaligu la, o wa ti koora tiŋgbanso o n ŋma n me u la niŋi.

eng: He drove out the human. To the east of the garden of Eden, he stationed winged creatures wielding flaming swords to guard the way to the tree of life.
local: O n nyisi u yi pu maa niŋi la, o ti malaakasi ni tokobiso n leegira bugum poli n jinna, pu maa wuluŋpusigu, a kogira nisaaniŋi, nira zaa o da nye soori ta nyevuri tii maa ziya.

eng: The LORD appeared to Abraham at the oaks of Mamre while he sat at the entrance of his tent in the days heat.
local: Dindaso wuntonkpaligu, Duuma daa n ta Ibrahim saani, a leeli Mamiri titintaasi. Ka Ibrahim daa n zinni o gbana sugiri noori. Jemanso zii n bii.

eng: He looked up and suddenly saw three men standing near him. As soon as he saw them, he ran from his tent entrance to greet them and bowed deeply.
local: Ka lalli o zu saa, o nyeni dabsi bata n za n leeli u. Noba maa niŋi o n si n nye ba, o chee n we ba saani, a wa ti gba, a naag o gbee zulisi tiŋŋa.

eng: He said, Sirs, if you would be so kind, dont just pass by your servant.
local: O suusi ba yeli, M yidaana jaande, ka m yela peri i, da pa ti saa m yiri.

eng: Let a little water be brought so you may wash your feet and refresh yourselves under the tree.
local: M ni ti ba ŋoo koom ti ti ya, ya pegi ya noba, ya zi tiyaa gbinni vuusira, m chena.

eng: Let me offer you a little bread so you will feel stronger, and after that you may leave your servant and go on your way—since you have visited your servant. They responded, Fine. Do just as you have said.
local: M ni mari bindira beela gba ta, a ni paasi ya niŋi, ya cheni ŋa puwa. Ya naa mani saa, ya n ta m laanta ŋa. Ba sagi, ka yeli u, Niŋi seema e ni yeli maa.

eng: So Abraham hurried to Sarah at his tent and said, Hurry! Knead three seahs of the finest flour and make some baked goods.
local: Ibrahim zo yomyom kpe o gbana sugiri puwa yeli Saara, Naag e zumsuma la bowate niŋi paanu yoom.

eng: Abraham ran to the cattle, took a healthy young calf, and gave it to a young servant, who prepared it quickly.
local: Ka chee n gaari o naagi puu wa ti gbaa nagipolli, so n mari kpaam, a ti ti o guruŋsi yindaana, o ku u, a maali u nene yoom ta.

eng: Then Abraham took butter, milk, and the calf that had been prepared, put the food in front of them, and stood under the tree near them as they ate.
local: O gbagba naa bisimkpaam, nagibisim, a ni valiga ti ti ba tii maa gbinni, ba dira, ka o za ba saani, tii maa gbinni.

eng: They said to him, Wheres your wife Sarah? And he said, Right here in the tent.
local: Niŋi n na, ba boosi u, E poga Saara baya? O yeli ba, ni o ba sugiri puwa.

eng: Then one of the men said, I will definitely return to you about this time next year. Then your wife Sarah will have a son! Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.
local: Ba yindaana yeli u, Yumini jemanaa zu, m ni pori n ta e saani kpe, e poga Saara ni dogi biduwa. Ka Saara begi dinoori wumina seema o n yetira maa.

eng: Now Abraham and Sarah were both very old. Sarah was no longer menstruating.
local: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.

eng: So Sarah laughed to herself, thinking, Im no longer able to have children and my husbands old.
local: A la zu, Saara la o maŋŋa, a yi o sufu niŋi, ka yeli, Pimparaŋa, seela m ni m sira zaa n kurigi n paag a, ni lee n tee n dogi.

eng: The LORD said to Abraham, Why did Sarah laugh and say, Me give birth? At my age.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma boosi Ibrahim, Wula n na Saara la, ka yeli ni o ki lee n dogi jemanso o n kurigaa?.

eng: Is anything too difficult for the LORD ? When I return to you about this time next year, Sarah will have a son.
local: Ya bo n ba toom ti Duuma? A maa seema ni yeli la, yumini jemani a zu, m ni lee n pori n ta, ka Saara dogi biduwa.

eng: Sarah lied and said, I didnt laugh, because she was frightened. But he said, No, you laughed.
local: A kpali Saara n daa zora dabeem zu, o chisi, M bi la. O yeli u, Yamonsi, e laya.

eng: The men got up from there and went over to look down on Sodom. Abraham was walking along with them to send them off.
local: Niŋi n na, niriba maa yi nisaaniŋi wa ti paa seela ba n di n za, ka nye Sodom, ka Ibrahim pa ba zu, o zaali ba soori.

eng: when the LORD said, Will I keep from Abraham what Im about to do?.
local: Ka Duuma yeli o sufu niŋi, M ki naa n sogi Ibrahim bunso ni ya n niŋi.

eng: Abraham will certainly become a great populous nation, and all the earths nations will be blessed because of him.
local: O yaŋsi ni ti niŋi zaaziikara dima ni kpeena baligu, o niŋi n na ma ni dolli n nafa balisi zaa.

eng: I have formed a relationship with him so that he will instruct his children and his household after him. And they will keep to the LORD s path, being moral and just so that the LORD can do for Abraham everything he said he would.
local: M nyisi u ni, o wusi o biisi ni o yaŋsi ba mari wumintiibu ti ma, ka niŋini bunso zaa ni tooli, a ni yamonsi. Ka ba yi niŋi lala, m ni niŋi n paali naŋgbanseesi zaa ni digili u la.

eng: Then the LORD said, The cries of injustice from Sodom and Gomorrah are countless, and their sin is very serious!.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli Ibrahim, Yadagiri seesi Sodom ni Gomora dima n niŋina la ti ba yabeeri zoo ga.

eng: I will go down now to examine the cries of injustice that have reached me. Have they really done all this? If not, I want to know.
local: A daga ni wa ti nyaasi, jiri n na bee yamonsi.

eng: The men turned away and walked toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing in front of the LORD .
local: Niŋi n na, dabsi bayi nyisigi n yi ba saani, a naa ba nufu to Sodom soori wara, ama ka Duuma wune na ba Ibrahim saani.

eng: Abraham approached and said, Will you really sweep away the innocent with the guilty?.
local: Ibrahim vuu n paa Duuma, ka boosi u, Yamonsitiri, e ya n murini yasuma ni yabeeri dima zaa?.

eng: What if there are fifty innocent people in the city? Will you really sweep it away and not save the place for the sake of the fifty innocent people in it?.
local: Ka nivusuma pisinu ba tiŋŋa maa puwa, e ni muri tiŋŋa maa zaa? E ki cheli u kpali niriba pisinu maa zu?.

eng: Its not like you to do this, killing the innocent with the guilty as if there were no difference. Its not like you! Will the judge of all the earth not act justly.
local: Yamonsitiri, e ki ku nivusuma ni nivubeeri maa zaa. Lala ki tee n niŋi. Ka e yi niŋi lala, nivusuma la ni nye tubidarigirisim paasi nivubeeri la niŋi. Lala ki tee n niŋi. Durinye ŋa zaa seriya dira daga o niŋi yela ni a soori.

eng: The LORD said, If I find fifty innocent people in the city of Sodom, I will save it because of them.
local: Duuma yeli, Ka nivusuma pisinu yi ba Sodom, a kpali ba zu, m ni che tiŋŋa maa.

eng: Abraham responded, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, even though Im just soil and ash.
local: Ibrahim lee n togsi, M Duuma, jaande, che n paŋŋi ma, dabilim so ni kpari n ma n togsira e saani a, nisaala n niŋi ma, m ka soori zaa m tee n yeli i sen.

eng: what if there are five fewer innocent people than fifty? Will you destroy the whole city over just five? The LORD said, If I find forty-five there, I wont destroy it.
local: Ama ka nivusuma pisinaasi ni banu n ba niŋi, ka ba bi paa pisinu, e ni muri tiŋŋa maa? Duuma yeli, M ki muri tiŋŋa maa, ka nivusuma pisinaasi ni banu n ba niŋi.

eng: Once again Abraham spoke, What if forty are there? The LORD said, For the sake of forty, I will do nothing.
local: Ibrahim lee n boosi u, Ka a niŋi maa niriba pisinaasi ko n na? O yeli, M ki muri ba, ka ba paa niriba pisinaasi.

eng: He said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak. What if thirty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it if I find thirty there.
local: Ibrahim suusi u, Jaande, da je suuri, Duuma, a daga m na togsi. Ka ba niŋi maa nivusuma pisita ko? O yeli u, M ki muri tiŋŋa maa, ka ba ba pisita.

eng: Abraham said, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, what if twenty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it, for the sake of twenty.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, Jaande, M Duuma, cheli n togsi e saani. Ka niriba pisiyi ko n ba niŋi? O yeli u, M ki muri tiŋŋa maa, ka niriba pisiyi ko ba niŋi.

eng: Abraham said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak just once more. What if there are ten? And the LORD said, I will not destroy it because of those ten.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, Jaande, da ti e suuri nyisigi, Duuma, m kpabira n togsi bunluŋko paasi. Ka nivusi pii ko ba niŋi? O yeli u, M ki muri ba, ka niriba pii ko ba niŋi.

eng: When the LORD finished speaking with Abraham, he left; but Abraham stayed there in that place.
local: O ni Ibrahim ni togsi n baasi, Duuma gaari, ka Ibrahim pori n gaari o yiri.

eng: The two messengers entered Sodom in the evening. Lot, who was sitting at the gate of Sodom, saw them, got up to greet them, and bowed low.
local: Dindaari maa zaanoori, malaakasi bayi la ta Sodom, ka Loti ba n zi tiŋŋa maa voori noori. Noba maa niŋi, o nye ba, ka nyisigi n wa ti tuusi ba. A gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa.

eng: He said, Come to your servants house, spend the night, and wash your feet. Then you can get up early and go on your way. But they said, No, we will spend the night in the town square.
local: Ka yeli ba, Ya gaafira, m bani kpe n tumini ya, a zu, ya jaande, ka ta m laanta. Ni ti ya koom, ya pegi ya noba, ka do bee o chee ya nyisigi n wara. Ba yeli u, Akai, kulooni ŋa zu n na ti n ya do yiiŋaa.

eng: He pleaded earnestly with them, so they went with him and entered his house. He made a big meal for them, even baking unleavened bread, and they ate.
local: O suusi ba ga, ba sagi, ka we o laanta, o niŋi bindira ti ba, ba di.

eng: Before they went to bed, the men of the city of Sodom—everyone from the youngest to the oldest—surrounded the house.
local: Poin, ka saamba maa ba do, tiŋŋa maa dabsi ti poli n jili laanta maa. Dabsi zaa, bipola ni dabkura.

eng: and called to Lot, Where are the men who arrived tonight? Bring them out to us so that we may have sex with them.
local: Ba booli Loti tasi n boosi u, Dabsi la n ta e saani yiiŋaa la ba ya? Mari ba yi n ta, ti mari ba do.

eng: Lot went out toward the entrance, closed the door behind him.
local: Loti yi, ka yo dinoori maa, ka ta ba saani.

eng: and said, My brothers, dont do such an evil thing.
local: O yeli ba, M zodima, m suusira ya ni, ya da pa ti niŋi niribaa, n ŋa yabeegaa.

eng: Ive got two daughters who are virgins. Let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them whatever you wish. But dont do anything to these men because they are now under the protection of my roof.
local: Ya nyaasi, m mari suŋgurisi bayi, seesi na n bi baŋŋi dabsi. Ya cheli m mari ba yi n ti ya, ya n tee n niŋi ba seema zaa ya n kpabira. Ama, ya da niŋi dabsaa yelli zaa, m saamba n na, ka a daga ni kogi ba.

eng: They said, Get out of the way! And they continued, Does this immigrant want to judge us? Now we will hurt you more than we will hurt them. They pushed Lot back and came close to breaking down the door.
local: Niŋi n na, ba yeli u, Maali n za ti nufu niŋi, ka ti ti soori, nyini saana, ŋani n niŋi i, e wusi ti bunso ti n di niŋi? Ti ni namsi i gaa ba. Ba turi Loti basi nyaaŋa, ka gaa ba wa ti saami dinoori maa.

eng: The men inside reached out and pulled Lot back into the house with them and slammed the door.
local: Dabsi bayi maa ba laanta ka darigi Loti kpe, ka yo dinoori maa.

eng: Then the messengers blinded the men near the entrance of the house, from the youngest to the oldest, so that they groped around trying to find the entrance.
local: Ka ba ti niriba maa zaa, nivukura ni bipola, n ba yaŋŋa la zoomi, ba kpabi dinoori maa je.

eng: The men said to Lot, Whos still with you here? Take away from this place your sons-in-law, your sons, your daughters, and everyone else you have in the city.
local: Dabsi bayi maa yeli Loti, Ka e mari niriba kpe, biduusi, bipunsi, deendabsi koo e nira zaa n ba tiŋaa niŋi, mari ba zaa yi kpe.

eng: because we are about to destroy this place. The LORD has found the cries of injustice so serious that the LORD sent us to destroy it.
local: A kpali, ti ya n saami u ni. Duuma wumini yabeseesi tiŋaa dima ni niŋina, ka tumi ti, ti ti saami u.

eng: Lot went to speak to his sons-in-law, married to his daughters, and said, Get up and get out of this place because the LORD is about to destroy the city. But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.
local: Niŋi n na, Loti wa ti yeli o deendima seesi n belimina o bipunsi la, Ya yi kpe yomyom, Duuma ya n saamini kpe. Ba nyaasi maa o yomsira ba ni.

eng: When dawn broke, the messengers urged Lot, Get up and take your wife and your two daughters who are here so that you are not swept away because of the evil in this city.
local: Bogufiili, nodakumisi jemani, malaakasi maa yeli Loti, ni ba yi yoom. Mari e poga ni e bipunsi bayi ŋa yi, lala jemani ya ki koŋŋi ya nyevuya, jemanso tiŋaa n ya n saami a.

eng: He hesitated, but because the LORD intended to save him, the men grabbed him, his wife, and two daughters by the hand, took him out, and left him outside the city.
local: Loti na mari nagima, ama Duuma zo u nimbaa, lala zu, dabsi bayi maa gbaa o ni o poga ni biisi nuusi darigi ba yi tiŋŋa maa puwa.

eng: After getting them out, the men said, Save your lives! Dont look back! And dont stay in the valley. Escape to the mountains so that you are not swept away.
local: Malaakasi maa yindaana yeli ba, Ya chee, a moligi ya nyevuya. Ya da pa loobi n nyaasi nyaaŋa, ka da pa za nantaŋaa puwa. Ya chee n du zoogaa, lala wune, ya ki kpi.

eng: But Lot said to them, No, my lords, please.
local: Niŋi n na, Loti yeli u, Jaande, da ti ti chee lala.

eng: Youve done me a favor and have been so kind to save my life. But I cant escape to the mountains since the catastrophe might overtake me there and Id die.
local: Ya niŋi yatintaa ga ti ma, ya ni moligi n nyevuraa. Ama, zoori maa zu ba wogilim ga, ka m n ya n chee paa nisaaniŋi, daaŋu maa ni gbaa ma, m kpi, ka m na bi paa nisaaniŋi.

eng: This city here is close enough to flee to, and its small. Its small, right? Let me escape there, and my life will be saved.
local: Nyaasi tiŋbilaa. Wune n leeli, ka bi zoo. Cheli m du n gaa niŋi. Niŋi n na, ma ni nye moligirisim.

eng: He said to Lot, Ill do this for you as well; I wont overthrow the city that you have described.
local: O yeli u, O veela, m sagiya, m ki saami lala tiŋŋa maa.

eng: Hurry! Escape to it! I cant do anything until you get there. That is why the name of the city is Zoar.
local: Chee yomyom, m ki tee n niŋi bunso zaa, ama, e paani niŋi poin. A kpali Loti ni booli u tiŋbila la zu, ba boona u Zowa.

eng: As the sun rose over the earth, Lot arrived in Zoar;.
local: Wuluŋu daa n si n pusirini, ka Loti paa zowa.

eng: and the LORD rained down burning asphalt from the skies onto Sodom and Gomorrah.
local: Noba maa niŋi, Duuma ti jiribi ni bugum yi saa ti sigi Sodom ni Gomora niŋi.

eng: The LORD destroyed these cities, the entire valley, everyone who lived in the cities, and all of the fertile lands vegetation.
local: a di tiŋsi ayi maa ni a tiŋgbana, a ni a niriba ni bumbula zaa nyem.

eng: When Lots wife looked back, she turned into a pillar of salt.
local: Ama, Loti poga loobi n nyaasi, ka loobi yaarim kugiri.

eng: Abraham set out early for the place where he had stood with the LORD .
local: A n chee bee boguuniŋi, Ibrahim chaŋŋi yoom ta seela o ni Duuma n si n za la.

eng: and looked out over Sodom and Gomorrah and over all the land of the valley. He saw the smoke from the land rise like the smoke from a kiln.
local: ka nyaasi Sodom ni Gomora a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa, o nyeni bugum karigu wusi n duura tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: When God destroyed the cities in the valley, God remembered Abraham and sent Lot away from the disaster that overtook the cities in which Lot had lived.
local: Jemanso Naawunni n daa n muri tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni tiŋsi ayi la, o teesi Ibrahim yela, ka nyisi Loti yi tiŋŋa maa mutigu niŋi, o nye tuligirisim.

eng: Since Lot had become fearful of living in Zoar, he and his two daughters headed up from Zoar and settled in the mountains where he and his two daughters lived in a cave.
local: Loti daa n zorini dabeem, a kpali o n zi Zowa la zu, o ni o bipunsi bayi maa yi tiŋŋa maa puu zo n wa ti ba zovoori puwa.

eng: The older daughter said to the younger, Our father is old, and there are no men in the land to sleep with us as is the custom everywhere.
local: Bikpeema daana la yeli o sunzo, Ti ba kurigiya, dabsi mi ka tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi ti n di n kulisi ba, a dogi biisi seema n daga.

eng: Come on, lets give our father wine to drink, lie down with him, and well have children from our father.
local: Ti ti m ba o kuuli, lala ti ni tee n mari u do, a dolli o niŋi dogi biisi.

eng: That night they served their father wine, and the older daughter went in and lay down with her father, without him noticing when she lay down or got up.
local: A dindaari yiiŋu, ba ti u daam o ŋu, kpeemdaana la mari o ba do. Ama, o n daa kuuli n gali zu, o bi baŋŋi bunso n niŋi.

eng: The next day the older daughter said to the younger, Since I lay down with our father last night, lets serve him wine tonight too, and you go in and lie down with him so that we will both have children from our father.
local: Bee n chee, bikpeema la yeli o sunzo, M mari u do yiiŋu la, ti lee n ti o kuuli yiiŋaa, ka e mi nunu ya do. Lala jemani, ti zaa ni dolli m ba niŋi dogi biisi, ti baligaa o da muri.

eng: They served their father wine that night also, and the younger daughter lay down with him, without him knowing when she lay down or got up.
local: A yiiŋu, ba lee n ŋuusi u, ka bibila daana maa mari u do. Kuuligu aligaaya zu, ŋala gba o bi baŋŋi.

eng: Both of Lots daughters became pregnant by their father.
local: N ŋa soori a niŋi n na Loti bipunsi bayi ŋa dolli ba ba niŋi nye puusi.

eng: The older daughter gave birth to a son and named him Moab. He is the ancestor of todays Moabites.
local: Kpeema daana la dogi biduwa, ka digili o yuuri Mowabi, nivuseesi ba n boona Mowabi dima la yaadaba n na la.

eng: The younger daughter also gave birth to a son and named him Ben-ammi. He is the ancestor of todays Ammonites.
local: Bibila daana la gba dogi biduwa, ka digili o yuuri Benami. Wune n niŋi Amoni dima yaadaba.

eng: Abraham traveled from there toward the land of the arid southern plain, and he settled as an immigrant in Gerar, between Kadesh and Shur.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, Ibrahim nyisigi Mamiri gaa Keenan sazu kiŋkagili wa ti zi Kadesi ni Sora sunsuuniŋi. A nyaaŋa, o lee n wa ti zi Gera.

eng: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, Shes my sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar took her into his household.
local: ka wusi maa Saara niŋini o sunzopoga. Gera naaba, Abimeliki ti ba naa Saara ti ti u.

eng: But God appeared to Abimelech that night in a dream and said to him, You are as good as dead because of this woman you have taken. She is a married woman.
local: Dindaso yiiŋu Naawunni ta o saani yi zaasim niŋi, a ti yeli u, E ni kpi, a kpali e n naa poga a, o kulisirini.

eng: Now Abimelech hadnt gone near her, and he said, Lord, will you really put an innocent nation to death?.
local: Ama, Abimeliki daa na bi kpabi u. Niŋi n na, o yeli, Duuma, nivupeeli n niŋi ma. E ni ku m ni m niriba a?.

eng: Didnt he say to me, Shes my sister, and didnt she—even she—say, Hes my brother? My intentions were pure, and I acted innocently when I did this.
local: Ibrahim gbagba n yeli maa o sunzopoga n na, ka o gba yeli lala, m naagi u ni a ni feresuŋŋu, m bi niŋi yabee.

eng: God said to him in the dream, I know that your intentions were pure when you did this. In fact, I kept you from sinning against me. Thats why I didnt allow you to touch her.
local: Naawunni yeli u zaasim maa niŋi, Mm, m baŋiya maa e naagi u ni, a ni feresuŋŋu, a zu ma ti e bi kpabi u, a niŋi yabee kpe n niŋi la.

eng: Now return the mans wife. Hes a prophet; he will pray for you so you may live. But if you dont return her, know that you and everyone with you will die.
local: Pimparaŋa, pori n naag u ti o sira, Naawun tumu n na, ka o suusi Naawunni ti i, lala jemani, e ki kpi. Ama ka e yi bi pori u ti u, m kpaasira e tubiri maa e ni kpi, e ni e niriba zaa.

eng: Abimelech got up early in the morning and summoned all of his servants. When he told them everything that had happened, the men were terrified.
local: Bee n chee boguuniŋi, Abimeliki booli o kpemba zaa, a yeli ba bunso zaa n ba niŋi, dabeem gbaa ba.

eng: Then Abimelech summoned Abraham and said to him, What have you done to us? What sin did I commit against you that you have brought this terrible sin to me and my kingdom, by doing to me something that simply isnt done.
local: Niŋi n na, Abimeliki booli Ibrahim, a ti boosi u, Bo n na e niŋi ti n ŋa? Bo yabee n na ma niŋi i, a taasi e ti yadagaa o ta m ni m paaligu zaa niŋi? E niŋi ma yaso n bi daga nira zaa, o niŋi o todaana.

eng: Abimelech said to Abraham, What were you thinking when you did this thing.
local: Wula zu e niŋi lala.

eng: Abraham said, I thought to myself, No one reveres God here and they will kill me to get my wife.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, M fe ni maa nira zaa bi zora Naawunni kpe, a kpali lala zu, ba ni ku ma ka naa m poga maa.

eng: She is, truthfully, my sister—my fathers daughter but not my mothers daughter—and shes now my wife.
local: M sunzopoga n na yamonsi, m ba bii n na, ama, ka m ma biya. Ka ma naag u.

eng: When God led me away from my fathers household, I said to her, This is the loyalty I expect from you: in each place we visit, tell them, He is my brother.
local: A zu jemanso Naawunni n nyisi ma yi m ba laanta wara paaligu yindaana tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi la, m yeli u ni maa ka ti si n gaa seela zaa o paasi m niŋi yeli maa o sunzodaba n niŋi ma.

eng: Abimelech took flocks, cattle, male servants, and female servants, and gave them to Abraham; and Abimelech returned his wife Sarah.
local: Niŋi n na, Abimeliki naag o poga pori n ti u, ka ti u peeri, naagi a ni gurunsi.

eng: Abimelech said, My land is here available to you. Live wherever you wish.
local: Ka yeli Ibrahim, M tiŋgbaŋŋu n na la, seela zaa n peri i, e zima.

eng: To Sarah, he said, Ive given your brother one thousand pieces of silver. It means that neither you nor anyone with you has done anything wrong. Everything has been set right.
local: O yeli Saara, m naa ni gbitisi tusiri ti e sunzodaba, a wusi maa nivupeeli n niŋi i, a yi seesi zaa n ba kpe, so zaa ni baŋŋi maa e zu pegiya.

eng: Abraham prayed to God; and God restored Abimelech, his wife, and his women servants to health, and they were able to have children.
local: A kpali ya so n niŋi, a yi Ibrahim poga Saara la zu, Naawunni niŋi u maa poga zaa o da tee n dogi, a yi nayiri maa. A zu, Ibrahim suusi Naawun ti Abimeliki.

eng: Because of the incident with Abrahams wife Sarah, the LORD had kept all of the women in Abimelechs household from having children.
local: Naawun tibi u, ka tibi o poga ni guruŋpogiseesi zaa n ba niŋi, lala jemani, ba zaa ni tee n dogi biisi.

eng: The LORD was attentive to Sarah just as he had said, and the LORD carried out just what he had promised her.
local: Duuma nafa Saara, a maa seema o ti noori la.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Abraham when he was old, at the very time God had told him.
local: o mari puwa, a dogi biduu ti Ibrahim jemanso o n kurigi. Ba daa n dogini bii maa jemanso Naawun n daa zaali maa ba n di n dogi u.

eng: Abraham named his son—the one Sarah bore him—Isaac.
local: Ibrahim digili o yuuri Yisaaka.

eng: Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old just as God had commanded him.
local: O dabaanii daari Ibrahim ŋma o yoori, a maa seema Naawunni n zaali u la.

eng: Abraham was years old when his son Isaac was born.
local: Ba n daa dogi Yisaaka maa, ka Ibrahim daa n niŋini yuma koowa.

eng: Sarah said, God has given me laughter. Everyone who hears about it will laugh with me.
local: Saara yeli, Naawunni mari sufupeelim ni laari ti ti ma, niso zaa ni wumi n ŋa ni paa ma la.

eng: She said, Who could have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse sons? But now Ive given birth to a son when he was old.
local: Niŋi n na, o lee n maali n yeli, Ŋani n di n tee n yeli Ibrahim maa Saara ni dogi biisi? Ama a zaa jaga, m dogi biduu ti u, a yi o nivukuritiri jemani.

eng: After these events, God tested Abraham and said to him, Abraham! Abraham answered, Im here.
local: Jemanso n daa ti paa, Naawunni niŋi Ibrahim yeride nyaasi. A booli u yeli, Ibrahim. O sagi, Mani n ŋa.

eng: The boy grew and stopped nursing. On the day he stopped nursing, Abraham prepared a huge banquet.
local: Niŋi n na, bii maa n karigi, dindaso ba n ŋma u bisiri, Ibrahim daa n ti ba di sufupeelim bindirigbaliŋu.

eng: God said, Take your son, your only son whom you love, Isaac, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him up as an entirely burned offering there on one of the mountains that I will show you.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli u, Naag e biduluŋko, Yisaaka, so e n kpabira ga la, a gaa Moriya tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a wa ti ku u, a kusi bugum, a nyisi sariga ti ma, a yi zo so ni ya n wusi i ŋa zu.

eng: Sarah saw Hagars son laughing, the one Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham.
local: Ama, Saara nyeya maa, biduso Heega, Yijipiti poga la n dogi n ti Ibrahim la n deemina.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, harnessed his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, together with his son Isaac. He split the wood for the entirely burned offering, set out, and went to the place God had described to him.
local: A n chee bee bogufiili, Ibrahim kpabi sariga daari pa o buŋŋu niŋi, ka mari o gurunsi bayi, a naa Yisaaka paasi, a wara seela Naawunni n wusi u la.

eng: So she said to Abraham, Send this servant away with her son! This servants son wont share the inheritance with my son Isaac.
local: Saara nye ba, ka yeli Ibrahim, Ti guruŋpogaa ni o biyaa ba wara. Pogaa biyaa bi daga o barigi e bini, kapete seesi m bii Yisaaka ni ya n ti di.

eng: Sarah lived to be years old; this was how long she lived.
local: Saara daa n niŋini yuma koowa ni pisiyi ni ayapoin.

eng: On the third day, Abraham looked up and saw the place at a distance.
local: A dabaata daari, cheni maa puwa, Ibrahim nyaasi yii wogila, ka nye seela n na maa.

eng: This upset Abraham terribly because the boy was his son.
local: N ŋa yelaa niŋi Ibrahim sufusaaŋu ga, a kpali Isimeel gba niŋini o biya.

eng: She died in Kiriath-arba, that is, in Hebron, in the land of Canaan; and Abraham cried out in grief and wept for Sarah.
local: ka kpi Kiriyaf-Aba , a yi Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi. Ibrahim daa n kumi o kuuri.

eng: Abraham said to his servants, Stay here with the donkey. The boy and I will walk up there, worship, and then come back to you.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim yeli o gurunsi maa, Ya ni buŋaa, ya ba kpe, ka m ni biyaa ti gaa sunsuwaa niŋi, a wa ti puusi Naawunni, ka ta.

eng: God said to Abraham, Dont be upset about the boy and your servant. Do everything Sarah tells you to do because your descendants will be traced through Isaac.
local: Ama Naawunni yeli Ibrahim, Da ti bii maa ni e guruŋpoga Heega yela tiri i. Niŋi bunso zaa Saara ni yeli i la, a kpali Yisaaka niŋi n na, e ni dolli n nye e yaŋseesi ni digili i noori la.

eng: After he got up from embracing his deceased wife, he spoke with the Hittites:.
local: Kpiiŋu maa daa na doni, ka Ibrahim nyisigi n wa ti togsi n ti Hititi dima, a yeli.

eng: Abraham took the wood for the entirely burned offering and laid it on his son Isaac. He took the fire and the knife in his hand, and the two of them walked on together.
local: Ibrahim naa daari la ziili o bii Yisaaka, ka mi naa bugum ni suwa mari, ba bayi maa naa n wara.

eng: But I will make of your servants son a great nation too, because he is also your descendant.
local: M ni ti e guruŋpoga bii maa gba bidamta, ba gba ba niŋi baligu. O gba niŋini e biya.

eng: I am an immigrant and a temporary resident with you. Give me some property for a burial plot among you so that I can bury my deceased wife near me.
local: M niŋini saana yi ya puwa, ya koosi tiŋgbaŋŋu ti ma, o niŋi seela m di n piira m kpiinsi.

eng: Isaac said to his father Abraham, My father? Abraham said, Im here, my son. Isaac said, Here is the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for the entirely burned offering.
local: Yisaaka booli Ibrahim, M ba. O ba sagi, Mm. O boosi u, Ti mari bugum, a ni daari, ka peso ti n ya ku, a kusi u nyisi sariga maa ba ya.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, took some bread and a flask of water, and gave it to Hagar. He put the boy in her shoulder sling and sent her away. She left and wandered through the desert near Beer-sheba.
local: A chee bee boguuniŋi, Ibrahim naa bindira, a ni koom loŋkoŋŋu, a nyegi Heega, ka yeli o ni bii maa, ba wara. O yi n wa ti ba Beesiiba tiirigu puu chaŋina.

eng: The Hittites responded to Abraham.
local: Ba yeli Ibrahim.

eng: Abraham said, The lamb for the entirely burned offering? God will see to it, my son. The two of them walked on together.
local: Ibrahim yeli, M biya, Naawunni ni ti ti sariga pee maa. Niŋi n na, ba lee n gbaa ba soori wara.

eng: Finally the water in the flask ran out, and she put the boy down under one of the desert shrubs.
local: Koom maa n ti baasi, o naa bii maa digili tii maasim.

eng: Listen to us, sir. You are an eminent man of God among us. Bury your dead in one of our own select burial sites. None of us will keep our own burial plots from you to bury your dead.
local: Ti yidaana, wumi ti saani. E niŋini nivutimsigu yi ti puwa, nyaasi ti piiba ziisaa, seela n peri i e pi u. Ti yindaana zaa ki moŋŋi i, seela e di n pi e kpiima.

eng: They arrived at the place God had described to him. Abraham built an altar there and arranged the wood on it. He tied up his son Isaac and laid him on the altar on top of the wood.
local: Ba n ti paa seela Naawunni ni wusi Ibrahim la, o maali sariga daaŋu, ka kaasi daari la pa o niŋi, ka gbaag o bii la, a le u pa sariga daaŋu maa niŋi.

eng: She walked away from him about as far as a bow shot and sat down, telling herself, I cant bear to see the boy die. She sat at a distance, cried out in grief, and wept.
local: ka larimi u wa ti zi, ka yeli, M ki tee n nyaasira m biya, ka o kpiira. Ka zi niŋi kumina.

eng: Abraham rose, bowed to the local citizens the Hittites.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim dobi ba tiŋŋa jilimatiri.

eng: Then Abraham stretched out his hand and took the knife to kill his son as a sacrifice.
local: Ka voo suwa, o ku o bii maa.

eng: God heard the boys cries, and Gods messenger called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, Hagar! Whats wrong? Dont be afraid. God has heard the boys cries over there.
local: Naawunni wumi bii maa ni chaatira. Malaaka togsi n ti Heega a yi saazu, Heega, bo n niŋi i sufusaaŋu? Da zora dabeem, Naawunni wumi bii maa kumsi.

eng: and spoke with them: If you yourselves allow me to bury my dead near me, listen to me and ask Ephron, Zohars son.
local: ka yeli ba, Ka o niŋi ya kiperi maa, ya n ti m pi u seela n peri ma, lala wune ya suusi Zoha bii Efuron ti ma.

eng: But the LORD s messenger called out to Abraham from heaven, Abraham? Abraham? Abraham said, Im here.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni malaaka goo yi saazu tasi n booli u yeli, Ibrahim, Ibrahim. O sagi, Mani n ŋa.

eng: Get up, pick up the boy, and take him by the hand because I will make of him a great nation.
local: Nyisigi saa wa ti naa bii maa, ka yeli u o sabi. Ni dolli o niŋi ti u balitintaa.

eng: to give me his own cave in Machpelah at the edge of his field. Let him give it to me for the full price, to be witnessed by you, as my own burial property.
local: ni o koosi o zovoori n ba Makipela, ka niŋi o tiŋgbaŋŋu torigu la ti ma. Samiso zaa o n zaali ma, m ni yo u ya nufu niŋi, ka nisaaniŋi o loobi m niriba piibu ziya.

eng: The messenger said, Dont stretch out your hand against the young man, and dont do anything to him. I now know that you revere God and didnt hold back your son, your only son, from me.
local: Malaaka maa yeli, Da pa siisi biyaa, a ti u daaŋu. Pimparaŋa m pa baŋŋi e n zora Naawunni, a ni wumintiibu, a kpali e naag e biluŋko nyisi sariga.

eng: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well. She went over, filled the water flask, and gave the boy a drink.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni ti o nufu yoo, o nye bulugu. O naa loŋkoŋŋu la ŋoo koom paali u, a naa n ŋuusi bii maa.

eng: Now Ephron was a native Hittite. So Ephron the Hittite responded to Abraham publicly in order that the Hittites and everyone at his citys gate could hear:.
local: Efuron gba daa n zinni niriba maa puwa, ka togsira Ibrahim saani, ka niriba maa zaa wumina, a yi tiŋŋa maa voori noori, seela ba n ziira.

eng: Abraham looked up and saw a single ram caught by its horns in the dense underbrush. Abraham went over, took the ram, and offered it as an entirely burned offering instead of his son.
local: Ibrahim n wumi goo maa, o lalli o zu, ka nye pedaa siila n gari tipolli za. O gbaa pee maa nyisi sariga maa, ka che o bii la.

eng: God remained with the boy; he grew up, lived in the desert, and became an expert archer.
local: Naawunni daa bani bii maa saani, halli o ti karigi, a yi Paran tiirigu puwa, a baŋŋi toosim yela ga.

eng: No, sir. Listen, I will give you the field, and I will give you the cave in it. In front of my peoples witnesses, I will give it to you. Bury your dead.
local: Akai, m yidaana, wumi m saani, m naa tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori maa zaa ti i, m naa n ti i niribaa zaa nufu niŋi, e naa n piira e niriba.

eng: Abraham named that place the LORD sees. That is the reason people today say, On this mountain the LORD is seen.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim digili nisaaniŋi yuuri Duuma ni ti. Halli zinni, niriba na yetirini, ni Naawunni zoo zu, o ni ti.

eng: He lived in the Paran desert, and his mother found him an Egyptian wife.
local: O ma naa Yijipiti poga ti u.

eng: Abraham bowed before the local citizens.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim dobi niriba maa zaa tiŋŋa.

eng: The LORD s messenger called out to Abraham from heaven a second time.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma malaaka goo lee n booli Ibrahim yi saazu buyi daana yeli.

eng: and spoke to Ephron publicly in the presence of the local citizens: If only you would accept my offer. I will give you the price of the field. Take it from me so that I can bury my dead there.
local: ka yeli Efuron, a yi niriba maa zaa nufu niŋi, Ka e yi naa n ti ma, jaande wumi n ti ma, m ni ti i tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ligiri, deeg a, ka m piira m niriba nisaaniŋi.

eng: At that time Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, said to Abraham, God is with you in everything that you do.
local: A jemani maa, Abimeliki ni Fikoli n niŋi o soojasi kpeema we Ibrahim saani wa ti yeli u, Bunso zaa e n niŋina, Naawunni ba ni e saani.

eng: and said, I give my word as the LORD that because you did this and didnt hold back your son, your only son.
local: Duuma yeli, A kpali, e n bi zagsi e n di naa e biluŋko nyisi sariga ti ma la zu, m pe m maŋŋa yuuri niŋi maa.

eng: Ephron responded to Abraham.
local: Niŋi n na, Efuron yeli Ibrahim.

eng: So give me your word under God that you wont cheat me, my children, or my descendants. Just as I have treated you fairly, so you must treat me and the land in which you are an immigrant.
local: A zu, pe Naawunni maa, e ki yomsi m ni m biisi ni m yaŋsi zaa. Jemandamta zaa, m paasira e niŋi, a zu, pe n ti ma maa, e ni paasi m ni m paaligu so niŋi e n niŋi saanaa.

eng: I will bless you richly and I will give you countless descendants, as many as the stars in the sky and as the grains of sand on the seashore. They will conquer their enemies cities.
local: nafa n na ma ni nafa i, e yaŋsi ni zoo maa ŋmeribiisi n ba saazu, a maa teeku noori tambisigu. E yaŋsi ni zabi n nyaŋŋi ba dindima.

eng: Sir, what is four hundred shekels of silver between me and you for the land so that you can bury your dead.
local: M yidaana, wumi n saani, tiŋgbaŋŋu maa samini niŋini gbitisi kobsinaasi. Ŋama bi niŋi sen yi ti sunsuuniŋi. A zu, piira e niriba nisaaniŋi.

eng: Abraham said, I give you my word.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, M digili i noori lala.

eng: All the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants, because you obeyed me.
local: A kpali e ni wumi n ti ma la zu, durinye puu balisi zaa ni nye nafa, a yi e yaŋsi puwa.

eng: Abraham accepted Ephrons offer and weighed out for Ephron the silver he requested publicly before the Hittites: four hundred shekels of silver at the current rate of exchange.
local: Ibrahim n wumi n ŋa, o zamsi gbitisi kobsinaasi ti Efuron, seema o n yeli niriba maa nufu niŋi la.

eng: Then Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well that Abimelechs servants had seized.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim yeli Abimeliki seema o gurunsi n fa o bulugu.

eng: After Abraham returned to the young men, they got up and went to Beer-sheba where Abraham lived.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim loobi n we o gurunsi saani, ba zaa gaa Beesiiba, Ibrahim zi nisaaniŋi.

eng: So the field of Ephron in Machpelah near Mamre—the field and the cave in it, and all the trees within the fields boundaries—was officially transferred.
local: A zu, Ibrahim da Efuron tiŋgbanso n ba Makipela tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi la, a leeli Mamiri, tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori ni tiisi zaa n ba o sunsuuniŋi ni gbarima niŋi zaa.

eng: Abimelech said, I dont know who has done this, and you didnt tell me. I didnt even hear about it until today.
local: Abimeliki yeli u, M bi baŋŋi o yela, e bi yeli ma, m na si n wumina u ni n na la.

eng: After these events, Abraham was told: Milcah has now also given birth to sons for your brother Nahor.
local: Jemanso n daa ti paa, Ibrahim wumi ni o sunzo Naho poga Milika dogini biisi banii ti u.

eng: to Abraham as his property in the presence of the Hittites and of everyone at his citys gate.
local: A zaa n daa loobi Ibrahim toori, a yi Hititi dima seesi zaa n daa n laŋsi tiŋŋa maa voori noori la nufu niŋi.

eng: Abraham took flocks and cattle, gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them drew up a treaty.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim naa peeri ni naagi ti Abimeliki, ba bayi maa zaa niŋi naŋgbanluŋko.

eng: They are Uz his oldest son, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram.
local: Wuz n daa niŋi wurikoŋ, a naa o sunzo Buz, Kemuwel so n daa dogi Aram.

eng: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, that is, Hebron, in the land of Canaan.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa n na, Ibrahim pi o poga Saara zovoori maa n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, a leeli Mamiri Keenan paaligu.

eng: Abraham set aside, by themselves, seven female lambs from the flock.
local: Ibrahim barigi pesaasi ayapoin yi o peeri niŋi.

eng: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
local: Keesi ni Hazo ni Pilidasi ni Jidalap a ni Betuwel.

eng: The field and the cave in it were officially transferred from the Hittites to Abraham as his burial property.
local: A zu, tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori la zaa daa n loobini Ibrahim kapete, a dolli Hititi dima niŋi, o piira o niriba.

eng: So Abimelech said to Abraham, What are these seven lambs youve set apart.
local: Abimeliki boosi u, Bo n na e niŋi lala.

eng: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These are the eight Milcah bore for Nahor, Abrahams brother.
local: Betuwel n niŋi Rabeeka ba. Milika n dogi biduusi banii ŋa ti Ibrahim sunzo Naho.

eng: Abraham said, These seven lambs that you take from me will attest that I dug this well.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, Dee pesaasi ayapoin ŋa. Ka e niŋi lala, e sagini maa mani n gbi bulugu a.

eng: His secondary wifes name was Reumah, and she gave birth to Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
local: Naho pogibila so ba daa n boona Reema gba daa n dogi Teba ni Gaham ni Tahasi a ni Maaka.

eng: Therefore, the name of that place is Beer-sheba because there they gave each other their word.
local: Lala zu, nisaaniŋi yuuri n niŋi Beesiiba , a kpali, niŋi n na ba bayi maa zaa digili naŋgbana.

eng: After they drew up a treaty at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, returned to the land of the Philistines.
local: Ba n sagi n ti taaba yi Beesiiba maa, Abimeliki ni Fikoli pori n gaari Filisiti dima paaligu.

eng: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and he worshipped there in the name of the LORD , El Olam.
local: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim buri tiya, a yi Beesiiba, ka puusi Duuma, Naawunni so n ba niŋi wara saŋa ni saŋa zaa la.

eng: Abraham lived as an immigrant in the Philistines land for a long time.
local: Jemanwogili n na Ibrahim daa zi Filisiti dima paaligu.

eng: Abraham married another wife, named Keturah.
local: Ibrahim daa n lee n naani poga, o yuuri n daa n niŋi Katuura.

eng: The children she bore him were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
local: O dogi Zimaran, Jokasina, Medan, Midiyan Isibaka a ni Suwa.

eng: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan. Dedans sons were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
local: Jokasina dogi Seba ni Dedan. Dedan biduusi n daa n niŋi Asurum dima, Letusi dima, a ni Lumiti dima.

eng: When a famine gripped the land, a different one from the first famine that occurred in Abrahams time, Isaac set out toward Gerar and toward King Abimelech of the Philistines.
local: Jemanso, kom daa n lini paaligu maa, pa kom so n daa n li Ibrahim jemani la, ŋala daa n kurini. Kom maa jemani n na Yisaaka nyisigi n we Filisiti naaba Abimeliki saani yi Gera.

eng: Midians sons were Ephah, Epher, Enoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were Keturahs sons.
local: Midiyan biduusi n daa n niŋi Efa, Efe Hanoki, Abida a ni Elidaa, ba ŋa zaa Katuura biisi ni o yaŋsi n daa n na la.

eng: The LORD appeared to him and said, Dont go down to Egypt but settle temporarily in the land that I will show you.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma nyisi o maŋŋa wusi Yisaaka, ka yeli u n ŋa, Da pa gaa Yijipiti paaligu, ba tiŋgbanso ni yetira i ŋa niŋi.

eng: Abraham gave everything he owned to Isaac.
local: Ibrahim naag o bunso zaa nyem ti Yisaaka.

eng: Stay in this land as an immigrant, and I will be with you and bless you because I will give all of these lands to you and your descendants. I will keep my word, which I gave to your father Abraham.
local: Ba kpe paaligu daba ayi, m ni ba e saani, ka nafa i. M ya n pa ni m naŋgbantiibu so ni ti e ba Ibrahim la, m ni naa tiŋgbaŋaa zaa ti e ni e yaŋsi.

eng: To the sons of Abrahams secondary wives, Abraham gave gifts and, while he was still living, sent them away from his son Isaac to land in the east.
local: Ama, jemanso o n daa n na ba o nyevuri niŋi, o daa n pini o poga seesi biduusi piini. Ka nyisi n ŋa biduusi a gaari tiŋŋa yindaana n ba wuluŋpusigu, ka che o bii Yisaaka.

eng: I will give you as many descendants as the stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of these lands. All of the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants.
local: M ni ti e yaŋsi wasi, a zoo maa ŋmeribiisi, m ni kpabira ba yela, ka dolli ba niŋi nafa durinye balisi zaa.

eng: Abraham lived to the age of .
local: Ŋama zaa nyaaŋa, Ibrahim daa kurigiya ga, o yuma koowa ni pisiyapoin ni anu, ka vuusi o baasigu vuusim, ka kpi, a gaari o yaadima saani.

eng: I will do this because Abraham obeyed me and kept my orders, my commandments, my statutes, and my instructions.
local: M ya n niŋini n ŋa, a kpali e ba Ibrahim ni wumi n ti ma, a pa m zaaligusi zaa zu la.

eng: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave in Machpelah, which is in the field of Zohars son Ephron the Hittite, near Mamre.
local: O biisi, Yisaaka ni Isimeel, pi u yi Makipela zovoori niŋi, a leeli Mamiri. Tiŋgbanso Ibrahim n daa n da, Zoha bii Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira la saani, niŋi n na ba pi o ni o poga Saara.

eng: So Isaac lived in Gerar.
local: A zu, Yisaaka zi Gera.

eng: After Abrahams death, God blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac lived in Beer-lahai-roi.
local: Ibrahim kum nyaaŋa, Naawunni nafa o biduu Yisaaka, ka o zi n leeli buluguso ba n boona nyevuri daana bulugu, so n nyaara ma.

eng: When the men who lived there asked about his wife, he said, Shes my sister, because he was afraid to say, my wife, thinking, The men who live there will kill me for Rebekah because shes very beautiful.
local: Rabeeka daa n niŋini pogiso n veela ga, nisaaniŋi dabsi daa n boosira u o yela, o yeli ni o sunzopoga n na. O daa n zorini dabeem, o n di n yeli ba ni o poga n na, ba n di n ku, ka naa poga maa.

eng: These are the descendants of Ishmael, Abrahams son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarahs servant, bore for Abraham.
local: Isimeel, so Heega, Saara Yijipiti guruŋbipoga n dogi ti Ibrahim, biisi yela n ŋa.

eng: After Isaac had lived there for some time, the Philistines King Abimelech looked out his window and saw Isaac laughing together with his wife Rebekah.
local: Yisaaka zi nisaaniŋi yuu. Dindaso Abimeliki Filisiti naaba nyaasi o tokuro niŋi, ka nye Yisaaka ni gbaa o poga Rabeeka kpabirisimtiri.

eng: When Isaac had grown old and his eyesight was failing, he summoned his older son Esau and said to him, My son? And Esau said, Im here.
local: Yisaaka n daa n ti kurigi, ka bi lee n nyaara nene, dindaso, o booli o bikpeema daana Isawu, M biya. O sagi Mani n ŋa.

eng: These are the names of Ishmaels sons, by their names and according to their birth order: Nebaioth, Ishmaels oldest son; Kedar; Adbeel; Mibsam;.
local: Isimeel biduusi yuya, a ni seema ba n pa taaba zu. Naboti, Keda, Adibeel, Mibisam.

eng: So Isaac summoned Jacob, blessed him, and gave him these orders: Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Yisaaka booli Yakubu ti nafa u, ka yeli u, Da pa ti naa Keenan paaligu poga.

eng: So Abimelech summoned Isaac and said, Shes your wife, isnt she? How could you say, Shes my sister? Isaac responded, Because I thought that I might be killed because of her.
local: Naaba Abimeliki booli Yisaaka, a boosi u, E poga n niŋi pogaa? Ka wula zu n na, e yeli e sunzopoga n niŋi u? Yisaaka yeli u, M feni maa ba ni ku ma kpali o zu.

eng: He said, Im old and dont know when I will die.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka yeli u, Pimparaŋa m kurigiya, ka bi baŋŋi jemanso m di n kpi.

eng: Mishma; Dumah; Massa;.
local: Misihama, Duma, Masa.

eng: Get up and go to Paddan-aram, to the household of Bethuel, your mothers father, and once there, marry one of the daughters of Laban, your mothers brother.
local: Ama, gaa Mesopoteemiya paaligu, a wa ti kpe e yaadaba Betuwel laanta. Naa poga yi nisaaniŋi, a yi e ma sunzodaba Leban bipunsi puwa.

eng: Abimelech said, What are you trying to do to us? Before long, one of the people would have slept with your wife; and you would have made us guilty.
local: Bo n na e niŋi ti seema? Ka dabsaa yinni zaa di mari e pogaa do, ti di niŋi yabetintaa.

eng: So now, take your hunting gear, your bow and quiver of arrows, go out to the field, and hunt game for me.
local: Nyisigi n naa e logu ni o piima gaa moo niŋi, a wa ti ku valiga ti ti ma.

eng: Hadad; Tema; Jetur; Naphish; and Kedemah.
local: Hadadi, Tema, Jetu, Nafisi a ni Kedema.

eng: God Almighty will bless you, make you fertile, and give you many descendants so that you will become a large group of peoples.
local: Naawunni, kpeeŋu zaa daana, o nafa e kulisigu, a ti ya bidamta. Lala jemani, e ni ti loobi balidamta ba.

eng: As the days went by and Abraham became older, the LORD blessed Abraham in every way.
local: Ibrahim daa n kurigi n kpe kpebilim niŋi, Naawunni mi daa n nafa u soori zaa niŋi.

eng: Abimelech gave orders to all of the people, Anyone who touches this man or his wife will be put to death.
local: A zu, naaba Abimeliki kpaasi so zaa tuba, ni so zaa ni siisi dabaa ni o pogaa, ni koŋŋi o nyevuri.

eng: Make me the delicious food that I love and bring it to me so I can eat. Then I can bless you before I die.
local: Ka e mari n ŋa valigaa ta, e naag u dugi bindira meesa ti ti ma, m di, ka nafa i poin, ka kpi.

eng: These are Ishmaels sons. These are their names by their villages and their settlements: twelve tribal leaders according to their tribes.
local: Barima n daa niŋi pii ni ayi la, ka ba naa ba yuya digili ba tiŋsi ni seelasi ba n zi.

eng: He will give you and your descendants Abrahams blessing so that you will own the land in which you are now immigrants, the land God gave to Abraham.
local: Wune n ni nafa e ni e yaŋsi maa seema o n daa n nafa Ibrahim la, a ti tiŋgbaŋaa loobi e toori, so niŋi e ni be ŋa, wune Naawunni n daa naa n ti Ibrahim la.

eng: Abraham said to the oldest servant of his household, who was in charge of everything he owned, Put your hand under my thigh.
local: O yeli o gurunsi zaa kpeema, so n nyaasira o yiri bunso zaa, ni o naa o nuu niŋi o kpiiligu gbinniŋi.

eng: Isaac planted grain in that land and reaped one hundred shearim that year because the LORD had blessed him.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka daa n ko, a buri bini tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, a niŋi ga, bumburubiluŋko zaa daa niŋini bindira koowa, a kpali Duuma n daa n nafa u zu.

eng: Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. When Esau went out to the field to hunt game to bring back.
local: Jemanso Yisaaka ni togsira o bii Isawu saani maa, ka o ma Rabeeka gba wumina a zaa nyem. Niŋi n na, Isawu gaa moo puwa, o wa ti kpabi valiga maa ta.

eng: Ishmael lived to the age of . He took his last breath and died, and was placed with his ancestors.
local: Isimeel daa dini yuma koowa ni pisita ni ayapoin poin ka kpi.

eng: So Isaac sent Jacob off, and he traveled to Paddan-aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean and brother of Rebekah, Jacob and Esaus mother.
local: A zu, Yisaaka ti Yakubu gaari Mesopoteemiya, Leban saani. Leban ba n niŋi Betuwel, so n niŋi Aram nira, ka niŋi Yakubu ni Isawu ma Rabeeka sunzodaba.

eng: By the LORD , God of heaven and earth, give me your word that you wont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women among whom I live.
local: M kpabirini maa, e pe, a yi Naawunni so n niŋi saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa Duuma la yuuri niŋi, maa e ki naa Keenan paaligu poga ti m bii Yisaaka.

eng: Isaac grew richer and richer until he was extremely wealthy.
local: O daa n loobini bindaana, ka o bini na si n paasira.

eng: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, I just heard your father saying to your brother Esau.
local: Niŋi Rabeeka yeli o bii Yakubu, Nyaasi, m wumi e ba n yetira e beeri Isawu.

eng: He established camps from Havilah to Shur, which is near Egypt on the road to Assyria. He died among all of his brothers.
local: Isimeel baligu maa daa zinni Havila ni Suu sunsuuniŋi, Yijipiti wuluŋpusigu, Asiriya soori. Ba ni ba niriba daa n bi lagimi n zi.

eng: Esau understood that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan-aram to marry a woman from there. He recognized that, when Isaac blessed Jacob, he had ordered him, Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Isawu wumi Yisaaka ni nafa Yakubu, ka ti o gaa Mesopoteemiya, o wa ti naa poga nisaaniŋi, ka lee n wumi maa o nafa u, ka yeli u, Da pa ti naa Keenan paaligu poga.

eng: When Rachel realized that she could bear Jacob no children, Rachel became jealous of her sister and said to Jacob, Give me children! If you dont, I may as well be dead.
local: Rachel nye maa o bi dogi bii zaa ti Yakubu, o beeri yela gbaag u pubeerim. A zu, o yeli Yakubu, Ti ma biisi, ka pa lala, ni kpi.

eng: Go to my land and my family and find a wife for my son Isaac there.
local: Ama, e gaa m yiri paaligu, m niriba gbagba puwa, a wa ti naa poga ti ti m bii Yisaaka.

eng: He had livestock, both flocks and cattle, and many servants. As a result, the Philistines envied him.
local: O daa marini naagi, peeri, a ni gurunsi. Binaa zu, o yela daa n marini Filisiti dima pubeerim.

eng: Jacob got to his feet and set out for the land of the easterners.
local: Yakubu lee n gbaa soori ti paa niseesi n ba wuluŋpusigu tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

eng: Jacob heard that Labans sons were saying, Jacob took everything our father owned and from it he produced all of this wealth.
local: Dindaso n na Yakubu wumi maa Leban biisi togsirini n ŋa, Yakubu naani ti ba bini zaa loobi o toori, a loobi daama daana.

eng: Bring me some game and make me some delicious food so I can eat, and I will bless you in the LORD s presence before I die.
local: ni o wa ti ku valiga ti dugi bindira meesa ti o di, ka nafa u poin, ka kpi.

eng: These are the descendants of Isaac, Abrahams son. Abraham became the father of Isaac.
local: Seema Ibrahim bii Yisaaka ni o baligu n piili n ŋa.

eng: and that Jacob had listened to his father and mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
local: Yakubu wumi o ba ni o ma noori ka gaa Mesopoteemiya.

eng: Jacob was angry at Rachel and said, Do you think Im God? God alone has kept you from giving birth.
local: Yakubu suuri nyisigi, o boosi u, Mani n niŋi Naawunni, a gbaag e dogim mari.

eng: The servant said to him, What if the woman doesnt agree to come back with me to this land? Shouldnt I take your son back to the land you left.
local: Niŋi n na, guruŋu maa boosi u, Ka poga maa bi kpabira o pa n zu ta kpe, ni tee n mari e bii maa pori n gaa niŋi.

eng: The Philistines closed up and filled with dirt all of the wells that his fathers servants had dug during his father Abrahams lifetime.
local: A zu, ba gbi tana nyetira buluseesi o ba Ibrahim n daa n ti o gurunsi gbi la zaa.

eng: He saw a well in the field in front of him, near which three flocks of sheep were lying down. That well was their source for water because the flocks drank from that well. A huge stone covered the wells opening.
local: Niŋi n na, o nye bulugu, ka peeri tulum buta zu do o see, a kpali, bulugu maa n na ba ŋoo n niŋini peeri maa. Kugiyeliŋu n daa pa bulugu maa noori.

eng: And Jacob saw that Laban no longer liked him as much as he used to.
local: Ka Yakubu mi nye maa Leban yaniŋisa bi lee n ba seema o n daa n be la.

eng: Now, my son, listen to me, to what Im telling you to do.
local: A zu, m biya, yoo tuba nene wumi, ka niŋi seema ni ya n yeli i ŋa.

eng: Isaac was years old when he married Rebekah the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean and the sister of Laban the Aramean, from Paddan-aram.
local: Yisaaka daa niŋini yuma pisinaasi, ka naa Rabeeka, Betuwel biya, ka niŋi Leban sunzopoga. Betuwel daa n niŋini Aram nira, ka zi Mesopoteemiya paaligu.

eng: Esau realized that his father Isaac considered Canaanite women unacceptable.
local: Isawu baŋŋi maa Keenan paaligu pogiba maa yela bi peri o ba Yisaaka.

eng: She said, Heres my servant Bilhah. Sleep with her, and she will give birth for me. Because of her, I will also have children.
local: Rachel yeli u, M bipoga Biliha n na la, mari u do, o ni dogi biisi ti ma. O niŋi n na mani dolli n loobi biisi ma.

eng: Abraham said to him, Be sure you dont take my son back there.
local: Ibrahim yeli u, Da pa ti ti m bii maa nobiri no nisaaniŋi.

eng: Abimelech said to Isaac, Move away from us because you have become too powerful among us.
local: Dindaso, naaba Abimeliki yeli Yisaaka, Yi ti puwa, a kpali bo, e kpeeŋu nyaŋŋi ti.

eng: When all of the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the wells opening, water the sheep, and return the stone to its place at the wells opening.
local: Peeri maa zaa yi ti laŋsi, pekpagiriba maa yi bilimini kugiri la, ka ŋoo koom niŋi peeri maa. Ba yi lee n porini kugiri maa pa bulugu maa noori.

eng: Then the LORD said to Jacob, Go back to the land of your ancestors and to your relatives, and I will be with you.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli Yakubu n ŋa. Pori n gaa e niriba saani, e yaadima tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, ka m ni ba e saani.

eng: Go to the flock and get me two healthy young goats so I can prepare them as the delicious food your father loves.
local: Yi dinyaaŋa we buŋkobiri la puwa, a wa ti gbaa bupola ayi ta, m dugi bindira meesa ti e ba.

eng: Isaac prayed to the LORD for his wife, since she was unable to have children. The LORD was moved by his prayer, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.
local: A kpali Rabeeka n daa n na bi dogi zu, Yisaaka suusi Naawunni. Duuma wumi o suusigu, Rabeeka mari puwa.

eng: So he went to Ishmael and married Mahalath daughter of Abrahams son Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his other wives.
local: A zu, o gaa Ibrahim bii Isimeel saani wa ti naa o bipuŋŋu Mahalaf, Nebayof sunzopoga, a paasi o pogiseesi o mari la niŋi.

eng: So Rachel gave her servant Bilhah to Jacob as his wife, and he slept with her.
local: A zu, o naag o bipoga Biliha ti u, a maa o poga. Yakubu mari u do.

eng: The LORD , God of heaven—who took me from my fathers household and from my familys land, who spoke with me and who gave me his word, saying, I will give this land to your descendants—he will send his messenger in front of you, and you will find a wife for my son there.
local: M Duuma Naawunni n ba alizaana, so n nyisi ma yi m ba yiri, a yi ti paaligu niŋi, ka togsi m saani, a digili noori ti ma maa, o ni naa kpe tiŋgbaŋaa ti m yaŋsi. O ni ti malaaka pa i yiya, e wa ti naa poga yi nisaaniŋi, a ti ti m biya.

eng: So Isaac moved away from there, camped in the valley of Gerar, and lived there.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka nyisigi ba puwa, a gaa Gera nantaŋŋu puwa, a wa ti sabi o gbana suga yi niŋi, a zi nisaaniŋi.

eng: Jacob said to them, Where are you from, my brothers? They said, Were from Haran.
local: Yakubu boosi pekpagiriba maa, M zodima, ya n na ya yi? Ba yeli u, Ti yini Haran.

eng: So Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah and summoned them into the field where his flock was.
local: Yakubu ni wumi n ŋa, o tolimi n yeli Rachel ni Liya, ni ba ta o saani, seela o ni buŋkobiri n be la.

eng: You can bring it to your father, he will eat, and then he will bless you before he dies.
local: e naa bindira maa wa ti ti e ba o di, ka nafa i poin, ka kpi.

eng: But the boys pushed against each other inside of her, and she said, If this is what its like, why did it happen to me? So she went to ask the LORD .
local: Jaasi puu n na Rabeeka daa n mari, biisaa mugisira taaba ba ma puu niŋi. O boosi Naawunni, Wula mini n na ŋa.

eng: Jacob left Beer-sheba and set out for Haran.
local: Yakubu yi Beesiiba wara Haran tiŋŋa.

eng: Bilhah became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: O mari puwa, a dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

eng: If the woman wont agree to come back with you, you will be free from this obligation to me. Only dont take my son back there.
local: Ka poga maa bi kpabira o pa e zu ta, naŋgbandigiligu maa bi lee n pa i. Ama, da pa mari m bii pori n gaa nisaaniŋi.

eng: Isaac dug out again the wells that were dug during the lifetime of his father Abraham. The Philistines had closed them up after Abrahams death. Isaac gave them the same names his father had given them.
local: O n gaa n niŋi maa, o lee maali n yaa buluseesi ba n daa n gbi Ibrahim jemani la, ka Filisiti dima daa nyeri a, Ibrahim kum nyaaŋa la, Yisaaka lee n naa yuseesi o ba n daa n digili a la gbagba n digili a.

eng: Then he said to them, Do you know Laban, Nahors grandson? They said, We know him.
local: O lee n boosi ba, Ya baŋŋi Naho yaŋŋu Leban? Ba sagi, Mm, ti baŋŋi u.

eng: He said to them, I am aware that your father no longer likes me as much as he used to. But my fathers God has been with me.
local: O yeli ba, M nyeya maa, m ni ya ba beebu bi lee n be seema o n daa n be la, ama m ba Naawunni la mi bani m saani.

eng: Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, My brother Esau is a hairy man, but I have smooth skin.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli o ma Rabeeka, Ka m beeri niŋa zaa ni mari koba, ka m mi niŋa n ka koba ŋa.

eng: And the LORD said to her, Two nations are in your womb; two different peoples will emerge from your body. One people will be stronger than the other; the older will serve the younger.
local: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli u, Balisi bayi n ba e puu niŋi, ba ni ti zabira taaba ŋuuri. Yinni ni mari kpeeŋu gaari o to, ka kpeema daana ni tumina bibila daana la.

eng: He reached a certain place and spent the night there. When the sun had set, he took one of the stones at that place and put it near his head. Then he lay down there.
local: O n paa seela, o do niŋi bee o chee, a kpali wuluŋu liya. Ka naa kugiri loobi zukogu kogi n do.

eng: Rachel said, God has judged in my favor, heard my voice, and given me a son. So she named him Dan.
local: Rachel yeli, Naawunni zabi n pa m zu. O wumi m suusigu, ka ti ma biduwa. Ka digili o yuuri Dan.

eng: So the servant put his hand under his master Abrahams thigh and gave him his word about this mission.
local: A zu, guruŋu maa naa o nuu niŋi Ibrahim kpiiligu gbinniŋi, ka pe, seema o n yeli u maa.

eng: Isaacs servants dug wells in the valley and found a well there with fresh water.
local: Dindaso, Yisaaka gurunsi gbi bulugu yi nantaŋŋu maa puwa, ka nye koom.

eng: He said to them, Is he well? They said, Hes fine. In fact, this is his daughter Rachel now, coming with the flock.
local: Yakubu boosi ba, O mari alaanfiya? Ba yeli u, O mari alaanfiya. Nyaasi, o bipuŋŋu Rachel ni o peeri n chena la.

eng: You know that Ive worked for your father as hard as I could.
local: Ya zaa mi nye seema ni peligi puu tumi ya ba.

eng: What if my father touches me and thinks Im making fun of him? I will be cursed instead of blessed.
local: Ka m ba siisi ma, o ni yeli mani n yomsira u, ka ki nafa ma, ama o ni si ma noori.

eng: When she reached the end of her pregnancy, she discovered that she had twins.
local: Puu maa dogirisim n ti paa, Rabeeka dogi jaasi, dabsi.

eng: He dreamed and saw a raised staircase, its foundation on earth and its top touching the sky, and Gods messengers were ascending and descending on it.
local: O zaasi zaasim, a nye darigu n za tiŋŋa wa ti tisi sagbana, ka Naawunni malaakasi duura o niŋi, ka sigira.

eng: Rachels servant Bilhah became pregnant again and gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Biliha lee n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa paasi n ti Yakubu.

eng: The servant took ten of his masters camels and all of his masters best provisions, set out, and traveled to Nahors city in Aram-naharaim.
local: Niŋi n na, guruŋu maa naa o yidaana nyogimasi piiya, ka naa piinisuma baligu zaa paasi n gaari Mesopoteemiya paaligu, Naho yiri.

eng: Isaacs shepherds argued with Gerars shepherds, each claiming, This is our water. So Isaac named the well Esek because they quarreled with him.
local: A daa n bi yuu, ka Gera buŋkokpagiriba ni Yisaaka buŋkokpagiriba daa zabira ni, bulugu maa niŋini ba toori. A zu, Yisaaka digili lala bulugu maa yuuri Zabiri.

eng: He said to them, Its now only the middle of the day. Its not time yet to gather the animals. Water the flock, and then go, put them out to pasture.
local: O yeli ba, Ya nyaasi, wuluŋu na zooya ga, jemani na bi paa maa peeri maa zaa di n laŋsi. Ya ŋoo koom niŋi a, ka mari a pori n gaa diibu ziya.

eng: But your father cheated me and changed my payment ten times. Yet God didnt let him harm me.
local: Ama, ya ba puni ma, o cherigi m sanyoori bupii zu, ama, Naawunni mi bi ti o nye daama niŋi ma daaŋu.

eng: His mother said to him, Your curse will be on me, my son. Just listen to me: go and get them for me.
local: O ma yeli u, Cheli naŋgbansiibu maa o niŋi m toori, nyiwune e niŋi seema ni yeli i maa de, wa ti gbaag a ti ti ma.

eng: The first came out red all over, clothed with hair, and she named him Esau.
local: Yipaara maa daa n niŋini nivuzee, ka o niŋa zaa mari koba, a maa o ni pili gbaŋŋu tagite. Lala zu, ba digili o yuuri Isawu.

eng: Suddenly the LORD was standing on it and saying, I am the LORD , the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac. I will give you and your descendants the land on which you are lying.
local: Ka Duuma za n begi darigu maa yi saazu, ka yeli, Mani n niŋi Duuma la, Ibrahim Naawunni, a ni Yisaaka Naawunni. Tiŋgbaŋŋu so niŋi e ni do ŋa, m ni naag u ti e ni e yaŋsi.

eng: Rachel said, Ive competed fiercely with my sister, and now Ive won. So she named him Naphtali.
local: Rachel yeli, M zabi m beeri kpeeŋtiri, ka mi nyaŋŋi. A zu, o digili bii maa yuuri Nafitali.

eng: He had the camels kneel down outside the city at the well in the evening, when women come out to draw water.
local: O n wa ti paa tiŋŋa maa, o ti nyogimasi maa gba tiŋŋa, bulugu see, zaanoori jemani, jemanso pogiba ni wara kulisi.

eng: They dug another well and argued about it too, so he named it Sitnah.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka gurunsi maa lee n gbi bulugu, ba ni Gera buŋkokpagiriba maa lee n mari o naŋgbanchee, Yisaaka digili o yuuri Diŋŋu.

eng: They said to him, We cant until all the herds are gathered, and then we roll the stone away from the wells opening and water the flock.
local: Ba yeli u, Ti ki tee, ama peeri maa zaa laŋsini poin, ka ti tee n bilimi kugiri maa yi bulugu maa noori, ka ŋoo koom niŋi a.

eng: If he said, The speckled ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to speckled young. And if he said, The striped ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to striped young.
local: Jemani zaa ka Leban daa n si n ti yeli ni buŋkoochericherisi la n niŋi e sanyoobu, a zaa si n ya n dogi, a dogirini binchericherisi. O n lee n yeli ni pimparaŋa, buŋkobseesi niŋa n too booma n niŋi e sanyoori, buŋkobiri maa zaa yi lee n dogirini lala biisi.

eng: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother made the delicious food that his father loved.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu wa ti gbaa buusi maa ti ti o ma, o dugi bindira meesa, seema gbagba Yisaaka ni kpabira la.

eng: Immediately afterward, his brother came out gripping Esaus heel, and she named him Jacob. Isaac was years old when they were born.
local: O n yiira la, o sunzo gbaa o nosiŋkpaari mari. Lala zu ba digili o yuuri Yakubu. Jemanso Rabeeka ni dogi biisaa, ka Yisaaka niŋini yuma pisiyoobu.

eng: Your descendants will become like the dust of the earth; you will spread out to the west, east, north, and south. Every family of earth will be blessed because of you and your descendants.
local: E yaŋsi ni sagi maa tambisigu yi durinye ŋa puwa, ya ni saligi n gaa wulunliya, wuluŋpusigu, kiŋkagili sagbinni, a ni kiŋkagili sazu. M ni nafa balisi zaa yi durinye ŋa puwa, a dolli e ni e yaŋsi niŋi.

eng: When Leah realized that she had stopped bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as his wife.
local: Liya n ti nye maa o bi lee dogira, o naa o bipoga Zilipa ti Yakubu maa o poga.

eng: He said, LORD , God of my master Abraham, make something good happen for me today and be loyal to my master Abraham.
local: Niŋi n na, o suusi Naawunni, M Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, ti ma zusuŋŋu zinaa, a wusi m yidaana Ibrahim e nivumantiri.

eng: He left there and dug another well, but they didnt argue about it, so he named it Rehoboth and said, Now the LORD has made an open space for us and has made us fertile in the land.
local: A kpali ba naŋgbanchee maa zu, Yisaaka nyisigi nisaaniŋi, a wa ti zi zipaali, ka lee n gbi bulupaali. Nisaaniŋi wune, naŋgbanchee daa n lee n ka niŋi, a zu, o digili niŋi yuuri niŋgbirimaasim.

eng: While he was still talking to them, Rachel came with her fathers flock since she was its shepherd.
local: Jemanso Yakubu na n togsira ba saani maa, ka Rachel mari o ba peeri ta, wune n daa kpagiri a.

eng: God took away your fathers livestock and gave them to me.
local: A zu, Naawunni deeni ya ba buŋkobiri maa ti ma.

eng: Rebekah took her older son Esaus favorite clothes that were in the house with her, and she put them on her younger son Jacob.
local: Ka naa o bii Isawu, so n niŋi kpeema la, bimpilisuma n ba dii pili o sunzo Yakubu.

eng: When the young men grew up, Esau became an outdoorsman who knew how to hunt, and Jacob became a quiet man who stayed at home.
local: Biisaa n ti karigi, Isawu loobi toosa, a baŋŋi toosilim ga, ka kpabira moo puu chaŋinisim. Ka Yakubu ba boin, ka kpabira yiri ga.

eng: I am with you now, I will protect you everywhere you go, and I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have done everything that I have promised you.
local: Teesira, m ni ba e saani, a kogira i seela zaa e n wara, m ni mari i pori n ta tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi. M ki cheli i, ama bunso zaa ni digili noori yeli, m ni niŋi maa niŋini.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: Zilipa dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

eng: I will stand here by the spring while the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water.
local: Nyaasi, m zani begi bulugaa, tiŋaa suŋgurisi ni ta n ti ŋoo koom.

eng: Then he went up from Gerar to Beer-sheba.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, o lee n nyisigi n gaa Beesiiba.

eng: When Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his uncle, and the flock of Laban, Jacob came up, rolled the stone from the wells opening, and watered the flock of his uncle Laban.
local: Yakubu n nye Rachel ni o nyesiba Leban peeri, o gaa bulugu maa see bilimi kugiri la, ka ŋoo koom niŋi o nyesiba peeri maa.

eng: When the flocks were mating, I looked up and saw in a dream that the male goats that mounted the flock were striped, speckled, and spotted.
local: Buŋkobiri maa duubu jemani, m daa zaasini zaasim, a nye maa, gbeesi dima la daa n bani chericheri, ka seesi gba mari booma.

eng: On his arms and smooth neck she put the hide of young goats.
local: Ka lee n naa buusi la gbana muuri Yakubu nuusi ni o nuŋgori, seelasi ni ka koba la.

eng: Isaac loved Esau because he enjoyed eating game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
local: Yisaaka kpabira Isawu yela, a kpali o n kuura valiga o ŋobira la zu, ka Rabeeka mi kpabira Yakubu yela.

eng: When Jacob woke from his sleep, he thought to himself, The LORD is definitely in this place, but I didnt know it.
local: Jemanso Yakubu ni nyisigi gbeem maa niŋi, o yeli, Yamonsitiri, Duuma ba kpe, ka ma bi baŋŋi, o lala.

eng: and Leah said, What good luck! So she named him Gad.
local: Liya yeli, M mari zusuŋŋu. Ka digili o yuuri Gadi.

eng: When I say to a young woman, Hand me your water jar so I can drink, and she says to me, Drink, and I will give your camels water too, may she be the one youve selected for your servant Isaac. In this way I will know that youve been loyal to my master.
local: Ti ba so zaa ni ya n yeli maa o ti ma koom n ŋu, o sagi n ti ma, ka o lee n yeli ni o ni ŋoo koom ti m nyogimasi gba, cheli lala poga maa o niŋi so e n nyisi n ti e guruŋu Yisaaka. Ka lala yi niŋi, ni baŋŋi maa nyini n niŋi m yidaana Ibrahim yasuŋŋu yamonsitiri.

eng: The LORD appeared to him that night and said, I am the God of your father Abraham. Dont be afraid because I am with you. I will bless you, and I will give you many children for my servant Abrahams sake.
local: Dindaari maa yiiŋu, Duuma nyisi o maŋŋa wusi u, ka yeli u, Mani n niŋi e ba Ibrahim Naawunni la, a zu, da zora dabeem, m ni ba e saani, ka nafa i, ka ti e yaŋsi zoo, a kpali m guruŋu Ibrahim zu.

eng: Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu mugisi Rachel, ka kumi, a ni sufupeelimtiri.

eng: In the dream, Gods messenger said to me, Jacob! and I said, Im here.
local: Zaasim maa puwa, Naawunni malaaka booli yeli ma, Yakubu. Ma sagi, Mani n ŋa.

eng: and the delicious food and the bread she had made she put into her sons hands.
local: O n pili u n ŋa baasi, o naa bindira maa ni paanu so o ni se la paasi.

eng: Once when Jacob was boiling stew, Esau came in from the field hungry.
local: Dindaso Yakubu daa dugira ziiri, ka Isawu yi moo puu ta, a ni kom.

eng: He was terrified and thought, This sacred place is awesome. Its none other than Gods house and the entrance to heaven.
local: Dabeem gbaag u, o yeli, Kpe yela mari dabeem gbagba. Ka buŋ zaa n lee na n paasi Naawunni dii ni Alizaana dinaŋgbanni.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Zilipa maali n dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

eng: Even before he finished speaking, Rebekah—daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah wife of Nahor, Abrahams brother—was coming out with a water jar on her shoulder.
local: O na suusirini Naawunni maa, ka Rabeeka bugi yuuri ta bulugu maa puwa, o ti ŋoo koom.

eng: So Isaac built an altar there and worshipped in the LORD s name. Isaac pitched his tent there, and his servants dug a well.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka me sariga daaŋu, ka kpeesi Duuma, ka sabi o gbana suga.

eng: Jacob told Rachel that he was related to her father and that he was Rebekahs son. She then ran to tell her father.
local: Ka yeli Rachel maa, o niŋini o ba sunzopoga Rabeeka biya. Rachel chee yoom wa ti yeli o ba.

eng: He said, Look up and watch all the striped, speckled, and spotted male goats mounting the flock. Ive seen everything that Laban is doing to you.
local: Malaaka maa yeli ma, Nyaasi, gbeesi dima la n duura la bani chericheri, ka seesi niŋa mari booma, m nye bunso zaa Leban ni niŋini i.

eng: Jacob went to his father and said, My father. And he said, Im here. Who are you, my son.
local: Niŋi n na, o dee bindira la gaa o ba saani, ka yeli, M ba. O ba sagi, m biya, Wunbo n na.

eng: and said to Jacob, Im starving! Let me devour some of this red stuff. Thats why his name is Edom.
local: A zu, Isawu yeli Yakubu, Ti ma e zimooma maa seesi, kom mari ma ga. Lala zu n na ba boona u Idom la.

eng: After Jacob got up early in the morning, he took the stone that he had put near his head, set it up as a sacred pillar, and poured oil on the top of it.
local: A n chee bee bogufiili, Yakubu naa kugiri la o n kogi la damsi n sabi, ka kpaa kpaam pa o niŋi, a naag u pobi Naawunni nuusi.

eng: and Leah said, Im happy now because women call me happy. So she named him Asher.
local: Liya yeli, Wula sufupeelim ma bi mari? Pogiba ni boona ma sufupeelim. Ka digili o yuuri Asa.

eng: The young woman was very beautiful, old enough to be married, and hadnt known a man intimately. She went down to the spring, filled her water jar, and came back up.
local: Bipuŋŋu maa daa n veela pogisara ga, ka mi na bi baŋŋi dabsi. O sigi kuliga maa puu wa ti ŋoo koom pori n du.

eng: But Abimelech set out toward him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his ally and Phicol the commander of his forces.
local: Dindaso, Abimeliki ni Ahuza, o jinatoora, a ni Fikoli o soojasi kpeema yi Gera ta Yisaaka saani.

eng: When Laban heard about Jacob his sisters son, he ran to meet him. Laban embraced him, kissed him, and invited him into his house, where Jacob recounted to Laban everything that had happened.
local: Leban ni si n wumi Rabeeka biduu Yakubu baaru, o zo yoom ti tuusi u. A ŋaami u tabi o nyaari, ka mugisi u, ka mari u ta laanta, Yakubu yeli u bunso zaa n niŋi.

eng: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a sacred pillar and where you made a solemn promise to me. Now, get up and leave this country and go back to the land of your relatives.
local: Mani n niŋi Naawunni so n daa n nyisi m maŋŋa wusi i yi Betel la, seela e n daa n kpaa kpaam duligi kugiri, ka digili noori ti ma la. Pimparaŋa, yi kpe, a gaa tiŋgbanso niŋi ba n dogi i la.

eng: Jacob said to his father, Im Esau your oldest son. Ive made what you asked me to. Sit up and eat some of the game so you can bless me.
local: Yakubu yeli o ba, Mani Isawu, e bikpeema n na. M niŋi seema e ni yeli ma ni n niŋi la, a zu, nyisigi n zi, a di bindira maa, ka nafa ma.

eng: Jacob said, Sell me your birthright today.
local: Yakubu yeli u, Naa e dogim kpebilim ŋa ti ma poin, ka m mi ti i ziiraa.

eng: He named that sacred place Bethel, though Luz was the citys original name.
local: Ka digili nisaaniŋi Betel. Tiŋŋa maa yuuri n daa n niŋi Luzi.

eng: During the wheat harvest, Reuben found some erotic herbs in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, Give me your sons erotic herbs.
local: Chi cheebu jemani, Rubin nye dogim tiim vaari yi moo puu ti ti o ma Liya. Rachel yeli Liya, Jaande, ti ma e biyaa dogim tiim vaari ŋa seesi.

eng: The servant ran to meet her and said, Give me a little sip of water from your jar.
local: Guruŋu maa chee n tuusi u, ka suusi u, Jaande, ti ma koom beela m ŋu.

eng: Isaac said to him, Why have you come after me? You resented me and sent me away from you.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka boosi ba, Bo n na ya chena m saani ya ti niŋi? Ni daa n ba ya saani, ya daa n chisi ma ni, ka kari ma yi ya saani.

eng: Laban said to him, Yes, you are my flesh and blood. After Jacob had stayed with Laban for a month.
local: Leban yeli u, e niŋini m valiga ni m zeem, Yakubu ba o saani ŋmerigunni.

eng: Rachel and Leah answered him, Is there any share or inheritance left for us in our fathers household?.
local: Niŋi n na, Rachel ni Liya yeli Yakubu, Ti bi lee n mari kapete zaa yi ti ba kapete puwa.

eng: Isaac said to his son, How could you find this so quickly, my son? He said, The LORD your God led me right to it.
local: Yisaaka boosi u, M biya, wula n na e nye e valigaa yoom seema? O yeli, Duuma so n niŋi e Naawunni la n ti ma zusuŋŋu.

eng: Esau said, Since Im going to die anyway, what good is my birthright to me.
local: Isawu yeli u, Nyaasi, kom n ya n ku ma, bo tono n na kpebilim mari.

eng: Jacob made a solemn promise: If God is with me and protects me on this trip Im taking, and gives me bread to eat and clothes to wear.
local: Yakubu digili noori yeli, Naawunni, ka e yi ba m saani, a kogira ma yi n cheni ŋa puwa, ka tira ma bindira ni bimpinna.

eng: Leah replied, Isnt it enough that youve taken my husband? Now you want to take my sons erotic herbs too? Rachel said, For your sons erotic herbs, Jacob may sleep with you tonight.
local: Liya boosi u, E n fa m sira ka che ma la, a na bi paali e nufu, e lee n ya n ti dee m bii dogim tiim vaari? Rachel yeli u, Ka e yi ti ma e biyaa dogim tiim vaari ŋa, e ni Yakubu ni tee n do yiiŋaa.

eng: She said, Drink, sir. Then she quickly lowered the water jar with her hands and gave him some water to drink.
local: O tuu koom maa yoom, ka yeli u, M yidaana, dee n ŋu.

eng: They said, We now see that the LORD was with you. We propose that there be a formal agreement between us and that we draw up a treaty with you:.
local: Ba yeli u, Ti nyeya maa Duuma bani e saani, a zu, ti ta, ti niŋi naŋgbanluŋko, a pe, ka digili noori ti taaba.

eng: Jacob looked up and saw Esau approaching with four hundred men. Jacob divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two women servants.
local: Yakubu daa n nyaasi, ka nye Isawu ni o niriba kobsinaasi n chena, a zu, o barigi o biisi la ti Liya, Rachel a ni ba bipogiba bayi la.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, You shouldnt have to work for free just because you are my relative. Tell me what you would like to be paid.
local: Leban boosi u, A kpali e nira n niŋi ma zu, e tumi n ti ma jaga? Yeli ma e sanyoori.

eng: Doesnt he think of us as foreigners since he sold us and has even used up the payment he received for us?.
local: M ba nyaasira ti ni maa saamba, o koosi ti di.

eng: Isaac said to Jacob, Come here and let me touch you, my son. Are you my son Esau or not.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka yeli u, Vuu n ta m saani, m siisi i nyee, m bii Isawu gbagba n na ŋa, koo ka wune n na.

eng: Jacob said, Give me your word today. And he did. He sold his birthright to Jacob.
local: Yakubu yeli u, Pe. Isawu sagi, ka pe, a naa o kpebilim maa tagi ziiri.

eng: and I return safely to my fathers household, then the LORD will be my God.
local: ma lee n ti pori n gaari m ba yiri ni alaanfiya, nyini n ni niŋi m Naawunni.

eng: When Jacob came back from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, You must sleep with me because Ive paid for you with my sons erotic herbs. So he slept with her that night.
