local: Zaanoori jemani, Yakubu n yi moo puu chena, Liya tuusi u soori, ka yeli u, M nini n ya n do yiiŋaa, a kpali bo, m naani m bii dogim tiim vaari yo o lala samini. O mari u do dindaari yiiŋu maa.

eng: When she finished giving him a drink, she said, Ill draw some water for your camels too, till theyve had enough to drink.
local: O n ti u koom maa, o ŋu n baasi, o yeli u, Ni ŋoo n ti e nyogimasi ŋa gba, a ŋu.

eng: you must not treat us badly since we havent harmed you and since we have treated you well at all times. Then we will send you away peacefully, for you are now blessed by the LORD .
local: a maa e ki niŋi ti daaŋu zaa, seema ti gba ni daa n bi namsi i, jemanso e n daa n ba ti saani la, ka yi ni niŋgbirimaasim. Ti baŋiya maa, Duuma nafa i ni.

eng: He put the servants and their children first, Leah and her children after them, and Rachel and Joseph last.
local: O ti bipogiba bayi la ni ba biisi pa yiya, Liya ni o biisi pa, ka Rachel ni Yisifu pa nyaaŋa.

eng: Now Laban had two daughters: the older was named Leah and the younger Rachel.
local: Bipunsi bayi n na Leban daa n mari, kpeema daana la yuuri n niŋi Liya, bibila daana la mi yuuri n niŋi Rachel.

eng: All of the wealth God took from our father belongs to us and our children. Now, do everything God told you to do.
local: A zu, bunso zaa Naawunni n dee n yi ti ba saani niŋini ti nini zaa biisi bini. A kpali lala zu, niŋi bunso zaa Naawunni n yeli e niŋi.

eng: So Jacob approached his father Isaac, and Isaac touched him and said, The voice is Jacobs voice, but the arms are Esaus arms.
local: Yakubu vuu n we o ba saani, o siisi u, ka yeli, E goo niŋini Yakubu goo, ka e nuusaa mi niŋi Isawu nuusi.

eng: So Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew. He ate, drank, got up, and left, showing just how little he thought of his birthright.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu ti u paanu ni tuya ziiri, o ŋobi, ka pebi ziiri maa, ka nyisigi n gaari. Seema Isawu n daa n zagsi o dogim kpebilim n na la.

eng: This stone that Ive set up as a sacred pillar will be Gods house, and of everything you give me I will give a tenth back to you.
local: Teesigu kugiraa ni sabi seela ŋa, ni niŋi e kpeesigu ziya, ka bunso zaa e n ti ma, m ni nyisi a piipii zu ti i.

eng: God responded to Leah. She became pregnant and gave birth to a fifth son for Jacob.
local: Naawun wumi Liya suusigu, o mari puwa, a dogi biduusi banu daana ti Yakubu.

eng: She emptied her water jar quickly into the watering trough, ran to the well again to draw water, and drew water for all of the camels.
local: O kpaa koom maa yomyom niŋi buŋkobiri maa koŋuurigu buŋ niŋi, ka lee n chee wa ti ŋoo koom ti kpaa n paasira, a zaa ti ŋu n piri.

eng: Isaac prepared a banquet for them, and they ate and drank.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka dugi bindira ga, ba di, ka ŋu.

eng: He himself went in front of them and bowed to the ground seven times as he was approaching his brother.
local: Ba n ti paagira o beeri, Yakubu gaa yii wa ti gba u tiŋŋa buyapoin.

eng: Leah had delicate eyes, but Rachel had a beautiful figure and was good-looking.
local: Liya nimbiisi daa n bi niŋi dede, ama Rachel wune daa n veela pogisara ga.

eng: So Jacob got up, put his sons and wives on the camels.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu naa o poga ni biisi ziili nyogimasi niŋi.

eng: Isaac didnt recognize him because his arms were hairy like Esaus arms, so he blessed him.
local: O daa n bi tee n baŋŋi u, o niŋa koba la zu, a kpali seema Isawu nuusi koba ni be n na la, a zu, o nafa u.

eng: Leah said, God gave me what I paid for, what I deserved for giving my servant to my husband. So she named him Issachar.
local: Liya yeli, Naawunni yo ma samini, a kpali ni naa m bipoga ti m sira zu. Ka digili bii maa yuuri Isaka.

eng: The man stood gazing at her, wondering silently if the LORD had made his trip successful or not.
local: Daba maa naa nufu digili tiŋŋa nyaasira u boin, a nye? Naawunni wumi o suusigu maa?.

eng: But Esau ran to meet him, threw his arms around his neck, kissed him, and they wept.
local: Isawu chee n ti ŋaami u tabi o puwa, a naag o kpuŋkpama vili o nuŋgori, ka mugisi u, ka ba zaa kumina.

eng: They got up early in the morning, and they gave each other their word. Isaac sent them off, and they left peacefully.
local: A n chee bee bogufiili, ba zaa nyisigi n digili noori ti taaba, ka Yisaaka beeli ba soori, ba mari sufupeelim kuli.

eng: Jacob loved Rachel and said, I will work for you for seven years for Rachel, your younger daughter.
local: Yakubu daa n kpabira Rachel, a zu, o yeli u, m ni tumi n ti i yuma ayapoin, ka e naa e bibila daana Rachel ti ma.

eng: Leah became pregnant again and gave birth to a sixth son for Jacob.
local: Liya lee n mari puu dogi biisi bayoobu daana ti Yakubu.

eng: and set out with all of his livestock and all of his possessions that he had acquired in Paddan-aram in order to return to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
local: ka kpagi o buŋkobiri zaa pa yiya, a ni binseesi zaa o n daa nye, a yi Mesopoteemiya paaligu paasi n gaa o ba Yisaaka saani, Keenan paaligu.

eng: As soon as the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold ring, weighing a half shekel, and two gold bracelets for her arms, weighing ten shekels.
local: Nyogimasi maa n ti ŋu n baasi, daba maa naa suga kunde niŋi o ŋoya, ka naa suga bensi ayi niŋi o nusogsi.

eng: Isaac said, Are you really my son Esau? And he said, I am.
local: Ama, poin ka o nafa u maa, o boosi u ni, M bii Isawu gbagba n ŋa? O sagi, Mm.

eng: Esau looked up and saw the women and children and said, Who are these with you? Jacob said, The children that God generously gave your servant.
local: Niŋi n na, Isawu nye pogiba ni biisi la, ka boosi u, Ya saani dima n ŋa? Yakubu yeli u, Biseesi Naawunni n zo e nachiima nimbaa, ka ti u n na la.

eng: That day Isaacs servants informed him about the well that they had been digging and said to him, We found water.
local: Dindaari maa gbagba, Yisaaka gurunsi ta o saani ti yeli u, seema ba n gbi bulugu, a paa koom n ŋa.

eng: Laban said, Id rather give her to you than to another man. Stay with me.
local: Leban yeli u, M ni naag u ti i n so, m ni naag u ti dabyindaana. Zi n saani kpe.

eng: and she said, God has given me a wonderful gift. Now my husband will honor me since Ive borne him six sons. So she named him Zebulun.
local: Ka yeli, Naawunni pi ma ni piinisuŋŋu, pimparaŋa m sira ni tira ma jilima, a kpali biduusi bayoobu n na ma dogi n ti u. Ka digili o yuuri Zebulun.

eng: Now, while Laban was out shearing his sheep, Rachel stole the households divine images that belonged to her father.
local: Poin ka ba yi, Leban daa n wa ti ŋmaasirini o peeri koba, ka Rachel zu o ba laanta buga.

eng: He said, Please tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your fathers house for us to spend the night.
local: Ka boosi u, Ŋani bii n niŋi i? Jaande, ka wusi ma, ti ni nye dii yi e ba laanta do.

eng: Isaac said, Bring some food here and let me eat some of my sons game so I can bless you. Jacob put it before him and he ate, and he brought him wine and he drank.
local: A zu, Yisaaka yeli u, Naa moo puu valiga ni bindira maa ta, m di, ka nafa i. Yakubu naa bindira maa ti ti u, o di, ka naa daseesi gba ti u, o ŋu.

eng: The women servants and their children came forward and bowed down.
local: Bipogiba la ni ba biisi ti gba tiŋŋa.

eng: He called it Shibah; therefore, the citys name has been Beer-sheba until today.
local: Yisaaka daa digili bulugu maa yuuri Siiba, o gbinni n niŋi naŋgbandigiligu. Seema a n di, ba boona tiŋŋa maa Beesiiba n na la, halli zinni.

eng: Jacob worked for Rachel for seven years, but it seemed like a few days because he loved her.
local: Yakubu tumi yuma ayapoin, a kpali Rachel zu, ka a ŋmaani maa daasi beela yi o saani, seema o n kpabira Rachel la zu.

eng: After this, she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.
local: A nyaaŋa, o dogi bipuŋŋu, ka digili o yuuri Dayina.

eng: Moreover, Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not sending word to him that he was leaving.
local: Yakubu gba daa n yomsini Leban, so n niŋi Aram daba la, o bi kpabira o baŋŋi maa ba warini.

eng: She responded, Im the daughter of Bethuel, who is the son of Milcah and Nahor.
local: O yeli u, M ba n niŋi Betuwel, Naho ni Milika biya.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Come here and kiss me, my son.
local: Niŋi n na, o ba Yisaaka yeli u, M biya, vuu n ti mugisi ma.

eng: Then Leah and her servants also came forward and bowed, and afterward Joseph and Rachel came forward and bowed.
local: Seesi n lee n pa, Liya ni o biisi ti gba tiŋŋa. A baasigu, Yisifu ni Rachel gba ti gba tiŋŋa.

eng: When Esau was years old, he married Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite.
local: Isawu daa niŋini yuma pisinaasi poin, ka daa n naa Hititi pogiba bayi, Judifu, Beeri biya, a ni Basemeti, Alon biya.

eng: Jacob said to Laban, The time has come. Give me my wife so that I may sleep with her.
local: Dindaso, Yakubu yeli Leban, M tuma la jemani baasiya, cheli m ni e bii Rachel kulisi.

eng: Then God remembered Rachel, responded to her, and let her conceive.
local: Naawunni teesi Rachel yela, a wumi o suusigu, ka yoog o dogim.

eng: So Jacob and his entire household left. He got up, crossed the river, and set out directly for the mountains of Gilead.
local: A kpali lala zu, Yakubu daa n naani o bunso zaa zo n wa ti du mogikarigu, so ba n boona Yufireeti la, a naa o zu kpa Giliyadi pelli, seela zoori ni be ga la.

eng: She continued, We have plenty of straw and feed for the camels, and a place to spend the night.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, o lee n yeli, Ti mari buŋkobiri bindira, a ni a bindoori, a ni saamba dii, ya n di n do.

eng: So he came close and kissed him. When Isaac smelled the scent of his clothes, he blessed him, See, the scent of my son is like the scent of the field that the LORD has blessed.
local: Yakubu vuu n wa ti mugisi u, Yisaaka ni wumi o bimpinna maa ŋuwa, o nafa u, ka yeli n ŋa, Ee, m biyaa si n ŋusirini maa tiŋgbaŋaa Duuma ni nafa.

eng: Esau said, Whats the meaning of this entire group of animals that I met? Jacob said, To ask for my masters kindness.
local: Isawu boosi u, Ka buŋkobseesi ni tuusi la mini, bo n na a mi wusi? Yakubu yeli u, M yidaana, e piini n na, m nye seema ti mabiilim di n gbaa taaba.

eng: They made life very difficult for Isaac and Rebekah.
local: Pogiba bayi maa daa ti ni beebu loobi toom ti Yisaaka ni o poga Rabeeka.

eng: So Laban invited all the people of that place and prepared a banquet.
local: A zu, Leban laŋsi tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa, a ti ba dugi poginaagirisim bindira ti ba.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son and said, God has taken away my shame.
local: o mari puwa, a dogi biduwa. Ka yeli, Naawunni nyisi ma yi vi niŋi, o ti ma dogi biduwa.

eng: Three days later, Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
local: Yakubu n daa zo maa dabaa ta daari, poin ka Leban wumi maa o zoya.

eng: The man bowed down and praised the LORD :.
local: Daba maa n wumi n ŋa, o gba tiŋŋa, a kpeesi Naawunni.

eng: May God give you showers from the sky, olive oil from the earth, plenty of grain and new wine.
local: Naawunni n ti i saazu magilim, a ti i pubindira ga, halli chi baligu ni buŋŋuura zaa.

eng: Esau said, I already have plenty, my brother. Keep whats yours.
local: Isawu yeli u, M sunzo, m sali n mari buŋgbaliŋu, mari e bini.

eng: However, in the evening, he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he slept with her.
local: Ama yiiŋu n zibi, o naa Liya ti Yakubu, o mari u do.

eng: She named him Joseph, saying to herself, May the LORD give me another son.
local: O digili o yuuri Yisifu, ka yeli, Naawunni o na kpari n paasi ma biduwa.

eng: Bless the LORD , God of my master Abraham, who hasnt given up his loyalty and his faithfulness to my master. The LORD has shown me the way to the household of my masters brother.
local: Ka yeli, Kpeesigu niŋini Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni la toori, so n bi zagsi o nivumantiri ni yamonsitiri yi m yidaana Ibrahim niŋi. Ama, ma wune, Duuma pa ma ni yii soori maa puu ta m yidaana niriba yiri.

eng: so Laban took his brothers with him, chased Jacob for seven days, and caught up with him in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Niŋi n na, Leban mari o niriba kari n pa Yakubu zu, dabaa ayapoin daari poin, ka o paag u, a yi Giliyadi paaligu, seela zoori n be ga maa.

eng: May the nations serve you, may peoples bow down to you. Be the most powerful man among your brothers, and may your mothers sons bow down to you. Those who curse you will be cursed, and those who bless you will be blessed.
local: Naawunni o ti balisi ba tumina i, ka durinye dima ba dobira i tiŋŋa. E ni niŋi yidaandaba yi e mabiisi niŋi. Ka niseesi ni si i noori ni nye naŋgbansiibu, ka seesi ni nafa i, ni nye nafa.

eng: Jacob said, No, please, do me the kindness of accepting my gift. Seeing your face is like seeing Gods face, since youve accepted me so warmly.
local: Yakubu suusi u, Jaande, ka a yi peligi e sufu maa ni za e saana, dee piini so ni ma n ti ti i ŋa. Seema ni si n za e saani a, a niŋini maa ni za Naawunni saani.

eng: In the morning, there she was—Leah! Jacob said to Laban, What have you done to me? Didnt I work for you to have Rachel? Why did you betray me.
local: Bee n ti chee Yakubu baŋŋi maa Liya n na. O wa ti boosi Leban, Bo n na e niŋi ma lala? M tumi i ni, ka e naa Rachel ti ma, wula zu n na e yomsi ma.

eng: After Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Send me off so that I can go to my own place and my own country.
local: Rachel n dogi Yisifu nyaaŋa, Yakubu yeli Leban, Basi ma soori m pori n gaa m yiri.

eng: The young woman ran and told her mothers household everything that had happened.
local: Bipuŋŋu maa chee n gaa o ma laanta, a wa ti yeli yela maa zaa.

eng: That night, God appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream and said, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: Dindaari yiiŋu, Naawunni nyisi o maŋŋa wusi Leban, a yi zaasim niŋi, ka yeli u, Dasi, ka da pa togsi yatogidagu zaa ŋmeŋsi u.

eng: After Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and just as Jacob left his father Isaac, his brother Esau came back from his hunt.
local: Yisaaka ni si n nafa Yakubu ti baasira, ka o beeri Isawu yi o moo puu goorim ta.

eng: Take this present that Ive brought because God has been generous to me, and I have everything I need. So Jacob persuaded him, and he took it.
local: Jaande, dee binseesi ni ma n ti ti i ŋa. Naawunni zo ma ni nimbaa, ka ti ma bunso zaa ni kpabira. A kpali Yakubu n suusi Isawu zu, o deeg a.

eng: Laban said, Where we live, we dont give the younger woman before the oldest.
local: Leban yeli u, Ti kpe, ti kaari chisirini bii ni daŋŋi kpeema kulisi.

eng: Jacob went on his way, and Gods messengers approached him.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu daa lee n naa soori wara, Naawunni malaakasi ti paag u.

eng: Give me my wives and children whom Ive worked for, and I will go. You know the work Ive done for you.
local: Naa m pogiba ni biisi seesi zu ni kpali m tumi i la ti ma, m gaari. E baŋŋi seema ni tumi i nene.

eng: Rebekah had a brother named Laban, and Laban ran to the man outside by the spring.
local: Rabeeka sunzodaba Leban n si n nye o ŋoya kunde la ni bensi la n ba o sunzopoga nuusi, ka wumi seema Rabeeka n yeli bunso daba maa n yeli u, o yi yoom gaa daba maa saani, a wa ti nye o n za n begi o nyogimasi, a yi kuliga maa noori.

eng: Laban reached Jacob after Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountains. So Laban and his brothers also pitched theirs in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Yakubu daa sabini o gbana suga yi Giliyadi zoori puwa, nisaaniŋi gbagba maa n na Leban gba ni o yidima sabi ba gbana suga.

eng: He too made some delicious food, brought it to his father, and said, Let my father sit up and eat from his sons game so that you may bless me.
local: Niŋi n na, o gba dugi bindira meesa ti ti o ba, ka yeli u, M ba, nyisigi n zi, a di m bindira, ka nafa ma.

eng: Esau said, Lets break camp and set out, and Ill go with you.
local: Isawu yeli u, Niŋi siriya ti gaari, m ni pa i yiya.

eng: Complete the celebratory week with this woman. Then I will give you this other woman too for your work, if you work for me seven more years.
local: Baasi e pogipaali yaniŋisa zaa daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, ka m naa Rachel ti i, ka e lee n tumi n ti ma yuma ayapoin.

eng: When Jacob saw them, he said, This is Gods camp, and he named that sacred place Mahanaim.
local: O n si n nye ba, o yeli, Naawunni ziiziya n ŋa. Ka digili niŋi yuuri Mahanayim.

eng: Laban said to him, Do me this favor. Ive discovered by a divine sign that the LORD has blessed me because of you.
local: Leban yeli u, Ka e nye m nimbaa, e zi kpe. M bogini nye maa, Duuma nafa ma ni, a ti ma bindaalim, a kpali e zu.

eng: Laban said, Come in, favored one of the LORD ! Why are you standing outside? Ive prepared the house and a place for the camels.
local: O yeli u, Chena, nyini so Duuma n nafa, bo n na e za yaŋŋa? M maali dii zaali i, e nyogimasi ŋa gba zii ba niŋi.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, What have you done? You have deceived me and taken off with my daughters as if they were prisoners of war.
local: Niŋi n na, Leban boosi Yakubu, Wula zu n na e yomsi ma, a mari m bipunsi zo maa ba n gbaa daaba yi toowa niŋi.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Who are you? And he said, Im your son, your oldest son, Esau.
local: O ba Yisaaka boosi u, Ŋani n na? Bii maa yeli, Mani e bikpeema Isawu.

eng: But Jacob said to him, My master knows that the children arent strong and that I am responsible for the nursing flocks and cattle. If I push them hard for even one day, all of the flocks will die.
local: Ama Yakubu yeli u, M yidaana, e baŋiya maa biisaa na ka kpeeŋu, ka a daga maa m dasi penyaana ni naginyaana seesi n na mari bibaala la niŋi. Ka ti si n kari a kpeeŋtiri dindaluŋko lala, buŋkobiri maa zaa ni kpi.

eng: So that is what Jacob did. He completed the celebratory week with this woman, and then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.
local: Yakubu sagi, ka baasi poginaagirisim yaniŋisa maa daba ayapoin maa, Leban naa o bipuŋŋu Rachel ti u o niŋi o poga.

eng: Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau, toward the land of Seir, the open country of Edom.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.

eng: so name your price and I will pay it.
local: Wusi ma m samini, m yo i.

eng: So the man entered the house. Then Laban unbridled the camels, provided straw and feed for them and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men with him.
local: Niŋi n na, daba maa gaa laanta, ka Leban lerigi nyogimasi la ziiri, ka naa kaga ni bindira ti ti a, ka ŋoo n ti ti daba maa ni niseesi n pa o zu la, ba pegi ba noba.

eng: Why did you leave secretly, deceiving me, and not letting me know? I wouldve sent you off with a celebration, with songs and tambourines and harps.
local: Wula zu n na e sogi n zo, ka bi yeli ma? Ka e di kali ma ni, m di n naa binŋmeera ŋme, a yiimi n beeli i soori.

eng: Isaac was so shocked that he trembled violently. He said, Who was the hunter just here with game? He brought me food, and I ate all of it before you came. I blessed him, and he will stay blessed.
local: A ku Yisaaka noori, o niŋa dammi u, o boosi, Ka ŋani mini n go moo, a ku valiga dugi n ti ti ma? M si n dini baasi, ka nafa u, beela ka e ta la. Yamonsi, o ni nye nafa.

eng: My master, go on ahead of your servant, but Ive got to take it easy, going only as fast as the animals in front of me and the children are able to go, until I meet you in Seir.
local: A zu, m yidaana, paara e nachiima yiya, ka m mari buŋkobiri la ni biisi maa chaŋina boinboin, a ti paag i yi Idom paaligu.

eng: Jacob slept with Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah. He worked for Laban seven more years.
local: Yakubu mari Rachel gba do, ka kpabira u gaa Liya. Niŋi n na, o lee n tumi yuma ayapoin ti Leban.

eng: He gave them these orders: Say this to my master Esau. This is the message of your servant Jacob: Ive lived as an immigrant with Laban, where Ive stayed till now.
local: Yatogso o n naa ti tumusi maa ba wa ti ti o beeri la n niŋi, ni ba yeli u, ni o nachiima, Yakubu, so n daa n ba Leban saani yuu, ka bi chena yiri la n na.

eng: Jacob said to him, You know how Ive worked for you, and how well your livestock have done with me.
local: Yakubu yeli u, E baŋŋi seema ni tumi n ti i, a ni seema e buŋkobiri n wasi m nuu niŋi.

eng: and set out a meal for him. But the man said, I wont eat until Ive said something. Laban replied, Say it.
local: Ba mari bindira ti ti u, ni o di, o yeli, M ki di, ama m nyisini m soori baaru. Leban yeli u, Togsima.

eng: You didnt even let me kiss my sons and my daughters good-bye. Now youve acted like a fool.
local: E mi bi lee n ti ma mugisi m yaŋsi, ka kali ba ni ba madima, e wusi zolim ga.

eng: When Esau heard what his father said, he let out a loud agonizing cry and wept bitterly. He said to his father, Bless me! Me too, my father.
local: Isawu n wumi seema o ba n yeli maa, o chaari kpeeŋtiri ni sufusaaŋu, ka yeli o ba, Nafa m gba, m ba.

eng: Esau said, Let me leave some of my people with you. But Jacob said, Why should you do this since my master has already been so kind to me.
local: Isawu yeli u, M ni ti e ni m niribaa seesi ya chena. Yakubu yeli u, Da niŋi lala. O bi tiri n ti, m si n kpabira ni m nye seema e sufu n peligi m niŋi.

eng: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb; but Rachel was unable to have children.
local: Duuma nye maa, o bi kpabira Liya maa Rachel, o yoog o dogim, ka Rachel niŋi poginyee.

eng: While in my care, what little you had has multiplied a great deal. The LORD blessed you wherever I took your livestock. Now, when will I be able to work for my own household too.
local: Beela seesi e n daa n mari poin, ka ma ta, a paasiya ga. Seela zaa ni matira, Naawunni nafara i ni. Jemani paaya maa, m gbagba m nye m toori.

eng: I own cattle, donkeys, flocks, men servants, and women servants. Im sending this message to my master now to ask that he be kind.
local: Ni o marini naagi, buŋsi, peeri, buusi, a ni gurunsi dabsi ni pogiba, ka o suusira i maa e zo u nimbaa.

eng: The man said, I am Abrahams servant.
local: Niŋi o yeli, Ibrahim guruŋu n niŋi ma.

eng: and I have the power to punish you. However, your fathers God told me yesterday, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: M mari daama, m di n ti ba namsi i, ama, soosila yiiŋu, e ba Naawunni la, yeli ma ni m dasi, ka da pa ti togsi yatogibee zaa ti i.

eng: Isaac said, Your brother has already come deceitfully and has taken your blessing.
local: E sunzo tee n ti yomsi ma, a deeg e nafa maa.

eng: That day Esau returned on the road to Seir.
local: A dindaari zaa n na, Isawu daa n pori n gaa Idom.

eng: Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben because she said, The LORD saw my harsh treatment, and now my husband will love me.
local: Liya mari puwa, a dogi biduwa, ka yeli, Duuma nye m nimbaa, pimparaŋa m sira ni kpabira ma. A zu, o digili o bii maa yuuri Rubin .

eng: Laban said, What will I pay you? Jacob said, Dont pay me anything. If you will do this for me, I will take care of your flock again, and keep a portion.
local: O boosi u, Bo n na e kpabira? Yakubu yeli u, Da ti ma sen. Ama, ka e ni sagi n niŋi yaso ni ya n yeli i ŋa, m na n kpari n nyaasira e buŋkobiri maa.

eng: The messengers returned to Jacob and said, We went out to your brother Esau, and hes coming to meet you with four hundred men.
local: Tumusi maa n pori n ta, n ŋa n na ba yeli Yakubu, Ti wa ti yeli e beeri Isawu, o mari niriba kobsinaasi tuusira i.

eng: The LORD has richly blessed my master, has made him a great man, and has given him flocks, cattle, silver, gold, men servants, women servants, camels, and donkeys.
local: Duuma nafa m yidaana ga, o loobi bindaana, o ti u peeri, buusi, naagi, gbitisi, sugasi, gurunsi dabsi ni pogiba, nyogimasi, a ni buŋsi.

eng: Youve rushed off now because you missed your fathers household so much, but why did you steal my gods.
local: M baŋiya maa e zoni, e ba yiri weebu yela n moog e nufu, ka wula n na e zu m yiri buga.

eng: Esau said, Isnt this why hes called Jacob? Hes taken me twice now: he took my birthright, and now hes taken my blessing. He continued, Havent you saved a blessing for me.
local: Niŋi n na, Isawu yeli o ba, O buyi daana n na la, o n puni ma, a jaa, a zu, o yuuri di Yakubu. O daa n dee m dogim kpebilim, ka lee n dee m nafa. Ka o lee n boosi o ba, Ama, e bi che nafa maa yinni zaali ma.

eng: but Jacob traveled to Succoth. He built a house for himself but made temporary shelters for his animals; therefore, he named the place Succoth.
local: Yakubu gaa Sukoti me dii, ka maali o buŋkobiri beebu ziisi. A zu n na ba boona nisaaniŋi Sukoti la.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, The LORD heard that I was unloved, so he gave me this son too, and she named him Simeon.
local: O lee n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa, ka yeli, Duuma na ti ma ni biduwaa, a kpali o wumiya maa m ka kpabira. A zu, o digili o yuuri Simiyon.

eng: I will go through the entire flock today, taking out all of the speckled and spotted sheep, all of the black male lambs, and all of the spotted and speckled female goats. That will be my price.
local: Ti m kpe buŋkobiri maa puu gamisi a binchericherisi buusi ni peeri maa zaa, a ni pebiraasi, a loobi n toori. M sanyoori zaa n na la.

eng: Jacob was terrified and felt trapped, so he divided the people with him, and the flocks, cattle, and camels, into two camps.
local: Niŋi n na, dabeem ni sufusaaŋu gbaa Yakubu, a zu, o barigi o niriba la ni buusi, peeri, naagi, a ni nyogimasi tulum buyaayi zu.

eng: My masters wife Sarah gave birth to a son for my master in her old age, and hes given him everything he owns.
local: M yidaana poga Saara, nivukuritiri jemani n na o dogi biduu ti Ibrahim, ka o naag o bunso zaa ti bii maa.

eng: Jacob responded to Laban, I was afraid and convinced myself that you would take your daughters away from me.
local: Yakubu yeli Leban n ŋa, M daa zora i ni dabeem, ka fe maa, ka m yeli i, e ni fa e bipunsi.

eng: Isaac replied to Esau, Ive already made him more powerful than you, and Ive made all of his brothers his servants. Ive made him strong with grain and wine. What can I do for you, my son.
local: Yisaaka yeli o bii Isawu, M tee n naag u loobi e yidaana, ka naag o mabiisi zaa loobi o nachiinsi. O ni nye chi baligu ga, a ni daam ga. Wula n na ma lee n ya niŋi i, m biya.

eng: Jacob arrived safely at the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan on his trip from Paddan-aram, and he camped in front of the city.
local: Yakubu n yi Mesopoteemiya paaligu la, o paa Sekem n niŋi Keenan dima tiŋŋa, a ni alaanfiya, ka sabi o gbana suga, a zi nisaaniŋi, a leeli tiŋŋa maa.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, Now, this time my husband will embrace me, since I have given birth to three sons for him. So she named him Levi.
local: O lee n maali n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa. Ka yeli, Pimparaŋa m sira ni vuu n kpari ma, a kpali m dogini biduusi bata ti u. A zu, o digili o yuuri Livai.

eng: I will be completely honest with you: when you come to check on our agreement, every female goat with me that isnt speckled or spotted and every male lamb with me that isnt black will be considered stolen.
local: O soosa zu, e ni nye maa m mari birikoŋsi koo m ka birikoŋsi. Jemanso e n ta a puwa, ka nye bimpeeli yi a niŋi, buu koo pee, a ni pe so n bi niŋi bimbiraa, ka wune, m zuuni n na la.

eng: He thought, If Esau meets the first camp and attacks it, at least one camp will be left to escape.
local: A kpali bo, o feni maa, ka Isawu ti zabi n nyaŋŋi tulum buluŋko, seesi n che la ni sogi n zo.

eng: My master made me give him my word: Dont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women, in whose land Im living.
local: Pimparaŋa, m yidaana ti ma pe maa, ni m da pa ti naa poga yi Keenan paaligu, seela o n zi ŋa, a ti o bii maa.

eng: Esau said to his father, Do you really have only one blessing, Father? Bless me too, my father! And Esau wept loudly.
local: Niŋi n na, Isawu boosi o ba, M ba, nafaluŋko n na e mari? Nafa m gba. Ka chaari kpeeŋtiri.

eng: Whomever you find with your divine images wont live. Identify whatever I have that is yours, in front of your brothers, and take it. Jacob didnt know that Rachel had stolen them.
local: Ka e nye e buga ŋa yi ti yindaana zaa saani, a daana bi daga o di nyevuri. Nyaasi m binaa zaa puwa, ka e yi nye bunso n niŋi e toori, e nyisima. Yakubu mi daa n bi baŋŋi maa Rachel n zu o ba buga maa.

eng: He bought the section of the field where he pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechems father, for one hundred qesitahs.
local: Yakubu dani tiŋgbaŋŋu maa yi Hamo, so n niŋi Sekem ba, biisi saani. Gbitisi koowa n na o da u.

eng: No, instead, go to my fathers household and to my relatives and choose a wife for my son.
local: Ama m gaari o ba yiri, o dogim puwa, o niriba gbagba saani, a naa poga ti ti o biya.

eng: Laban said, All right; lets do it.
local: Leban yeli u, M sagi seema e n yeli maa.

eng: Jacob said, LORD , God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac, who said to me, Go back to your country and your relatives, and Ill make sure things go well for you.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu suusi Naawunni, M yaadaba Ibrahim ni m ba Yisaaka Naawunni, m yidaana, nyini n daa n yeli ma, ni m pori n gaa m paaligu, m niriba saani, e ni ti bunso zaa chaŋŋi nene ti ma.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, This time I will praise the LORD . So she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing children.
local: O lee n maali n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa, ka yeli, Pimparaŋa wune m ni naa kpeesigu ti m Duuma. A zu, o digili bii maa yuuri Juuda. Niŋi n na, o dogim tisi.

eng: His father Isaac responded and said to him, Now, you will make a home far away from the olive groves of the earth, far away from the showers of the sky above.
local: O ba Yisaaka yeli u, E ziiziya ni tiŋgbansuŋŋu ni ba wogilim, magilim ki yi saazu ti maag e tiŋgbaŋŋu.

eng: I said to my master, What if the woman wont come back with me.
local: Niŋi n na, ma boosi m yidaana, ka poga maa yeli o ki pa m zu ta?.

eng: However, on that very day Laban took out the striped and spotted male goats and all of the speckled and spotted female goats—any with some white in it—and all of the black male lambs, and gave them to his sons.
local: Dindaari maa zaa Leban gamisi budaasi ni bunyaana seesi n ba chericheri, a ni pebiraasi ni a binchericherisi zaa nyem ti o biisi, ba nyaasira a.

eng: I dont deserve how loyal and truthful youve been to your servant. I went away across the Jordan with just my staff, but now Ive become two camps.
local: Mani e guruŋu di bi daga e niŋi ma bunso ni nye ŋa, e kpabirisim ni e yamonsi n ka baasigu so e wusi a. M daa n duni Joodan mogiraa ni daŋkpalli ko, ama, zinni, m niriba ni m buŋkobiri ba tulum buyi zu.

eng: Laban went into Jacobs tent, Leahs tent, and her two servants tent and didnt find them. So he left Leahs tent and went into Rachels.
local: Leban kpe Yakubu gbana suga dii, a yuligi n kpabira o buga, a ni Liya ni ba bipogiba diisi zaa n na o kpe. A nyaaŋa, o kpe Rachel sugiri puwa.

eng: Then he set up an altar there and named it El Elohe Israel.
local: Nisaaniŋi n na Yakubu me Naawunni sariga daaŋu, ka digili u El-Elohe Izireel.

eng: You will live by your sword; you will serve your brother. But when you grow restless, you will tear away his harness from your neck.
local: E tokobi niŋi n na, e ni dolli n dira, ka e ni niŋi e sunzo nachiima. Ama, dindaso zaa e ni zagsi suurisi maa, e ni fa e zu yi o nuusi niŋi.

eng: He said to me, The LORD , whom Ive traveled with everywhere, will send his messenger with you and make your trip successful; and you will choose a wife for my son from my relatives and from my fathers household.
local: O yeli ma, ni Duuma so o n wumi n tira la ni tumi malaaka o paa ma gaari, bunso zaa, a maali, m naa poga yi o niriba puwa, a yi o ba laanta ti ti o biya.

eng: He put a three-day trip between himself and Jacob, while Jacob was watching the rest of Labans flock.
local: O naa dabaa ata mari buŋkobiri maa chaŋŋi n gaa wogila, ka Yakubu nyaasira a seesi n che la.

eng: Save me from my brother Esau! Im afraid he will come and kill me, the mothers, and their children.
local: A zu, m suusira i ni e moligi ma yi m beeri Isawu nuusi niŋi. M zorini dabeem maa o chena o ti zabi m ni pogiba ni biisi toowa.

eng: Esau was furious at Jacob because his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, When the period of mourning for the death of my father is over, I will kill my brother.
local: Niŋi n na, Isawu chisi Yakubu, a kpali nafa la o ba n nafa u la zu, ka fe n ŋa yi o sufu niŋi. A ki lee n yuu, ka m ba o kpi, ti kumi o kuuri, kuuri maa nyaaŋa n na ma ni ku m sunzo Yakubu.

eng: Then Jacob took new branches from poplar, almond, and plane trees; and he peeled white stripes on them, exposing the branches white color.
local: Yakubu wa ti kpabi tibalisi ata, a ŋma a wulimaasa foli n foli a pegiri, boomabooma, a peligi n peligi.

eng: Now Rachel had taken the divine images and put them into the camels saddlebag and sat on them. Laban felt around in the whole tent but couldnt find them.
local: Rachel mi daa n naani o ba buga maa niŋi o nyogima kogu gbinniŋi, ka zi a niŋi. Niŋi n na, Leban nyaasi gbana sugiri maa zaa puu nyem, o bi nye bunso zaa.

eng: You were the one who told me, I will make sure things go well for you, and I will make your descendants like the sand of the sea, so many you wont be able to count them.
local: Ama, nyini n daa n yeli ma, ni e n ti m loobi bindaana, ka ti m baligu saligi maa teeku puu tambisigu seesi di n ki tee n kaali.

eng: If you go to my relatives, you will be free from your obligation to me. Even if they provide no one for you, you will be free from your obligation to me.
local: Ni ka m yi gaari o niriba saani, peebu maa ki lee n gbaa ma, halli ka ba moŋŋi ma poga maa, naŋgbansamini maa bi lee n pa ma.

eng: Rebekah was told what her older son Esau was planning, so she summoned her younger son Jacob and said to him, Esau your brother is planning revenge. He plans to kill you.
local: Rabeeka ni baŋŋi seema Isawu ni le o sufu niŋi, o tumi n booli o bibila daana Yakubu, a yeli u, E beeri Isawu ya n ku i ni, a yo samini.

eng: He set the branches that he had peeled near the watering troughs so that they were in front of the flock when they drank, because they often mated when they came to drink.
local: Niŋi n na, o naa daari digili n digili buŋkobiri maa koom ŋuubu ziya, a kpali, buŋkobiri maa yi duurini taaba koom ŋuubu maa ziya.

eng: Jacob spent that night there. From what he had acquired, he set aside a gift for his brother Esau:.
local: Yakubu daa doni niŋi kaa bee, ka nyisi binseesi o naa wa ti pi o beeri Isawu piini.

eng: Rachel said to her father, Sir, dont be angry with me because I cant get up for you; Im having my period. He searched but couldnt find the divine images.
local: Rachel yeli o ba Leban, Jaande, da pa ti je suuri niŋi ma, a kpali ni bi nyisigi n za e yii zu, m pegirini. Niŋi n na, Leban nyaasi seela zaa, ka bi nye o yiri buga maa.

eng: Today I arrived at the spring, and I said, LORD , God of my master Abraham, if you wish to make the trip Im taking successful.
local: Jemanso m ni paa kuligaa puu zinaa, ma yeli, M Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, ka a niŋi e kiperi, jaande, ti m nye zusuŋŋu, m cheni ŋa puwa.

eng: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams.
local: O daa nyisini bunyaana kobsiyi, gbeesidima pisiyi, penyaana kobsiyi, a ni pedaasi pisiyi.

eng: So now, my son, listen to me: Get up and escape to my brother Laban in Haran.
local: A zu, biya, wumi nene, ka niŋi seema ni ya n yeli i ŋa. Yi kpe yoom, a zo n gaa m sunzodaba Leban saani, a yi Haran tiŋŋa.

eng: When the flock mated in front of the branches, they gave birth to striped, speckled, and spotted young.
local: Buŋkobiri maa si n za daari maa see duura, a dogirini binchericherisi.

eng: Jacob was angry and complained to Laban, What have I done wrong and whats my crime that youve tracked me down like this?.
local: Yakubu je suuri niŋi Leban, ka boosi u, Yabee bo n na ma niŋi i, e kpali lala zu kari n pa m zu?.

eng: when Im standing by the spring and the young woman who comes out to draw water and to whom I say, Please give me a little drink of water from your jar.
local: Nyaasi, m zani kuligaa puu n na la, ka suŋguri zaa taya, o ti ŋoo koom, ka ma yeli u, Jaande, ti ma e koom maa beela m ŋu.

eng: thirty nursing camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, and twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
local: nyogimanyaana pisita a ni a biisi, naginyaana pisinaasi a ni nagidaasi piiya, buŋnyaana pisiyi ni bindaasi piiya.

eng: Live with him for a short while until your brothers rage subsides.
local: Ka ba o saani daba ayi, e beeri suuri o maa poin.

eng: Jacob sorted out the lambs, turning the flock to face the striped and black ones in Labans flock but keeping his flock separate, setting them apart from Labans flock.
local: Yakubu barigi pesaasi maa yi Leban buŋkobiri maa niŋi. Ka penyaana maa ya n ti du, o yi tini a nyaasira Leban buŋkobseesi n ba chericheri maa. N ŋa n na Yakubu niŋi n barigi o buŋkobiri yi Leban toori niŋi.

eng: Youve now felt through all of my baggage, and what have you found from your households belongings? Put it in front of our relatives, and let them decide between us.
local: Pimparaŋa, e n nyaasi m bini zaa puusi, bo n na e nye, ka o niŋi e toori? Bunso zaa e ni nye, ka o niŋi e toori, e naag u digili so zaa, a ni e niriba ba nye, ka nye so n mari yamonsi.

eng: and she responds to me, Drink, and I will draw water for your camels too, may she be the woman the LORD has selected for my masters son.
local: Ka o yeli ma, Ŋuma, ka m ŋoo n ti e nyogimasi ŋa gba, cheli o niŋi so Duuma n nyisi n ti m yidaana Ibrahim biya.

eng: He separated these herds and gave them to his servants. He said to them, Go ahead of me and put some distance between each of the herds.
local: O daa n nyisini buŋkobiri yiri zaa kpagira, ka yeli ba, Ya pa ma yii wara, ka ti taaba lagima.

eng: until your brothers anger at you goes away and he forgets what you did to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back from there. Why should I suffer the loss of both of you on one day.
local: Ka o suuri ti maa, m ni tolimi booli i. A kpali, m bi kpabira n koŋŋi ya bayi zaa dindaluŋko.

eng: Whenever the strongest of the flock mated, Jacob put the branches in front of them near the watering troughs so that they mated near the branches.
local: Buŋkobnyaana seesi n mari kpeena si n ti duura, Yakubu yi naa ni daari maa digili a koom ŋuubu zii maa, a duura nisaaniŋi.

eng: Before I finished saying this to myself, Rebekah came out with her water jar on her shoulder and went down to the spring to draw water. And I said to her, Please give me something to drink.
local: M na bi baasi Naawunni suusigu maa m sufu niŋi, ka Rabeeka bugi o koom yuuri ta, a sigi kuliga ŋoo koom, ma yeli u, Jaande, ka ti ma koom beela m ŋu.

eng: He ordered the first group, When my brother Esau meets you and asks you, Who are you with? Where are you going? And whose herds are these in front of you.
local: Niŋi n na, o yeli o guruŋtumusi maa yipaara, Ka m beeri Isawu tuusi ya soori, ka boosi i, E yidaana n niŋi ŋani? koo Ya n na e wara? koo Ŋani buŋkobiri n ŋa.

eng: Rebekah then said to Isaac, I really loathe these Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women, like the women of this land, why should I go on living.
local: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka yeli o sira Yisaaka, M sufu bi maa ma, a kpali, Hititi pogiba la zu. Ka Yakubu gba lee n naa Hititi poga, m di kpiya so.

eng: She immediately lowered her water jar and said, Drink, and I will give your camels something to drink too. So I drank and she also gave water to the camels.
local: O tuu o koom yuuri maa yomyom yi o bogu niŋi, ka yeli ma, Ŋu ma, m ni ŋoo n ti e nyogimasi ŋa gba. A zu, ma ŋu, ka o ti nyogimasi maa gba.

eng: say, They are your servant Jacobs, a gift sent to my master Esau. And Jacob is actually right behind us.
local: e yeli u, A niŋini e nachiima Yakubu bini, o tumi ti, ni ti naa n ti ti i, ama o gba ba nyaaŋa chena.

eng: For these twenty years Ive been with you, your female sheep and goats havent miscarried, and I havent eaten your flocks rams.
local: M nini ni ba niŋi, yuma pisiyi ŋa zaa, m na bi ku e pedaa koo budaa zaa ŋobi, ka e penyaana ni bunyaana mi bi saasi n basi.

eng: But he didnt put branches up for the weakest of the flock. So the weakest became Labans and the strongest Jacobs.
local: Ama, a bimpugiri la wune si n ti duura, o bi diŋina daari maa niŋi. A zu, bimpugiri maa loobirini Leban toori, ka bi n kpeena la mi loobira Yakubu toori.

eng: Then I asked her, Whose daughter are you? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahors son whom Milcah bore him. I put a ring in her nose and bracelets on her arms.
local: Ma boosi u, Ŋani bii n niŋi i? O yeli, M ba n niŋi Betuwel, Naho ni Milika biya. Niŋi n na, ma naa kunde niŋi o ŋoya, ka naa bensi niŋi o nusogsi.

eng: When animals were killed, I didnt bring them to you but took the loss myself. You demanded compensation from me for any animals poached during the day or night.
local: Ka moo puu buŋkoowa yi gbaag e buu koo pee, mani n yi yo o samini. Buŋkoowa so zaa ba n daa n yi zu, yiiŋu koo wuntooŋu, e yi tini ma yo o samini.

eng: He also ordered the second group, the third group, and everybody following the herds, Say exactly the same thing to Esau when you find him.
local: Lala yatogiraa gbagba n na o naa n ti tumusi bayi daana maa gba, a ni bata daana maa, ni ba zaa ba yeli o beeri Isawu.

eng: The man Jacob became very, very rich: he owned large flocks, female and male servants, camels, and donkeys.
local: N ŋa sooraa n na Yakubu naa n loobi bindaana, a mari buŋkobdamta, a ni guruŋbipogiba, guruŋnachiinsi, nyogimasi, a ni buŋsi.

eng: I bowed and worshipped the LORD and blessed the LORD , the God of my master Abraham, who led me in the right direction to choose the granddaughter of my masters brother for his son.
local: Ma gba tiŋŋa puusi Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, so n pa ma yiya, a ni seema n daga, ma nye m yidaana Ibrahim mabii yaŋŋu ti o biya.

eng: Say also, Your servant Jacob is right behind us. Jacob thought, I may be able to pacify Esau with the gift Im sending ahead. When I meet him, perhaps he will be kind to me.
local: Ya daŋŋi n yeli u, E nachiima Yakubu ba nyaaŋa chena. Yakubu daa feni maa, Ka o naa n ŋa piini pa yii wa ti ti o beeri Isawu, o puu ni peligi, ka o deeg u nuusi ayi.

eng: Now if youre loyal and faithful to my master, tell me. If not, tell me so I will know where I stand either way.
local: A zu, ya yeli ma, ka ya ni wusi m yidaana nivumantiri ni yamonsitiri, ya ti m baŋŋi, ka ya mi ki lee ti, ya yeli ma, m baŋŋi, soso m di n dolli.

eng: The dry heat consumed me during the day, and the frost at night; I couldnt sleep.
local: Jemanso wuluŋu ni waari n daa n kuura ma, ka ma daa n ki tee n gbisi.

eng: So Jacob sent the gift ahead of him, but he spent that night in the camp.
local: A zu, o ti ni piini ŋa pa yiya, ka ba do nisaaniŋi.

eng: Laban and Bethuel both responded, This is all the LORD s doing. We have nothing to say about it.
local: Niŋi n na, Leban ni Betuwel yeli, Yela ŋa zaa yini Duuma saani, a zu, ti wune bi lee n mari sen ti n di n yeli.

eng: Ive now spent twenty years in your household. I worked for fourteen years for your two daughters and for six years for your flock, and you changed my pay ten times.
local: N ŋa beebu puu ma daa n be, a yi e saani yuma pisiyi. M tumi i yuma pii ni anaasi poin, ka nye e bipunsi bayi naa, yuma ayoobu n na ma lee n nyaasi e buŋkobiri, ka m gba mi nye m toori, a ni ŋama zaa, e cherigi m sanyoori bupii zu.

eng: Jacob got up during the night, took his two wives, his two women servants, and his eleven sons, and crossed the Jabbok Rivers shallow water.
local: Dindaari maa yiiŋu, Yakubu mari o pogiba bayi ni o pogibilisi bayi, a ni ba biisi pii ni yinni, a loo n du Jabok mogiri.

eng: Here is Rebekah, right in front of you. Take her and go. She will be the wife of your masters son, just as the LORD said.
local: Rabeeka n ŋa, mari u wa ti ti e yidaana bii maa, o niŋi o poga, a maa seema Duuma ni zaali la.

eng: If the God of my father—the God of Abraham and the awesome one of Isaac—hadnt been with me, youd have no doubt sent me away without anything. God saw my harsh treatment and my hard work and reprimanded you yesterday.
local: Ka Naawunni, so m ba dima, Ibrahim ni Yisaaka ni daa n tumina la, di ka m saani, e di kari ma basi nuyoosi, ama, Naawunni nye m nam, a ni seema ni tumi i, ka yiiŋu la, o kpaasi e tubiri.

eng: He took them and everything that belonged to him, and he helped them cross the river.
local: O n mari ba du mogiri maa baasi, o pori n ti mari o bunso zaa n che du.

eng: When Abrahams servant heard what they said, he bowed low before the LORD .
local: Ibrahim guruŋu maa ni wumi seema ba ni yeli la, o gba tiŋŋa, a naa puusigu ti Duuma.

eng: Laban responded and told Jacob, The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, and the flocks are my flocks. Everything you see is mine. But what can I do now about my daughters and about their sons?.
local: Niŋi n na, Leban yeli Yakubu, E pogiba ŋa niŋini m biisi, ka e biisi a gba mi niŋini m yaŋsi, buŋkobiri ŋa gba niŋini m bini, binseesi e nyaasira ŋa zaa niŋini m bini, ama pimparaŋa, wula ma lee n ya n niŋi m bipunsaa ni ba biisaa yela?.

eng: But Jacob stayed apart by himself, and a man wrestled with him until dawn broke.
local: A nyaaŋa, o ko pori n ti ba o ziizii maa, dindaari yiiŋu maa. Dabso ti mari u moora, halli bee ti kaa.

eng: The servant brought out gold and silver jewelry and clothing and gave them to Rebekah. To her brother and to her mother he gave the finest gifts.
local: Niŋi n na, o nyisi sugasi ni gbitisi pogisara bini, a ni bimpinna ti Rabeeka, ka ti o sunzodaba ni o ma piiniseesi n mari ligiri ga.

eng: Come, lets make a treaty, you and me, and let something be our witness.
local: A zu, m niŋini siri ta, m nini ti digili noori ti taaba, o niŋi toŋine ti sunsuuniŋi.

eng: When the man saw that he couldnt defeat Jacob, he grabbed Jacobs thigh and tore a muscle in Jacobs thigh as he wrestled with him.
local: Lala daba maa n nyaasi, ka o ki tee n nyaŋŋi Yakubu, o salli Yakubu kpiiligu, o kpiiligu maa furisi.

eng: He and the men with him ate and drank and spent the night. When they got up in the morning, the servant said, See me off to my master.
local: O n niŋi n ŋa baasi, o ni niseesi n daa n na la di, ka ŋu, ka do niŋi. A n kaa bee boguuniŋi, Ibrahim guruŋu maa suusi soori, Ya ti ma soori, m gaa m yidaana saani.

eng: So Jacob took a stone, set it up as a sacred pillar.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu naa kugiri sabi, a duligi nisaaniŋi.

eng: The man said, Let me go because the dawn is breaking. But Jacob said, I wont let you go until you bless me.
local: Niŋi n na, daba maa yeli Yakubu, Cheli m gaari, bee kaaya. Yakubu yeli u, M ki ti e gaa, ama e nafa ma ni.

eng: Her brother and mother said, Let the young woman stay with us not more than ten days, and after that she may go.
local: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka sunzodaba ni o ma yeli, Cheli Rabeeka o na ba kpe dapii poin, ka ya mari u wara.

eng: and said to his relatives, Gather stones. So they took stones, made a mound, and ate there near the mound.
local: O n niŋi n ŋa baasi, o yeli o nyaaŋa dima, ni ba laŋsi kuga pa taaba niŋi. Ba laŋsi a pa taaba niŋi, ka ba zi n begi kuga maa, a di.

eng: He said to Jacob, Whats your name? and he said, Jacob.
local: Daba maa boosi u. E yuuri? M yuuri n niŋi Yakubu.

eng: But he said to them, Dont delay me. The LORD has made my trip successful. See me off so that I can go to my master.
local: O yeli ba, Ya da laŋŋi ma nyaaŋa. Duuma ti ma ni zusuŋŋu, a yi m cheni ŋa puwa, a zu, ya basi ma soori m gaa m yidaana saani.

eng: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
local: Leban daa n digilini nisaaniŋi yuuri Jaga Sahaduta, ka Yakubu gba mi digili nisaaniŋi yuuri Galiidi.

eng: Then he said, Your name wont be Jacob any longer, but Israel, because you struggled with God and with men and won.
local: Niŋi n na, daba maa yeli u, Za zinaa wara, e yuuri bi lee n niŋi Yakubu, e yuuri n niŋi Izireel , a kpali bo? E ni Naawunni ni niriba n mo, e nyaŋŋi ba la zu.

eng: They said, Summon the young woman, and lets ask her opinion.
local: Niŋi n na, ba yeli, Cheli ti booli bipuŋŋu maa gbagba boosi u.

eng: Laban said, This mound is our witness today, and, therefore, he too named it Galeed.
local: Niŋi n na, Leban yeli Yakubu, Kuga ŋa n laŋsi n do ŋa n niŋi ti toŋine zinaa. A zu n na ba daa n digili niŋi Galiidi la.

eng: Jacob also asked and said, Tell me your name. But he said, Why do you ask for my name? and he blessed Jacob there.
local: Yakubu yeli u, Wusi ma e yuuri. Bo zu n na e kpabira e baŋŋi m yuuri? Ka nafa Yakubu.

eng: They called Rebekah and said to her, Will you go with this man? She said, I will go.
local: Ba booli Rabeeka boosi u, E ni paa dabaa gaari? O sagi Mm, m ni gaa.

eng: He also named it Mizpah, because he said, The LORD will observe both of us when we are separated from each other.
local: Leban gba yeli, Naawunni o nyaasira ti zaa jemanso ti n ya n barigi taaba ŋa. A zu, o yuyindaana n daa n niŋi Mizipa.

eng: Jacob named the place Peniel, because Ive seen God face-to-face, and my life has been saved.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli, M nye Naawunni nufu niŋi, ka ma na mari nyevuri. A zu, Yakubu digili niŋi yuuri Peniyel.

eng: So they sent off their sister Rebekah, her nurse, Abrahams servant, and his men.
local: A zu, ba sagi, ka ti Rabeeka ni o bipoga paa Ibrahim guruŋu la ni o niriba maa gaari.

eng: If you treat my daughters badly and if you marry other women, though we arent there, know that God observed our witness.
local: Leban yeli, Ka e si n namsira m biisaa, koo a naa poga paasi ba niŋi, halli ti n barigira taaba ŋa zaa, teesira maa Naawunni n nyaara ti.

eng: The sun rose as Jacob passed Penuel, limping because of his thigh.
local: Wuluŋu daa n pusini poin, ka Yakubu yi Peniyel, ka kpagsira o kpiiligu la niŋi.

eng: And they blessed Rebekah, saying to her, May you, our sister, become thousands of ten thousand; may your children possess their enemies cities.
local: Ba suusi Naawunni ti Rabeeka ka yeli, Sunzopoga, Naawunni n nafa i, ka ti e biisi wasi tusatusa, ka e dogim puu dima zaa ni nyaŋŋi ba dindima, a ni ba tiŋsi.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, Here is this mound and here is the sacred pillar that Ive set up for us.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, Leban lee n yeli Yakubu, Kuga ŋa ni laŋsi n do ŋa, a ni so m ni sabi a, n niŋi bunso n ba m nini sunsuuniŋi.

eng: Therefore, Israelites dont eat the tendon attached to the thigh muscle to this day, because he grabbed Jacobs thigh muscle at the tendon.
local: Halli a ti yinni zinni, n ŋa zu n na Izireel dima bi dira buŋkoo gberi tomu ziya, a kpali Naawunni ni daa n salli Yakubu kpiiligu la zu.

eng: Rebekah and her young women got up, mounted the camels, and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and left.
local: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka ni pogiseesi n pa o zu niŋi siriya du ba nyogimasi, a paa daba maa gaari.

eng: This mound and the sacred pillar are witnesses that I wont travel beyond this mound and that you wont travel beyond this mound and this pillar to do harm.
local: Kuga ŋa n za n ti ti naŋgbandigiligu maa toŋinesi. M ki gali ŋama zaa nyaaŋa, a namsi i, e mi gba e da pa ta, a ti namsi ma.

eng: Now Isaac had come from the region of Beer-lahai-roi and had settled in the arid southern plain.
local: Yisaaka daa n yini buluguso ba n boona nyevuri daana bulugu so n nyaara ma, tambisigu tiirigu, a wa ti ba Keenan kiŋkagili sazu Negef.

eng: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor will keep order between us. So Jacob gave his word in the name of the awesome one of his father Isaac.
local: Ibrahim ni Naho Naawunni la ni di ti seriya. Niŋi n na, Yakubu za Naawunni so, o ba Yisaaka ni daa tumina la, yuuri niŋi, a pe maa o ni pa ba naŋgbandigiligu maa niŋi.

eng: One evening, Isaac went out to inspect the pasture, and while staring he saw camels approaching.
local: Dindaso zaanoori, Yisaaka yi n we laŋgari, a wa ti poli n jinna, ka nye nyogimasi n chena.

eng: Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain, and invited his relatives to a meal. They ate together and spent the night on the mountain.
local: O ku buŋkoowa nyisi sariga, a yi zoori maa zu, ka booli o dogiriba zaa ba ti di. Ba n ti di n baasi, ba do zoori maa puwa.

eng: Rebekah stared at Isaac. She got down from the camel.
local: Rabeeka gba nyaasi, o nyeni Yisaaka, ka sigi nyogima maa niŋi.

eng: Laban got up early in the morning, kissed his sons and daughters, blessed them, and left to go back to his own place.
local: A n kaa bee, Leban nyisigi, a mugisi o yaŋsi, a ni o bipunsi la, ka nafa ba, ka pori n kuli o yiri.

eng: and said to the servant, Who is this man walking through the pasture to meet us? The servant said, Hes my master. So she took her headscarf and covered herself.
local: ka boosi guruŋu maa, Yasaani daba n ŋa, so n tuusira ti ŋa? Guruŋu maa yeli u, M yidaana n na. O naa o saata muuri o nufu.

eng: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
local: Ba n ti paa Yisaaka saani, guruŋu maa yeli u yaseesi zaa o ni wa ti niŋi.

eng: Isaac brought Rebekah into his mother Sarahs tent. He received Rebekah as his wife and loved her. So Isaac found comfort after his mothers death.
local: Yisaaka mari Rabeeka kpe o ma gbana sugiri puwa, a naag u loobi o poga, ka daa n kpabira u ga. O ma kum sufusaaŋu n daa mari u, sufupeelim pa ta.

eng: Dinah, the daughter whom Leah had borne to Jacob, went out to meet the women of that country.
local: Dindaso n na, Dayina, so n niŋi Yakubu ni Liya bipuŋŋu la, yi n gaa o taaba pogiba saani yi tiŋŋa maa puwa.

eng: When Shechem the son of the Hivite Hamor and the countrys prince saw her, he took her, slept with her, and humiliated her.
local: Niŋi n na, Sekem, Hamo so n niŋi Hivati nira, ka niŋi paaligu maa naaba biduwa, nye Dayina, ka gbaag u wa ti bibi u kpabi.

eng: He was drawn to Dinah, Jacobs daughter. He loved the young woman and tried to win her heart.
local: Ama, o n nye bipuŋŋu maa, o yela peri u ga, o togsi u kpabirisim yela, a kpabira maa o loobi o poga.

eng: Shechem said to his father Hamor, Get this girl for me as my wife.
local: Sekem yeli o ba Hamo, Naa bipuŋŋaa ti ma, o niŋi m poga.

eng: Now Jacob heard that Shechem defiled his daughter Dinah; but his sons were with the animals in the countryside, so he decided to keep quiet until they got back.
local: Yakubu n wumi maa ba saami o bii Dayina la, ka o biduusi na mari o buŋkobiri ba moopuwa, o na muuri yelli maa, a ti paa jemanso ba n ta yiri.

eng: Meanwhile, Hamor, Shechems father, went out to Jacob to speak with him.
local: Niŋi n na, Sekem ba Hamo wa ti togsi Yakubu saani.

eng: Just then, Jacobs sons got back from the countryside. When they heard what had happened, they were deeply offended and very angry, because Shechem had disgraced Israel by sleeping with Jacobs daughter. Such things are simply not done.
local: Yakubu biduusi n yi moo puu ta, ba wumi yaso n niŋi maa, sufusaaŋu ni suuri gbaa ba ga, seema Sekem n niŋi n ŋa ŋaaba yelli, a yi Yakubu biisi puwa, a kpabi Yakubu biya, yaso n di n bi daga o niŋi.

eng: Hamor said to them, My son Shechems heart is set on your daughter. Please let him marry her.
local: Hamo yeli Yakubu, M bii Sekem kpabira e bipuŋŋaa ga. Jaande, naag u ti u, o niŋi o poga.

eng: Arrange marriages with us: give us your daughters and take our daughters for yourselves.
local: Ti naagira taaba, ya ti ti naagira ya bipunsi, ka ya gba ya naagira ti bipunsi.

eng: Live with us. The land is available to you: settle down, travel through it, and buy property in it.
local: Ya ni tee n zi tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, ya niŋi bunso zaa ya n kpabira. Ya zi o niŋi, a dira yawu, a nye bindira yi o niŋi.

eng: Shechem said to Dinahs father and brothers, If you approve of me, tell me what you want, and I will give it to you.
local: Niŋi n na, Sekem yeli Dayina ba ni o sunzodabisi, Ya paasi m niŋi, ka bunso zaa ya n kpabira, m ni ti ya.

eng: Make the bride price and marriage gifts as large as you like, and I will pay whatever you tell me. Then let me marry the young woman.
local: Ya wusi ma samiso n di n yo poga maa zu, seema zaa ya n kpabira samini maa sagi, m ni yo. Ka ya ni sagi n naag u ti ma n na la.

eng: Jacobs sons responded deviously to Shechem and his father Hamor because Shechem defiled their sister Dinah.
local: A kpali ba n sagimi ba sunzopoga Dayina zu, Yakubu biduusi yomsi Sekem ni o ba Hamo.

eng: They said to them, We cant do this, allowing our sisters to marry uncircumcised men, because its disgraceful to us.
local: Ba yeli ba, Ti ki tee n niŋi lala, ti ki tee n naa ti sunzopoga ti daba so yoori n bi ŋma, o lala n niŋi ŋaaba ti ti.

eng: We can only agree to do this if you circumcise every male as we do.
local: Ti ni zaali ya zaaligu luŋko, a maa, ya ba seema ti ni be ŋa, dabsi zaa ba ŋma ba yoya.

eng: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters for ourselves. We will live with you and be one people.
local: Lala wune, ti ni naa ti bipunsi tira ya, ka ti gba ti naagira ya bipunsi. Ti zi ya puwa, ti ni ya zaa ti loobi luŋko.

eng: But if you dont listen to us and become circumcised, we will take our daughter and leave.
local: Ama, ka ya yi bi sagi maa, ya ni ŋma ya yoya wune, ti ni mari ti sunzopoga gaari.

eng: Their idea seemed like a good one to Hamor and Hamors son Shechem.
local: Bunso ba n yeli maa ŋmaani yasuŋŋu ti Hamo ni o bii Sekem.

eng: The young man didnt waste any time doing this because he liked Jacobs daughter so much. He was more respected than anyone else in his fathers household.
local: Bipollaa, so n niŋi jilima zaa daana, a daga, a yi o ba laanta, o bi lee n mari yela maa seema ba n yeli maa yuu, a kpali, o daa n kpabira Yakubu bipuŋŋu maa ga.

eng: Hamor and his son Shechem went to their citys gate and spoke to the men of their city:.
local: A zu, Hamo ni o bii Sekem yi n gaa tiŋŋa maa voori noori, seela ba n laŋsira, ba wa ti togsi n ti tiŋŋa maa dima.

eng: These men want peace with us. Let them live in the land and travel through it; theres plenty of land for them. We will marry their daughters and give them our daughters.
local: Ba yeli ba, Niribaa niŋini nivumansi ti ti, ya cheli ti ti ba zi tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, ba dira yawu, zii maa yeligiya ba n di n zi, ti ni tee n naagira ba bipunsi, ka ba gba ba naagira ti bipunsi.

eng: But the men will agree to live with us and become one people only if we circumcise every male just as they do.
local: Niriba maa ni sagi ti ni ba ti zi maa nivu luŋko, ka ti sagiya maa ti ni ŋma ti yoya, a maa barima.

eng: Their livestock, their property, and all of their animals—wont they be ours? Lets agree with them and let them live with us.
local: Ba buŋguuna ŋa ni ba bindaalim binaa zaa ki loobi ti bini? A zu, ya ti ti wumi n ti ba, ka ti ni ba ti zi.

eng: Everyone at the city gate agreed with Hamor and his son Shechem, so every able-bodied male in the city was circumcised.
local: Niseesi zaa ni laŋsi maa wumi n ti Hamo ni o bii Sekem, ka daba zaa n ba tiŋŋa maa puu ŋma ba yoya.

eng: On the third day, when they were still in pain, two of Jacobs sons and Dinahs brothers Simeon and Levi took their swords, came into the city, which suspected nothing, and killed every male.
local: Dabaa ata nyaaŋa, jemanso niriba maa zaa ni ba beerim niŋi, Yakubu biduusi bayi, Simiyon ni Livai, Dayina sunzodabisi naa ba tokobisi kpe tiŋŋa maa puwa, seema niriba maa ni bi fe, a ku ba dabsi zaa nyem.

eng: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with their swords, took Dinah from Shechems household, and left.
local: Ba ku Hamo ni o bii Sekem, ka kpe Sekem dii mari Dayina yi n gaa.

eng: When Jacobs other sons discovered the dead, they looted the city that had defiled their sister.
local: Niriba maa kuubu nyaaŋa, Yakubu biduseesi n che la kpe tiŋŋa maa puu nye kpiinsi maa, ka kaasi ba kapete bini, a kpali ba n sagimi ba sunzopoga la zu.

eng: They took their flocks, their cattle, and their donkeys, whether in the city or in the fields nearby.
local: Ba gbaa ba peeri, buusi, naagi, a ni buŋsi ni bunso zaa n ba tiŋŋa maa puwa, a ni ba puuri niŋi.

eng: They carried off their property, their children, and their wives. They looted the entire place.
local: Ba naa ba bindaalim bini zaa, a ni ba pogiba ni biisi, a kaasi ba diisi puu bini zaa, a di ba afuwa.

eng: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Youve put me in danger by making me offensive to those who live here in the land, to the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I have only a few men. They may join forces, attack me, and destroy me, me and my household.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli Simiyon ni Livai, Ya kpabi yela ti ma, ya ti ma yubee yi niseesi n ba tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, Keenan dima ni Periziti dima balisaa. Ti bi zoo, ba wune si n laŋsi taaba, ba ni ku ma, m ni m laanta zaa ni muri.

eng: They said, But didnt he treat our sister like a prostitute.
local: Ba yeli u, Seema o n niŋi ti sunzopogaa, a maa jagimilo ŋa, o veela.

eng: God said to Jacob, Get up, go to Bethel, and live there. Build an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you ran away from your brother Esau.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli Yakubu Nyisigi n gaa Betel wa ti zi, ka me sariga daaŋu ti mani Naawunni, so n daa n ta e saani, ka e daa n kpali e mabii Isawu zu zora la.

eng: Jacob said to his household and to everyone who was with him, Get rid of the foreign gods you have with you. Clean yourselves and change your clothes.
local: Yakubu yeli o laanta dima zaa, Ya naa ya buga zaa basi, ka niŋi ya maŋsi nyem, a cherigi ya bimpinna.

eng: Then lets rise and go up to Bethel so that I can build an altar there to the God who answered me when I was in trouble and who has been with me wherever Ive gone.
local: Ka ya ta ti gaa Betel. Nisaaniŋi n na, ma ya n me sariga daaŋu ti Naawunni, so n daa n wumi m suusigu, jemanso ni daa n ba sufusaaŋu niŋi, ka ba m saani seela zaa ni wara.

eng: So they gave Jacob all of the foreign gods they had, as well as the rings in their ears, and Jacob buried them under the terebinth at Shechem.
local: A zu, ba naa ba buga ni ba kundesi zaa ti Yakubu, o pi a, a yi tikarigu gbinni, a leeli Sekem.

eng: When they set out, God made all of the surrounding cities fearful so that they didnt pursue Jacobs sons.
local: Niŋi n na, ba yi soori, Naawunni ti dabeem gbaa tiŋseesi n poli n jili ba maa zaa, lala zu, nira zaa bi kari n pa ba zu.

eng: Jacob and all of the people with him arrived in Luz, otherwise known as Bethel, in the land of Canaan.
local: Yakubu ni o niriba maa zaa ta Luzi , a yi Keenan dima tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

eng: He built an altar there and named the place El-bethel, because God had revealed himself to him there when he ran away from his brother.
local: Nisaaniŋi n na, o me sariga daaŋu, ka digili u El-Betel, a kpali, nisaaniŋi n na Naawunni daa n nyisi o maŋŋa wusi u jemanso o n daa n kpali o beeri zu zora la.

eng: Rebekahs nurse Deborah died and was buried at Bethel under the oak, and Jacob named it Allon-bacuth.
local: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka bipoga Debora kpi, ba pi u tikarigu gbinni, a yi Betel falimini puwa. A zu, ba boona nisaaniŋi Kumsi Tiya.

eng: God appeared to Jacob again, while he was on his way back from Paddan-aram, and blessed him.
local: Yakubu n daa n pori n yi Mesopoteemiya ta la, Naawunni lee n maali n nyisi o maŋŋa wusi u, ka nafa u.

eng: God said to him, Your name is Jacob, but your name will be Jacob no longer. No, your name will be Israel. And he named him Israel.
local: Naawunni yeli u, E yuuri n niŋi Yakubu, ama, ba ki lee n boona i Yakubu, pimparaŋa ba ni boona i Izireel.

eng: God said to him, I am El Shaddai. Be fertile and multiply. A nation, even a large group of nations, will come from you; kings will descend from your own children.
local: Naawunni yeli u, Mani n niŋi Naawunni, kpeeŋu zaa daana, dogi n wasi n paali ziya. E baligaa niŋi n na balidamta ni nadima ya n yi.

eng: The land I gave to Abraham and to Isaac, I give to you; and I will give the land to your descendants after you.
local: Tiŋgbaŋaa m ni daa n naa n ti Ibrahim ni Yisaaka la, m naag u ti i, ka m ni naag u ti e yaŋsi.

eng: Then God ascended, leaving him alone in the place where he spoke to him.
local: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yi seela o n togsi o saani maa gaa saazu.

eng: So Jacob set up a sacred pillar, a stone pillar, at the place God spoke to him. He poured an offering of wine on it and then poured oil over it.
local: Yakubu naa kugiri sabi seela Naawunni n togsi o saani la ti u, ka kpaa buŋŋuura sariga pa o niŋi, ka lee n kpaa kpaam pa o niŋi.

eng: Jacob named the place Bethel where God spoke to him.
local: Yakubu digili seela Naawunni n daa n togsi o saani la yuuri Betel.

eng: They left Bethel, and when they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel went into hard labor.
local: Yakubu ni o nyaaŋa dima n daa n yi Betel wara Efirata, ka na bi leeli. Niŋi n na, dogim paa Rachel, dogim maa mi daa n ba toom ti u.

eng: During her difficult labor, the midwife said to her, Dont be afraid. You have another son.
local: O dogim maa n daa ba toom maa zu, pogiso n deegira dogim maa yeli u, Rachel, da zora dabeem, biduu n na e lee n nye.

eng: As her life faded away, just before she died, she named him Ben-oni, but his father named him Benjamin.
local: O n digili bii maa yuuri Benoni, o vuusini o baasigu vuusim, ka kpi. Ama bii maa ba digili o yuuri Benjamin .

eng: Rachel died and was buried near the road to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
local: A zu, Rachel daa n kpini yi Efirata soori, ba pi u sogbarimini. .

eng: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. Its the pillar on Rachels tomb thats still there today.
local: Yakubu daa n naa kugiri sabi o kaa zu, a ti yini zinni, kugiri maa na sabi Rachel kaa maa zu.

eng: Israel continued his trip and pitched his tent farther on near the tower of Eder.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu lee n maali n nyisigi nisaaniŋi naag o gbana suga wa ti gali Migdal-Eda, a sabi.

eng: While Israel stayed in that place, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah his fathers secondary wife, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons.
local: Jemanso Yakubu n daa n zi nisaaniŋi paaligu maa n na, Rubin kpabi o ba pogibila Biliha, o ba n wumi lala yelli maa, o suuri nyisigi. Yakubu biduusi pii ni bayi yela n ŋa.

eng: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacobs oldest son, and Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
local: Liya biisi n ŋa: Rubin, Yakubu bikpeema, Simiyon, Livai, Juuda, Isaka, a ni Zebulun.

eng: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
local: Rachel biisi n ŋa: Yisifu ni Benjamin.

eng: The sons of Bilhah, Rachels servant, were Dan and Naphtali.
local: Rachel bipoga Biliha biisi n ŋa: Dan ni Nafitali.

eng: The sons of Zilpah, Leahs servant, were Gad and Asher. These were Jacobs sons born to him in Paddan-aram.
local: Liya bipoga Zilipa biisi n ŋa: Gadi ni Asa. Yakubu biisi n na la, seesi ba n daa dogi, a yi Mesopoteemiya.

eng: Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, that is, Kiriath-arba. This is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac lived as immigrants.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu pori n gaa o ba Yisaaka saani, a yi Mamiri, a leeli Kiriyaf-Aba seela Ibrahim ni Yisaaka n daa n be.

eng: At the age of years.
local: Yisaaka daa n dini yuma koowa ni pisinii.

eng: Isaac took his last breath and died. He was buried with his ancestors after a long, satisfying life. His sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
local: Niŋi n na, o vuusi o baasigu vuusim, a ka niŋi, a gaari o yaadima saani, ka o kurigiya nene. O biisi Isawu ni Yakubu pi u.

eng: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan where his father was an immigrant.
local: Yakubu daa n zinni Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, seela o ba n daa n zi, a niŋi saana la.

eng: This is the account of Jacobs descendants. Joseph was years old and tended the flock with his brothers. While he was helping the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his fathers wives, Joseph told their father unflattering things about them.
local: Yakubu ni o biisi yela n ŋa. Yisifu yuma pii ni ayapoin bipolli. O ni o beseesi n niŋi Biliha ni Zilipa biisi, o ba pogiba, nyaasira peeri ni buusi. Yisifu ti ti o ba baaru dagiri yi ba pelli.

eng: Now Israel loved Joseph more than any of his other sons because he was born when Jacob was old. Jacob had made for him a long robe.
local: Yakubu kpabira Yisifu ga, a gaari o biisi maa zaa, a kpali, o kurigirisim jemani n na o dogi u, o se tagite wogili so n veela ga ti u.

eng: When his brothers saw that their father loved him more than any of his brothers, they hated him and couldnt even talk nicely to him.
local: Jemanso o beya n nye maa ba ba kpabira u gaari ba, ba chisi u, ka bi togsira o saani kpabirisim yatoga.

eng: Joseph had a dream and told it to his brothers, which made them hate him even more.
local: Dindaso, Yisifu zaasi zaasim, ka togsi o yela ti o beya maa, ba chisi u ga paasi.

eng: He said to them, Listen to this dream I had.
local: O yeli ba, Ya wumi m zaasimaa.

eng: When we were binding stalks of grain in the field, my stalk got up and stood upright, while your stalks gathered around it and bowed down to my stalk.
local: Ti zaa ba ni pu, a leera chi kapaasi, noba maa niŋi, m toori nyisigi n damsi n za, ka ya bini mi ti poli n jili u, ka dobi u tiŋŋa.

eng: His brothers said to him, Will you really be our king and rule over us? So they hated him even more because of the dreams he told them.
local: O beya maa boosi u, E wusini maa, e ni niŋi ti naaba, ka nyaasira ti? Ba chisi u ga paasi, kpali o zaasim la ni seema o n yeli la zu.

eng: Then Joseph had another dream and described it to his brothers: Ive just dreamed again, and this time the sun and the moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.
local: O lee n maali n zaasi zaasim, ka yeli o beya, Ya wumi, m lee n zaasini zaasim, ŋala wune, wuluŋu, ŋmeriga, a ni ŋmeribiisi pii ni yinni n ti dobi ma tiŋŋa.

eng: When he described it to his father and brothers, his father scolded him and said to him, What kind of dreams have you dreamed? Am I and your mother and your brothers supposed to come and bow down to the ground in front of you.
local: O n poli n wusi o ba seema o n yeli o beya la, o ba tasi u, ka boosi u, Bo zaasim n na e zaasi lala? Yamonsitiri, m ni e ma ni e beya ŋa zaa ni ti dobi i tiŋŋa.

eng: His brothers were jealous of him, but his father took careful note of the matter.
local: O yela daa n marini o beya la pubeerim, ama o ba wune daa n naani yela maa niŋi o fere niŋi.

eng: Josephs brothers went to tend their fathers flocks near Shechem.
local: Dindaso Yisifu beya daa gaa Sekem, a wa ti nyaasira ba ba buŋkobiri.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, Arent your brothers tending the sheep near Shechem? Come, Ill send you to them. And he said, Im ready.
local: O ba Yakubu daa n booli u yeli, E beya ba Sekem, a nyaasira buŋkobiri, m ni tumi i ba saani. Yisifu sagi, To.

eng: Jacob said to him, Go! Find out how your brothers are and how the flock is, and report back to me. So Jacob sent him from the Hebron Valley. When he approached Shechem.
local: Gaa n wa ti nyaasi e beya maa alaanfiya, a ni buŋkobiri maa ni ba seem, ka pori n ti yeli ma bunso n ba niŋi. O ba tumi u, o yi Hebiron nantaŋŋu puu chaŋŋi n we Sekem.

eng: a man found him wandering in the field and asked him, What are you looking for.
local: O n daa n paa, ka chaŋŋi n kpabira o beya maa yi moo maa puwa, dabso daa nye u, ka boosi u, Bo n na e kpabira kpe.

eng: Joseph said, Im looking for my brothers. Tell me, where are they tending the sheep.
local: Yisifu yeli u, M beya n ta kpe, jaande, ka e baŋŋi seela ba ni ba buŋkobiri ni be, e wusi ma.

eng: The man said, They left here. I heard them saying, Lets go to Dothan. So Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.
local: Daba maa yeli u, Ba lee n ka kpe, m daa wumi ba n yetira taaba, ni ba gaa Dodan tiŋŋa. Niŋi n na, Yisifu kari ba bobiri gaa Dodan tiŋŋa maa, a paa ba niŋi.

eng: They saw Joseph in the distance before he got close to them, and they plotted to kill him.
local: O daa na bani wogila chena, ka o beya maa nye o n chena ba saani. Ba niŋi naŋgbanluŋko maa, ka o yi ta, ba ku u.

eng: The brothers said to each other, Here comes the big dreamer.
local: O n chena maa, ba yeli taaba, Zaasim zaasira la n chena la.

eng: Come on now, lets kill him and throw him into one of the cisterns, and well say a wild animal devoured him. Then we will see what becomes of his dreams.
local: Ya cheli ti ku u lobi n niŋi bulukuuma ŋa yindaana niŋi, ka ti wa ti yeli moo puu buŋ n gbaag u, ka ti nye bunso n lee n ya n yi o zaasim niŋi.

eng: When Reuben heard what they said, he saved him from them, telling them, Lets not take his life.
local: Rubin n wumi n ŋa, o niŋi aniya, seema o n di n niŋi n moligi biyaa yi o beya ŋa saani. Ka yeli ba, Ti da ku u.

eng: Reuben said to them, Dont spill his blood! Throw him into this desert cistern, but dont lay a hand on him. He intended to save Joseph from them and take him back to his father.
local: Ya da kpaag o zeem. Ti naag u lobi n niŋi bulukuuŋaa yindaana niŋi, moogaa puwa, ka da siisi u. O daa n yelini n ŋa, lala jemani o ni nye daama moligi u, ka mari u wa ti ti o ba.

eng: When Joseph reached his brothers, they stripped off Josephs long robe.
local: Yisifu n daa n ti paa o beya maa saani, ba gbaag u, ka pirigi o tagite wogiliso, o n daa n pili maa.

eng: took him, and threw him into the cistern, an empty cistern with no water in it.
local: Ba naag u lobi n niŋi bulugu so koom n ka niŋi.

eng: When they sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with camels carrying sweet resin, medicinal resin, and fragrant resin on their way down to Egypt.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, ba zi tiŋŋa ba di bindira, ka nye Isimeel baligu yawdiriba seesi n yi Giliyadi, a wara Yijipiti. Yawdiriba ŋa nyogimasi daa n zinni tulalesi, dugisi, a ni buŋŋusa baligu baligu.

eng: Judah said to his brothers, What do we gain if we kill our brother and hide his blood?.
local: Juuda boosi o mabiisi maa, Bo tono n na ti ya n nye, ka ti ku ti sunzo ŋa, ka naag o yela sogi?.

eng: Come on, lets sell him to the Ishmaelites. Lets not harm him because hes our brother; hes family. His brothers agreed.
local: Ti koosi u ti Isimeel dimaa, o so ti n ku u, koo, a niŋi u daaŋu. Ti sunzo n na, ka ti niŋi zeem luŋko. Niŋi n na, o taaba maa wumi seema o n yeli maa.

eng: When some Midianite traders passed by, they pulled Joseph up out of the cistern. They sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they brought Joseph to Egypt.
local: Jemanso Isimeel yawdiriba ŋa n ya n ti gaari, o beya maa darigi u nyisi bulukuuŋu la puwa, a koosi u gbitisi pisiyi ti ba, ba mari u gaari Yijipiti.

eng: When Reuben returned to the cistern and found that Joseph wasnt in it, he tore his clothes.
local: Niŋi n na, Rubin daa n lee n pori ta bulugu maa ziya, ka bi nye Yisifu yi o puwa, sufusaaŋu gbaag u, o darigi o bimpinna kasigi.

eng: Then he returned to his brothers and said, The boys gone! And I—where can I go now.
local: Ka we o sunzoba la saani, a wa ti boosi ba, Biyaa lee n ka bulugu maa puwa? Wula n na ma ya n niŋi.

eng: His brothers took Josephs robe, slaughtered a male goat, and dipped the robe in the blood.
local: A zu, ba gbaa buu korigi, a naag o zeem sariga Yisifu tagite wogili maa.

eng: They took the long robe, brought it to their father, and said, We found this. See if its your sons robe or not.
local: ka naag u kuli, a wa ti wusi ba ba, ka boosi u, Nyaasi, bunso ti ni nye, e bii la tagite n ŋa? Koo ka wune n na.

eng: He recognized it and said, Its my sons robe! A wild animal has devoured him. Joseph must have been torn to pieces.
local: O nyaasi u, ka baŋŋi u, ka sagi, Mm, m bii maa tagite n na, ama moo puu buŋ n gbaag u toog u ŋobi.

eng: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put a simple mourning cloth around his waist, and mourned for his son for many days.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu darigi o bimpinna kasigi, ka naa sufusaaŋu bimpinna pili, a kumi o bii maa kuuri ga.

eng: All of his sons and daughters got up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, telling them, Ill go to my grave mourning for my son. And Josephs father wept for him.
local: O biduusi ni bipunsi zaa daa n ti maagira o sufu, o zagsi, ka yeli ba, M ni sufusaaŋu ni kumsi n ni gaa kaa puwa, a zu, o ba kpali o zu kumina.

eng: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold Joseph to the Egyptians, to Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, commander of the royal guard.
local: Jemanso yawdiriba la n daa n da Yisifu maa, ba mari u gaa Yijipiti paaligu, a koosi u ti daba yindaana, ba n boona Potifa. Dabaa daa n niŋini Yijipiti paaligu zaa naaba guuriba la kpeema.

eng: Some time later, both the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king offended their master, the king of Egypt.
local: Jemandamta n gaari, niso n yi n tira Yijipiti paaligu naaba buŋŋuura, a ni so n niŋina o paanu n daa niŋi n kpe ba yidaana niŋi.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Naaba maa je suuri niŋi ba.

eng: When Joseph had been taken down to Egypt, Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, the commander of the royal guard and an Egyptian, purchased him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
local: Isimeel baligu dima n daa da Yisifu la, ba mari u gaa Yijipiti paaligu, a koosi u ti dabso ba n boona Potifa. Dabaa daa n niŋini Yijipiti paaligu zaa naaba guuriba la kpeema.

eng: and he put them under arrest with the commander of the royal guard in the same jail where Joseph was imprisoned.
local: o ti ba yo ba dii, seela gbagba ba n daa yo Yisifu la, a yi nayiri, seela soojasi kpeema n nyaasira naaba maa la n be.

eng: The LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man and served in his Egyptian masters household.
local: Duuma daa n bani Yisifu saani, o yela wara yiya, o ba o yidaandaba Yijipiti nira maa laanta.

eng: The commander of the royal guard assigned Joseph to assist them. After they had been under arrest for some time.
local: Soojasi kpeema maa daa naa ba pobi Yisifu nuwa, ni o nyaasira ba.

eng: His master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD made everything he did successful.
local: O yidaandaba nye maa Duuma ba ni o saani, ka bunso zaa o n niŋina, Duuma tira u nyaama.

eng: both of them—the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king who were imprisoned in the jail—had dreams one night, and each mans dream had its own meaning.
local: Niriba bayi ŋa, Yijipiti naaba n daa ti ba yo ba dii maa, daa n zaasi zaasima, ka zaasima ayi ŋa mi zaa gbina kuri.

eng: Potiphar thought highly of Joseph, and Joseph became his assistant; he appointed Joseph head of his household and put everything he had under Josephs supervision.
local: Yisifu yela paali o nufu, o naag u zaali wune Potifa zaa ziya, o naag o laanta ni bunso zaa nyem pobi o nuusi.

eng: Two years later, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing near the Nile.
local: Yuma ayi nyaaŋa, Yijipiti paaligu naaba maa zaasi zaasim, ni o n za Naayel mogiri naŋgbanni.

eng: When Joseph met them in the morning, he saw that they were upset.
local: Yisifu nyisigi n we ba saani boguuniŋi, a wa ti nye ba, o baŋŋi ba n mari sufusaaŋu.

eng: From the time he appointed Joseph head of his household and of everything he had, the LORD blessed the Egyptians household because of Joseph. The LORD blessed everything he had, both in the household and in the field.
