local: Jemanso o n naag o laanta ni o bunso zaa pobi o nuusi la, Duuma nafa Yijipiti nira maa laanta maa zaa, a kpali Yisifu zu. Nafa maa daa n ba ni Potifa bunso zaa niŋi, o laanta ni o pu zaa.

eng: In front of him, seven healthy-looking, fattened cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: Nagikara ayapoin n karigi n nyelisira yi mogiri maa puwa du n za, a ŋobira mogiri maa naŋgban moori.

eng: He asked the officers of Pharaoh who were under arrest with him in his masters house, Why do you look so distressed today.
local: Niŋi n na, o boosi ba, Bo n mari ya, ya dugi nufusi zinaa lala.

eng: So he handed over everything he had to Joseph and didnt pay attention to anything except the food he ate. Now Joseph was well-built and handsome.
local: A zu, o naa bunso zaa o n mari ti Yisifu nyaasira, o n si n ti Yisifu nyaasira a, kpeemaa bi lee n niŋina sen zaa, ama bindira dirisim ko. Yisifu daa n meya nene, ka veela nachiima.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, terrible-looking and scrawny, climbed up out of the Nile after them and stood beside them on the bank of the Nile.
local: A nyaaŋa, nagikogiri ayapoin n kogi n dagi gba yi mogiri maa puu du n ti za n begi ŋarima la.

eng: They answered, Weve both had dreams, but theres no one to interpret them. Joseph said to them, Dont interpretations belong to God? Describe your dreams to me.
local: Ba yeli u, Ti si zaasini zaasima, ti mi bi nye nira o wusi ti a gbina. Yisifu yeli ba, Zaasima gbina baŋinisim zaa bani Naawunni saani. Ya yeli ma ya zaasima maa.

eng: Some time later, his masters wife became attracted to Joseph and said, Sleep with me.
local: A n ti paa jemani yinni, o yidaana poga nyaasi u, ka yeli u, Ti mari ma do.

eng: The terrible-looking, scrawny cows devoured the seven healthy-looking, fattened cows. Then Pharaoh woke up.
local: Nagiseesi n kogi la naa nagiseesi n karigi n nyelisira la vali. Niŋi n na, naaba maa yirimi n yoo.

eng: The chief wine steward described his dream to Joseph: In my dream there was a vine right in front of me.
local: Niŋi n na, buŋŋuura nyaasira kpeema la yeli Yisifu o zaasim. M zaasim puwa, m nyeni tiso wala ba n ŋmemsi n niŋina daam la n za n yiya.

eng: He refused and said to his masters wife, With me here, my master doesnt pay attention to anything in his household; hes put everything he has under my supervision.
local: O zagsi, ka yeli u, Nyaasi, m yidaana bi lee n niŋina bunso zaa yiri a puwa, a kpali bunso zaag o n mari, o naani a zaa pobi m nuusi.

eng: He went back to sleep and had a second dream, in which seven ears of grain, full and healthy, grew on a single stalk.
local: O lee n gbisira, ka zaasi, chi zu karasuma ayapoin n tomi gbinluŋko niŋi.

eng: and on the vine were three branches. When it budded, its blossoms appeared, and its clusters ripened into grapes.
local: Wula ata n ba tii maa niŋi, ka o bosi vaari, a puumi, a wali wala, halli, a moo.

eng: No one is greater than I am in this household, and he hasnt denied me anything except you, since you are his wife. How could I do this terrible thing and sin against God.
local: Nira zaa n bi lee n gaari ma laanta ŋa puwa. Bunso zaa n ka laanta ŋa puwa, ka m yidaana bi naa n pobi m nuwa, ama, e ko, a kpali, o poga n niŋi i. Wula n na ma ya n niŋi yadagaa, ka niŋi yabee kpe Naawunni niŋi..

eng: Just then, seven ears of grain, scrawny and scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: A nyaaŋa, chi zu nyogirinyogirisi ayapoin, seesi wuluŋpusigu sauwa n pemsi n kuu gba yi.

eng: Pharaohs cup was in my hand, so I took the grapes, crushed them into Pharaohs cup, and put the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: Lala jemani, ka naaba maa kobee ba m nuusi puwa, ma po tii maa wala ŋmemsi a niŋi kobee la niŋi ti u.

eng: Every single day she tried to convince him, but he wouldnt agree to sleep with her or even to be with her.
local: Bee zaa, o yetirini Yisifu ni o mari u do, o mi zagsi, ka bi kpabira o kpatira u.

eng: and the scrawny ears swallowed up the full and well-formed ears. Then Pharaoh woke up and realized it was a dream.
local: Bin nyogirinyogirisi la naa bunsuma ayapoin la vali. Naaba maa lee n yirimi n yoo, ka zaasim n na.

eng: Joseph said to him, This is the dreams interpretation: The three branches are three days.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli u, E zaasim maa gbinni n ŋa, tiwula ata la, niŋini dabaata.

eng: One day when Joseph arrived at the house to do his work, none of the households men were there.
local: Dindaso, o kpe laanta, o tumi o tuma, ka yiri maa puu gurunsi maa yindaana zaa ka niŋi.

eng: In the morning, he was disturbed and summoned all of Egypts religious experts and all of its advisors. Pharaoh described his dreams to them, but they couldnt interpret them for Pharaoh.
local: A boguuniŋi, sufusaaŋu gbaa u ga, o tumi n booli Yijipiti yambaŋiniba ni dibodibo niŋiniba. Naaba maa poli o zaasima maa wusi ba, ama, ba yindaana zaa bi baŋŋi a gbina yeli u.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and return you to your position. You will put Pharaohs cup in his hand, just the way things were before when you were his wine steward.
local: Dabaata sunsuuniŋi, naaba maa ni nyisi i yi diyoorigaa puwa, a we seela e n daa n be, a matira o kobee la.

eng: She grabbed his garment, saying, Lie down with me. But he left his garment in her hands and ran outside.
local: Poga maa ti gbaa Yisifu tagite, ka yeli u, Ti mari ma do. O chee n yi dii maa puwa, ka che tagite maa pobi o nuusi.

eng: Then the chief wine steward spoke to Pharaoh: Today Ive just remembered my mistake.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa buŋŋuura nyaasira kpeema la yeli u, Zinni, m teesi seema ni bi niŋi n nene.

eng: But please, remember me when you are doing well and be loyal to me. Put in a good word for me to Pharaoh, so he sets me free from this prison.
local: Ama, m suusirini, ka yela ti maali n ti i, e teesi m yela, ka e yeli naaba maa o nyisi ma yi diyoorigaa puwa.

eng: When she realized that he had left his garment in her hands and run outside.
local: Poga maa n nye seema o n pirigi o tagite basi u, ka yi n chee la.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me and the chief baker under arrest with the commander of the royal guard.
local: Dindaso, e n daa n je suuri niŋi m ni niso n niŋina e paanu, ka naa ti wa ti yo dii, a yi soojasi kpeema so n nyaasira i la laanta la.

eng: I was stolen from the land of the Hebrews, and here too Ive done nothing to be thrown into this dungeon.
local: A kpali bo, ba daa n gbaa mani kpeeŋtiri yi Hiburu dima paaligu. M ni lee n ta kpe ŋa gbaa, m bi niŋi yaso n daga ba yo ma dii.

eng: she summoned the men of her house and said to them, Look, my husband brought us a Hebrew to ridicule us. He came to me to lie down with me, but I screamed.
local: o booli o laanta maa gurunsi, a ti yeli ba, Ya nyaasi, Hiburu biyaa m sira n ma n ta ŋa nyaasira tini jaga, o ya n ti puri ma ni, ma chaari kpeeŋu.

eng: We both dreamed one night, he and I, and each of our dreams had its own interpretation.
local: Ti bayi maa zaa zaasi zaasima yiiŋluŋko, ka a mari gbina, a kuri.

eng: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, It was the same for me. In my dream, there were three baskets of white bread on my head.
local: Paanu niŋina la ni nye seema Yisifu ni wusi o todaana la zaasim gbinni meesimtiri, o yeli Yisifu, M gba zaasini zaasim, a nye m ni zi paanusi peeri ata.

eng: When he heard me raise my voice and scream, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Jemanso o n wumi ni chaari kpeeŋtiri, a kpabira faara la, o chee n yi, ka che o tagite ŋa basi.

eng: A young Hebrew man, a servant of the commander of the royal guard, was with us. We described our dreams to him, and he interpreted our dreams for us, giving us an interpretation for each dream.
local: Hiburu bipolli yindaana daa n paasi ti niŋi, ka niŋi soojasi kpeema la guruŋu. Ti poli ti zaasima maa wusi u, o wusi ti so zaa o zaasim gbinni.

eng: In the basket on top there were baked goods for Pharaohs food, but birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.
local: Peso n ba saa la, naaba maa bindira seera baligu baligu ba n naa paanu zum maali, a tomini m zu, ka niinsi ti dira a.

eng: His interpretations came true exactly: Pharaoh restored me to my position but hanged him.
local: Yela maa niŋi seema gbagba o n wusi ti a gbina maa, ba mari ma pori n ta m tuma niŋi, ka yagili m to maa daana o kpi.

eng: She kept his garment with her until Josephs master came home.
local: O naa tagite maa zaali n guri Yisifu yidaandaba o ta.

eng: Joseph responded, This is the dreams interpretation: The three baskets are three days.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli u, E zaasim maa gbinni n ŋa, peeri ata la niŋini dabaata.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Joseph, and they quickly brought him from the dungeon. He shaved, changed clothes, and appeared before Pharaoh.
local: A zu, naaba maa tumi, ni ba wa ti mari Yisifu ta, noba maa niŋi. Ba wa ti nyisi Yisifu yi diyoorigu maa puwa. Yisifu puni o zu, ka cherigi o bimpinna, ka ta naaba maa saani.

eng: and she told him the same thing: The Hebrew slave whom you brought to us, to ridicule me, came to me;.
local: O yeli o sira, Hiburu bii la e n ma n ta la, ya n ti puri ma ni.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and will hang you from a tree where birds will peck your flesh from you.
local: Dabaata sunsuuniŋi, naaba maa ni ti ba nyisi i yi kpe, a wa ti ti ba ŋma e zu, ka le i yagili tii zu, niinsi, a ti dira i.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I had a dream, but no one could interpret it. Then I heard that when you hear a dream, you can interpret it.
local: Naaba maa yeli Yisifu, M zaasini m zaasim, nira zaa mi bi baŋŋi o gbinni. Ama m wumiya maa, ka e n si n wumi zaasim, e ni baŋŋi o gbinni.

eng: but when I raised my voice and screamed, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Ama, ni si n chaari kpeeŋutiri, o naag o tagite basi, ka chee n yi.

eng: The third day was Pharaohs birthday, and he gave a party for all of his servants. Before all of his servants, he gave an audience to the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Dabaata nyaaŋa naaba maa dogim daa n na, o laŋsi o tumtuminiba, ba ti di ba noori. Niŋi n na, o nyisi o kobee matira kpeema, a ni o paanu niŋina kpeema la, a yi diyoorigu puu ta tumtuminiba maa zaa yiya.

eng: Joseph answered Pharaoh, Its not me. God will give Pharaoh a favorable response.
local: Yisifu yeli naaba maa, M ki tee n wusi o gbinni, ama Naawunni n ni tee n wusi i o gbinni.

eng: When Josephs master heard the thing that his wife told him, This is what your servant did to me, he was incensed.
local: O yidaandaba n wumi seema o poga n yeli u, E guruŋu maa n niŋi ma seem n na la, o suuri nyisigi u ga.

eng: He returned the chief wine steward to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: O pori so n daa n nyaasira o buŋŋuura la gaa seela o n daa be, a tumina, o lee n naa naaba maa kobee ni o buŋŋuura tira u.

eng: So Pharaoh said to Joseph, In my dream I was standing on the bank of the Nile.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa yeli Yisifu, M zaasini, m ni za Naayel mogiri naŋgbanni.

eng: Josephs master took him and threw him in jail, the place where the kings prisoners were held. While he was in jail.
local: Yisifu yidaana naag u wa ti niŋi diyoorigu puwa, seela naaba n yoora o niriba, Yisifu daa bani diyoorigu maa puu gba.

eng: But the chief baker he hanged, just as Joseph had said would happen when he interpreted their dreams for them.
local: Ama, paanu daana la wune, o daa n tini ba ku u yagili tii zu, a maa seema Yisifu n daa yeli u la.

eng: In front of me, seven fattened, stout cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: ka nye nagikarisuma ayapoin n yi koom maa puu du n za n ŋobira moo.

eng: the LORD was with Joseph and remained loyal to him. He caused the jails commander to think highly of Joseph.
local: ka Duuma daa n ba o saani, ka wusira u o nivumantiri, a ti niso n nyaasira diyooriba maa kpabira o yela.

eng: But the chief wine steward didnt remember Joseph; he forgot all about him.
local: Yela ŋa zaa nyaaŋa, buŋŋuura nyaasira kpeema la, daa n bi lee n teesi Yisifu yela.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, weak and frail and thin, climbed up after them. Ive never seen such awful cows in all the land of Egypt.
local: A nyaaŋa, nagikogiri dagiri ayapoin gba yi. M na bi nye lala nagikogiri taaba zaa yi Yijipiti paaligaa.

eng: The jails commander put all of the prisoners in the jail under Josephs supervision, and he was the one who determined everything that happened there.
local: A zu, kpeema maa naa bunso zaa yi diyoorigu maa puu pobi Yisifu nuusi, wune n daa n niŋi tuma maa yipaara.

eng: Then the thin, frail cows devoured the first seven, fattened cows.
local: Binkogiri maa naa binkara ayapoin n veela la vali, seesi n piili n yi la.

eng: The jails commander paid no attention to anything under Josephs supervision, because the LORD was with him and made everything he did successful.
local: Kpeema maa bi lee n nyaasira Yisifu yela niŋi, a yi tuma maa pelli, a kpali Duuma daa bani Yisifu saani, ka ti o yela wara yiya, bunso zaag o n niŋina.

eng: But after they swallowed them whole, no one would have known it. They looked just as bad as they had before. Then I woke up.
local: Ama, a zaa nyaaŋa, nira zaa n ki baŋŋi maa a vali sen, a na si n kogini dagi seema a n kuri n be maa, ka ma yirimi n yoo.

eng: I went to sleep again and saw in my dream seven full and healthy ears of grain growing on one stalk.
local: M lee n zaasi n nye chi zuuri ayapoin n bi, a veela ga, ka tomi chi gbinluŋko niŋi.

eng: Just then, seven hard and thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: A nyaaŋa, chi zu nyogirinyogiriseesi wuluŋpusigu sauwa n pemsi a kuu gba yi.

eng: and the thin ears swallowed up the healthy ears. I told the religious experts, but they couldnt explain it to me.
local: Chi nyogirinyogirisi maa naa binsuma la vali. Ma poli a wusi dibodibo niŋiniba, ba yindaana zaa bi tee n yeli ma, a gbina.

eng: Joseph said to Pharaoh, Pharaoh has actually had one dream. God has announced to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli naaba maa, E zaasima maa zaa gbina niŋini luŋko, Naawunni nyisini wusi i bunso o n ya n niŋi.

eng: The seven healthy cows are seven years, and the seven healthy ears of grain are seven years. Its actually one dream.
local: Nagisuma ayapoin la niŋini yuma ayapoin, ka chi zusuma ayapoin la mi niŋi yuma ayapoin, a gbina niŋini luŋko.

eng: The seven thin and frail cows, climbing up after them, are seven years. The seven thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, are seven years of famine.
local: Nagikogiri ayapoin, dagseesi n lee n yi la niŋini yuma ayapoin, lala n niŋi chi nyogirinyogirisi ayapoin seesi sauwa n yi sagbinni ti pemsi a kuu la gba. A niŋini kom yuma ayapoin.

eng: Its just as I told Pharaoh: God has shown Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: A si n niŋini maa seema ni yeli i la, naaba. Naawunni wusi i ni seema o n ya n niŋi.

eng: Seven years of great abundance are now coming throughout the entire land of Egypt.
local: Yuma ayapoin, bindira ni niŋi ga, a yi Yijipiti paaligaa.

eng: After them, seven years of famine will appear, and all of the abundance in the land of Egypt will be forgotten. The famine will devastate the land.
local: Yuma ayapoin nyaaŋa mini n lee n niŋi kom yuma, ya tami Yijipiti yuma ayapoin bindirigbaliŋu la yela. Kom maa ni saami Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

eng: No one will remember the abundance in the land because the famine that follows will be so very severe.
local: Ba ni tami bindirigbaliŋu maa zaa yela, a kpali kom, so n ya n ta maa, ni niŋi aligaaya.

eng: The dream occurred to Pharaoh twice because God has determined to do it, and God will make it happen soon.
local: Bunso zu e n zaasi u buyi zu n niŋi, Naawunni niŋini siriya maa, a niŋi. A ki yuu, Naawunni ni ti lala niŋi.

eng: Now Pharaoh should find an intelligent, wise man and give him authority over the land of Egypt.
local: Pimparaŋa, naaba, kpabi nivuso n mari yam, a baŋŋi yela, a naa Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu, a pobi o nuusi.

eng: Then Pharaoh should appoint administrators over the land and take one-fifth of all the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
local: Kpabi tumtuminiba niŋi Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ba deegira yuma ayapoin bindira seesi n ya n niŋi ga la, bunu zu zaa luŋko.

eng: During the good years that are coming, they should collect all such food and store the grain under Pharaohs control, protecting the food in the cities.
local: Ba deegira bindira maa yumsuma seesi n chena ŋa, a zaali a nyini naaba kpeeŋu niŋi. Ba zaali a tiŋkarigu zaa puwa.

eng: This food will be reserved for the seven years of famine to follow in the land of Egypt so that the land wont be ravaged by the famine.
local: Bindira ŋa, ba deeg a, a zaali n guri paaligaa, ti ti naag a paasi ti niŋi, a yi kom yuma seesi n chena Yijipiti la niŋi. Lala jemani, kom maa ki muri paaligaa.

eng: This advice seemed wise to Pharaoh and all his servants.
local: Yaseesi Yisifu n daa yeli maa daa peri naaba maa ni o kpemba zaa.

eng: and Pharaoh said to his servants, Can we find a man with more God-given gifts than this one.
local: Naaba maa boosi ba, Ti ni tee n nye nira maa dabaa, seema Naawunni sii n paali o niŋaa.

eng: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since God has made all this known to you, no one is as intelligent and wise as you are.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa yeli Yisifu, Seema Naawunni ni nyisi o yela zaa gbina wusi i ŋa, nira zaa ka niŋi, a baŋŋi Naawunni yela lala.

eng: You will be in charge of my kingdom, and all my people will obey your command. Only as the enthroned king will I be greater than you.
local: A zu, m ni naag i loobi kpeema, e kpebilim o pa m zu, e nyaasira m yiri bunso zaa, a ni m niriba. M niriba zaa ni wumi n tiri i, m naalim ko n ya n pa i yiya.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Know this: Ive given you authority over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Ka o lee n yeli Yisifu, M naa Yijipiti paaligaa zaa kpebilim pobi e nuusi.

eng: Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Josephs hand, he dressed him in linen clothes, and he put a gold necklace around his neck.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa nyisi o naalim beŋŋu yi o nuwa, a niŋi Yisifu nuwa. Ka pili u bimpinsuma, ka naa suga jaagba niŋi o nuŋgori.

eng: He put Joseph on the chariot of his second-in-command, and everyone in front of him cried out, Attention! So Pharaoh installed him over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Ka o ti ba naa Yisifu ziili binduurigusi turuku niŋi, a wusi maa so n pa o zu, ka ti niso n gutira u pa yiya, a tasi n yetira, ya ti soori, ya ti soori. Lala o daa n niŋi, a naa Yisifu loobi Yijipiti zaa kpeema.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I am Pharaoh; no one will do anything or go anywhere in all the land of Egypt without your permission.
local: Ka naaba maa daa n lee n yeli Yisifu, Mani n niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa naaba, nira zaa ki tee n niŋi sen Yijipiti paaligaa zaa, ka e bi ti a daana soori.

eng: Pharaoh renamed Joseph, Zaphenath-paneah, and married him to Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera the priest of Heliopolis. Then Joseph assumed control of the land of Egypt.
local: Niŋi n na, naaba maa ti Yisifu Yijipiti yuuri, Zafenati-Paniiya, ka lee n ti u poga, Asenati, o ba n niŋi Potifera, so n niŋi Oon tindaana. Ka Yisifu daa n chaŋŋi n kaa Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

eng: Joseph was years old when he began to serve Pharaoh, Egypts king, when he left Pharaohs court and traveled through the entire land of Egypt.
local: Yisifu daa n niŋini yuma pisita, ka kpe naaba maa tuma niŋi, a yi Yijipiti paaligu, ka chaŋŋi n kaa Yijipiti paaligu zaa la.

eng: During the seven years of abundance, the land produced plentifully.
local: Yuma ayapoin, bindira daa n niŋi ga.

eng: He collected all of the food during the seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt, and stored the food in cities. In each city, he stored the food from the fields surrounding it.
local: Lala yuma maa, Yisifu daa laŋsini bindira maa zaali tiŋkara maa niŋi. Tiŋkarigu zaa puuri niŋi n na, o daa laŋsini bindira maa zaali.

eng: Joseph amassed grain like the sand of the sea. There was so much that he stopped trying to measure it because it was beyond measuring.
local: Bindira maa Yisifu n daa n laŋsira maa daa n ti niŋi zoogirisim, o daa n cheli a kaaligu, a daa n zooya ga, a maa teeku tambisigu.

eng: Before the years of famine arrived, Asenath the daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, gave birth to two sons for Joseph.
local: Poin, ka kom maa daa n ti li, ka Yisifu ni o poga Asenati daa n dogini biduusi bayi.

eng: Joseph named the oldest son Manasseh, because, he said, God has helped me forget all of my troubles and everyone in my fathers household.
local: Yisifu daa n digili o bikpeema maa yuuri Manaasi, ka yeli, Naawunni ti ma tami nam la zaa, a ni m ba laanta niriba zaa yela.

eng: He named the second Ephraim, because, he said, God has given me children in the land where Ive been treated harshly.
local: Yuso o n daa n lee n digili biisi bayi daana maa n niŋi, Efirem, ka yeli, Naawunni ti ni ma nye dogim, a yi paaliguso ni nye nam.

eng: The seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt came to an end.
local: Yuma ayapoin seesi bindira n daa n niŋi ga maa jemani n daa n ti gaari.

eng: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. The famine struck every country, but the entire land of Egypt had bread.
local: yuma ayapoin kom n daa n ti piili, a maa seema Yisifu ni daa n yeli la. Kom maa daa n ti li paaliguseesi paasi, ama Yijipiti paaligu wune, bindira daa n ba niŋi.

eng: When the famine ravaged the entire land of Egypt and the people pleaded to Pharaoh for bread, Pharaoh said to all of the Egyptians, Go to Joseph. Do whatever he tells you.
local: A n daa n ti wumina Yijipiti dima, a yi kom pelli, ba daa n fobili n ti Yijipiti naaba, o yeli ba, Ya we Yisifu saani, a wa ti niŋi bunso zaa o n yeli ya niŋi.

eng: The famine covered every part of the land, and Joseph opened all of the granaries and sold grain to the Egyptians. In the land of Egypt, the famine became more and more severe.
local: Kom maa n daa n ti saligi seela zaa, Yisifu yoo o bindira diisi la, a nyisi chi maa koosi n tira Yijipiti dima.

eng: Every country came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph, because in every country the famine had also become more severe.
local: A kpali kom maa n daa n ba kpeeŋu ga seela zaa zu, niriba daa n yini paaliguseesi gba, daa n ti daara chi maa yi Yisifu saani.

eng: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, Why are you staring blankly at each other?.
local: Yakubu n daa n ti wumi ni bindira ba Yijipiti paaligu, o boosi o biduusi n ŋa, Wula n na ya na zi n nyaasira taaba.

eng: Ive just heard that theres grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy some for us so that we can survive and not starve to death.
local: O daa n lee n yeli ba, M wumiya maa bindira ba Yijipiti, a zu, ya we niŋi, a wa ti da seesi ta, ti naa n guri ti zu, lala jemani, kom ki lee n ku ti.

eng: So Josephs ten brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu beya pii we Yijipiti, ni ba wa ti da chi.

eng: However, Jacob didnt send Josephs brother Benjamin along with his brothers because he thought something bad might happen to him.
local: Yakubu daa n bi ti ba sunzo Benjamin paa ba gaari, a kpali, o daa n zorini dabeem maa, o ni nye daaŋu.

eng: Israels sons came to buy grain with others who also came since the famine had spread to the land of Canaan.
local: Kom maa daa n lini Keenan paaligu gba, a zu, Yakubu biduusi maa daa n paani niseesi we bindira maa daabu.

eng: The famine was severe in the land.
local: Kom maa daa n na ba kpeeŋu yi paaligu maa niŋi.

eng: As for Joseph, he was the lands governor, and he was the one selling grain to all the lands people. When Josephs brothers arrived, they bowed down to him, their faces to the ground.
local: Yisifu mini n daa niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa kpeema, wune n daa n koosira chi maa tira paaligu maa dima. O beya maa n daa n ti paa Yijipiti, ba kpe Yisifu saani, a gba u tiŋŋa, ka zulisi ba zuuri.

eng: and when they had eaten all the grain that they brought from Egypt, their father said to them, Go back and buy us a little food.
local: ba n daa n di ba chiseesi ba n daa n da n yi Yijipiti la baasi, ba ba yeli ba, Ya lee n gaa n wa ti da bindira beela ti ti ti.

eng: When Joseph saw his brothers, he recognized them, but he acted like he didnt know them. He spoke to them with a harsh tone and said, Where have you come from? And they said, From the land of Canaan to buy food.
local: Yisifu n daa n si n nye o beya maa, o baŋŋi ba, ka niŋi maa o bi baŋŋi ba, ka togsi ba saani suuritiri, a boosi ba, Ya n na ya yi? Ba yeli u, Ti yini Keenan, ti tani ti ti da bindira.

eng: Judah said to him, The man was absolutely serious when he said, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Juuda yeli u, Daba maa kpaasi ti tubiri ga, Ya ki lee n nye m nufuaa see, ama ya ni ya mabii maa n ta.

eng: Joseph recognized his brothers, but they didnt recognize him.
local: Yisifu daa n baŋŋi o beya maa nene, ama ba wune daa n bi lee n baŋŋi u.

eng: If you agree to send our brother with us, then we will go down and buy you food.
local: Ka e ni ti ti mabiiaa pa ti zu wune, ti ni lee n wa ti da bindira ti ti i.

eng: Joseph remembered the dreams he had dreamed about them, and said to them, You are spies. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Niŋi n na, o teesi o zaasima yi ba pelli, ka yeli ba, Ya niŋini daatiriba, ya tani kpe, ya ti daari paaligaa, a nye seela ti pugirisim n be.

eng: But if you dont agree to send him, then we cant go down because the man said to us, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Ama, ka e yi ki sagi wune, ti ki gaari, a kpali, daba maa yeli ti, ni, ti ki lee n nye o nufusee, ka ti ni ti mabii maa bi na.

eng: They said to him, No, Master. Your servants have just come to buy food.
local: Ba yeli u, Akai, ti yidaana, pa lala n na, e gurunsi tani, ti ti da bindira.

eng: Israel said, Why have you caused me such pain by telling the man you had another brother.
local: Yakubu boosi ba, Bo n na ya yeli daba maa ni ya mabii na ba niŋi, a taasi ma garidama ŋa.

eng: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men. Your servants arent spies.
local: Ti zaa niŋini mabiluŋko, dabluŋko biisi, ti niŋini nivumansi, ti bi niŋi daatiriba.

eng: They said, The man asked us pointedly about our family: Is your father still alive? Do you have a brother? So we told him just what weve said. How were we to know hed say, Bring your brother down here.
local: Ba yeli u, Daba maa boosi ti ni boosa, maa o n baŋŋi ti ni ti laanta zaa yela. Ya ba na ba niŋi? Ya na kpari n mari mabiya? Ti sagi. Wula n na ti ya n niŋi n baŋŋi, o ya n yeli, ya mari u paasi n ta.

eng: He said to them, No. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Yisifu yeli ba, Akai, ya tani ya ti daari ti paaligaa, seela ti pugirisim n be.

eng: Judah said to his father Israel, Send the young man with me. Lets get ready to leave so that we can stay alive and not die—we, you, and our children.
local: Juuda yeli o ba Yakubu, Naa bii maa pobi m nuusi, ti gaa pimparaŋa, ka ti nini, a ni ti biisi da kpi ni kom.

eng: They said, We, your servants, are twelve brothers, sons of one man in the land of Canaan. The youngest is now with our father, but one is gone.
local: Ba yeli u, Tirima, e gurunsi, ti zaa daa n bani pii ni bayi, dabluŋko biisi, ka o zi Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, ti luŋko lee n ka niŋi, ka ti bibila daana mi ba ti ba saani.

eng: I will guarantee his safety; you can hold me responsible. If I dont bring him back to you and place him here in front of you, it will be my fault forever.
local: M gbagba ni za n ti o nyevuri moligirisim. Naag o tarijugu ziili ma. Ka m yi bi mari u ti ti i, ni alaanfiya, o tarijugu, o pa ma, m nyevuri puu zaa.

eng: Joseph said to them, Its just as Ive said to you. You are spies!.
local: Yisifu yeli ba, Wune n na ma si n yeli ya la, ya niŋini daatiriba.

eng: If we hadnt waited so long, we wouldve returned twice by now.
local: Seema o n be ŋa, ka ti di bi nagimi, ti di gaaya, ka pori n ta buyi daana.

eng: But here is how to prove yourselves: As Pharaoh lives, you wont leave here until your youngest brother arrives.
local: N ŋa n na ma ya n niŋi ya yatoga ŋa nyaasi, a baŋŋi ya yamonsi. M pe Yijipiti naaba nyevuri maa, ya ki yi Yijipiti, ama ya sunzo la gba tani kpe.

eng: Their father Israel said to them, If it has to be, then do this. Take in your bags some of the lands choice produce, and bring it down to the man as a gift: a little medicinal resin, a little honey, gum, resin, pistachios, and almonds.
local: Ba ba Yakubu yeli ba, Ka lala wune n na, ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa tiŋgbaŋaa binseesi wa ti pi u piini: kpasarigira beela, sikpaam beela, a ni binseesi n tira ziiri ŋusuma, tulalesi, a ni tiwalibiseesi n meesa.

eng: Send one of you to get your brother, but the rest of you will stay in prison. We will find out if your words are true. If not, as Pharaoh lives, you are certainly spies.
local: A zu, ya tumi ya yinni, o wa ti mari ya sunzo maa ta, ama, ba ni yo ya seesi n che la dii, lala jemani, ya yatoga puu n na, ti ni naa n baŋŋi maa yamonsi n na ya yetira. Ama, ka lala bi niŋi, a yi naaba maa nyevuri zu, ya niŋini daatiriba.

eng: Take twice as much silver with you, and take back the silver returned in the top of your sacks. It might have been a mistake.
local: Ya naa ligiri tulumbuyaayi zu, ya wa ti yo seesi n daa n ba ya bowatesi puusi la. Saŋa, ba daa n naag a ni niŋi, ka bi baŋŋi.

eng: He put them all in prison for three days.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu ti ba yo ba dii dabaa ata.

eng: And take your brother, get ready, and go back to the man.
local: Ya mari ya mabii maa paasi n gaa yoom.

eng: On the third day, Joseph said to them, Do this and you will live, for Im a God-fearing man.
local: Dabaa ata maa daari, Yisifu daa yeli ba, M niŋini Naawunni zora, ya niŋi seema ni ya n wusiya ŋa, ka nye ya zu.

eng: May God Almighty make the man compassionate toward you so that he may send back your other brother and Benjamin with you. But me, if Im left childless, then Im left childless.
local: Naawunni kpeeŋu zaa daana, o zo ya nimbaa yi daba maa saani. O ti ya mari ya mabii maa ni Benjamin zaa pori n ta. Ama, ma wune yi bi lee n nye ba gba, wune n na la.

eng: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers stay in prison, and the rest of you, go, take grain back to those in your households who are hungry.
local: Ka ya niŋi yamonsi diriba, ya ti ya luŋko, o na ba diyoorigaa puwa, ka ya seesi n che la, ya naa chi pori n wa ti ti ya yidima, kom n kuura la.

eng: So the men took this gift. They took twice as much silver with them, together with Benjamin. They left, traveled down to Egypt, and received an audience with Joseph.
local: A zu, dabsi maa naa ba piinisi ni ligiri tulumbuyaayi, ka mari Benjamin gba paasi, ba niŋi yi n gaa Yijipiti, wa ti naa ba maŋŋa we Yisifu saani.

eng: But bring your youngest brother back to me so that your words will prove true and you wont die. So they prepared to do this.
local: Ama, ya mari ya sunzobila la ti ti ma. N ŋa ni wusi maa yamonsi ba ya yatoga puwa. Lala jemani, ya ki kpi. Ba sagi.

eng: Joseph gave commands to his household manager: Fill the mens sacks with as much food as theyll hold, and put each mans silver at the top of his sack.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli o laanta nachiinsi kpeema n ŋa, Niŋi bindira niŋi niribaa bowatesi a puwa, a paali seema ba n di n tee n zi, ka e pori so zaa ligiri niŋi o bowate niŋi.

eng: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the manager of his household, Bring the men to the house and slaughter an animal and prepare it because the men will have dinner with me at noon.
local: Jemanso Yisifu n nye ba ni Benjamin la, o yeli o nachiima, Mari niribaa gaari m laanta, ka korigi buŋkoowa, ba niŋi bindira, m ni ba n ya n di wuntooŋaa.

eng: The brothers said to each other, We are clearly guilty for what we did to our brother when we saw his life in danger and when he begged us for mercy, but we didnt listen. Thats why were in this danger now.
local: Ba yeli taaba, O doni soori maa yaso ti n daa n niŋi ti sunzo Yisifu la zu n na, ti nye n ŋa tubidarigirisim la. Ti daa n nye nimbirimoso n daa n paag u, o suusi ti, ni ti cheli u, ka ti zagsi, lala nimbirimoo n paa ti gba la.

eng: Put my cup, the silver cup, on top of the youngest brothers sack, together with the silver for his grain. So he did just as Joseph told him to do.
local: Ka naa m gbiti kobee la, a ni ligiseesi ba sunzobila la n di n ya n naa da bindira maa paasi n niŋi o bindira bowate maa puwa. Niŋi n na, nachiima maa niŋi seema Yisifu n yeli u maa.

eng: The man did as Joseph told him and brought the men to Josephs house.
local: Daba maa niŋi bunso zaa Yisifu ni yeli u la, o mari niriba maa gaa Yisifu laanta.

eng: Reuben responded to them, Didnt I tell you, Dont do anything wrong to the boy? But you wouldnt listen. So now this is payback for his death.
local: Ka Rubin boosi ba, M daa n bi yeli ya, ni ya da pa ti niŋi biyaa sen, ka ya zagsi? To, bunso ti n daa n niŋi u la, o zeem maa samini n yoora ti la.

eng: At dawn, the men and their donkeys were sent off.
local: Bee n daa n ti chee, bogufiili, o beya maa niŋi ba buŋsi siriya, a gbaa yiri soori.

eng: When they were brought to Josephs house, the men were frightened and said, Weve been brought here because of the silver put back in our sacks on our first trip so he can overpower us, capture us, make slaves of us, and take our donkeys.
local: Dabeem daa n gbaani niriba maa, ba n mari ba gaari o laanta la. Ba feni maa, Ba mari ti ni ta kpe kpali ti ligiri la n daa n ba ti bowatesi puusi la zu. O ya n zabini nyaŋŋi ti, ka naa ti loobi o gurunsi, a naa ti buŋsaa paasi.

eng: They didnt know that Joseph was listening to them because they were using an interpreter.
local: Ba daa bi baŋŋi maa Yisifu wumini ba yatoga la, a kpali, nira n daa n loobira ba yatoga maa tiri u.

eng: They had left the city but hadnt gone far when Joseph said to his household manager, Get ready, go after the men and catch up with them! Ask them, Why have you repaid hospitality with ingratitude?.
local: Ba n daa n yi tiŋkarigu maa wara, ba daa n na bi paa wogila, ka Yisifu yeli o nachiinsi kpeema maa n ŋa, Zo yomyom kari niriba la bobiri. Ka e paa ba, e boosi ba, Wula n na ya naa yabeeri yoora yasuma samini?.

eng: They approached the man who was Josephs household manager and spoke to him at the houses entrance:.
local: Ba wa ti togsi Yisifu nachiima maa saani, a yi laanta maa voori noori.

eng: He stepped away from them and wept. When he returned, he spoke with them again. Then he took Simeon from them and tied him up in front of them.
local: Ba n daa n togsira, Yisifu gaa seela kumi, ka n lee n loobi n ti togsi ba saani. O daa n ti ni ba gbaa Simiyon yi ba puwa, a le u, ba zaa nufu niŋi.

eng: Isnt this the cup my master drinks from and uses to discover Gods plans? What youve done is despicable.
local: Wula n na ya zu m yidaana gbiti kobee? Wune n na o ma n ŋuura bini, ka lee n naag u baŋina soosizu yela. Ya niŋi yabee ga.

eng: Please, Master, we came down the first time just to buy food.
local: Ba yeli u, Gaafira, ti n daa n ta kpe ti da bindira piiligu la.

eng: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, to put back each mans silver into his own sack, and to give them provisions for their trip, and it was done.
local: Yisifu ti ba niŋi chi niŋi o beya bowatesi niŋi, ka pori ba ligiri niŋi ba bowatesi puusi, ka lee n ti ba soori puu bindira. O n niŋi ba n ŋa baasi.

eng: When he caught up to them, he repeated these words.
local: Nachiinsi kpeema maa n wa ti paa ba, o yeli ba seema gbagba o yidaana n yeli u la.

eng: but when we stopped to spend the night and opened our sacks, there was the exact amount of each mans silver at the top of his sack. Weve brought it back with us.
local: soori puu seela n na, yiiŋu zibi ti, ti zaa lerigi ti bowatesi, ka nye ti ligiri n ba ti bowatesi naŋgbana niŋi, seema gbagba ti n da. A zu, ti mari a pori n ta.

eng: They loaded their grain onto their donkeys, and they set out.
local: ba kaasi ba ziiri pa ba buŋsi niŋi, a gbaa soori.

eng: They replied, Why does my master talk to us like this? Your servants would never do such a thing.
local: Niŋi n na, ba yeli u, Bo n na e yeli ti lala? Ti niŋini e gurunsi, ti ki sagi n niŋi lala!.

eng: and weve brought down with us additional silver to buy food. We dont know who put our silver in our sacks.
local: Ka ti na mari ligiri paasi n ta, ti ti daa bindira. Ti bi baŋŋi niso n naa ti ligiri pori n niŋi ti bowatesi niŋi.

eng: When they stopped to spend the night, one of them opened his sack to feed his donkey, and he saw his silver at the top of his sack.
local: Ba n wa ti paa seela ba n ya n do n kaa bee, yindaana lerigi o bowate, ka o niŋi bindira ti o buŋŋu, ka nye o ligiri n pa saa.

eng: The silver that we found at the top of our sacks, weve just brought back to you from the land of Canaan. We didnt steal silver or gold from your masters house.
local: Ti n daa we ti paa ti yiri, ka nye ti ligiri, ti daa n bi lee n pori a yi Keenan zaa ti ti ya? Ka wula zu n na ti ya n zu e yidaana gbiti koo suga, a yi o yiri?.

eng: He said, You are fine. Dont be afraid. Your God and your fathers God must have hidden a treasure in your sacks. I received your money. Then he brought Simeon out to them.
local: O yeli ba, A zaa veela, ya da zora dabeem, ya Naawunni, ya ba Naawunni n ti ya ligiri yi ya bowatesi niŋi, ma wune dee ya ligiri. Niŋi n na, o mari Simiyon ti paasi ba niŋi.

eng: He said to his brothers, My silvers been returned. Its right here in my sack. Their hearts stopped. Terrified, they said to each other, What has God done to us.
local: O yeli o mabiisi la, Ba pori m ligiri la niŋi ma, ŋarima n ba m bowate puu la. Dabeem gbaa ba, ba niŋa ŋme taaba niŋi, ka ba boosi, Bo yela n na Naawunni niŋi ti ŋa.

eng: Whoever of your servants is found with it will be put to death, and well be my masters slaves.
local: Ka e yi nye u yi ti yindaana zaa saani, kum n na a daana ni kpi, ka ti seesi n che la, ti mi loobi m yidaana daaba.

eng: The manager brought the men into Josephs house and gave them water to wash their feet and feed for their donkeys.
local: Niŋi n na, nachiima la mari ba kpe Yisifu dii, ka ti ba koom ba pegi ba noba, ka lee n ti ba buŋsi bindira.

eng: When they got back to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan, they described to him everything that had happened to them:.
local: Ba n ta ba ba Yakubu saani yi Keenan paaligu, ba yeli u yatogso zaa n paa ba, ba yeli.

eng: He said, Fine. Well do just as youve said. Whoever is found with it will be my slave, and the rest of you will go free.
local: O yeli ba, M wumiya. Ama ya so saani ti n nye kobeegaa ko ni loobi m daabiri, ka ya seesi n che la, ya nye ya zu.

eng: They prepared the gift, anticipating Josephs arrival at noon, since they had heard that they would have a meal there.
local: Ba wumiya maa nisaaniŋi n na ba ya n di wuntooŋu maa. A zu, ba niŋi ba piinisi siriya gutira Yisifu, o ta.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, spoke to us with a harsh tone and accused us of being spies in the country.
local: Dabso n niŋi paaligu maa kpeema maa togsi ti halibee yatoga ga, ka niŋi ti maa ti n niŋi daatiriba yi paaligu maa niŋi.

eng: Everyone quickly lowered their sacks down to the ground and each opened his sack.
local: A zu, ba zaa lerigi ba bowatesi yi ba buŋsi niŋi yoom zaali tiŋŋa, ka lerigi a noya.

eng: When Joseph came into the house, they presented him the gift they had brought with them into the house, and they bowed low in front of him.
local: Jemanso Yisifu n ta yiri, ba naa ba piiniseesi ba n ma n ta la ti u, ka gba u tiŋŋa, ka zulisi ba zuuri.

eng: We told him, Were honest men, not spies.
local: Ama, ti yeli u maa, ti bi niŋi daatiriba, ti niŋini jilima dima.

eng: He searched the oldest first and the youngest last, and the cup was found in Benjamins sack.
local: Yisifu nachiinsi kpeema maa yuligira ba ziiri puusi nyaasira, ba kpeema daana ziiri niŋi n na o piili yuligi, o yuligi lala a ti paa bibila daana, Benjamin. O bowate puu n na o nye gbiti kobee maa.

eng: He asked them how they were and said, How is your elderly father, about whom you spoke? Is he still alive.
local: O boosi ba alaanfiya yela, ka boosi ba, Ya ba, dakuriso yela ya n daa n yeli ma la, ba wula? O na ba o nyevuri niŋi.

eng: We are twelve brothers, all our fathers sons. One of us is gone, but the youngest is right now with our father in the land of Canaan.
local: Ka yeli u, ti daa n bani pii ni bayi, dabluŋko biisi, yindaana lee n ka niŋi, ka ti bibila daana ba ti ba saani yi Keenan.

eng: At this, they tore their clothing. Then everyone loaded their donkeys, and they returned to the city.
local: O beya maa darigi ba bimpinna kasigi, a kpali sufusaaŋu zu, ka ba lee n le ba ziirisi maa pa ba buŋsi niŋi, a pori n gaa tiŋŋa maa puwa.

eng: They said, Your servant our father is fine. Hes still alive. And they bowed down again with deep respect.
local: Ba yeli u, E guruŋu, ti ba, na ba niŋi ni alaanfiya. Ka ba gba u tiŋŋa jilimatiri.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, told us, This is how I will know you are honest men: Leave one of your brothers with me, take grain for those in your households who are hungry, and go.
local: Niŋi n na, dabso n niŋi Kpeema yi paaligu maa yeli ti, N ŋa n na ma ni baŋŋi maa ya niŋini jilima niriba, ya ti ya yindaana o zi m saani, ka ya seesi n che la, ya naa chi la wara yiri, kom la zu.

eng: When Judah and his brothers arrived at Josephs house, he was still there, and they fell to the ground in front of him.
local: Juuda ni o sunzoba maa n daa n pori n we tiŋŋa maa puwa, ba we Yisifu yiri, a wa ti do u tiŋŋa.

eng: Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mothers son, and he said, Is this your youngest brother whom you told me about? God be gracious to you, my son.
local: O nyaasi ba poli n jili n ti nye o sunzo Benjamin, o ma bii gbagba, o boosi, Ya sunzo so yela ya n yeli ma la n ŋa? Ka yeli, Naawunni nimbaa nuu o pa e niŋi, m biya.

eng: But bring back your youngest brother to me. Then I will know that you are not spies but honest men. I will give your brother back to you, and you may travel throughout the country.
local: Ama, ya mari ya sunzobila la ta, lala m ni baŋŋi maa, ya bi niŋi daatiriba, ama jilima niriba, ka m pori ya mabii ti ya, ka ya dira yawu yi kpe.

eng: Joseph said to them, Whats this youve done? Didnt you know someone like me can discover Gods plans.
local: Yisifu boosi ba, Bo n na ya niŋi seema? Ya bi baŋŋi maa, ka nira ba maa seema ni be ŋa, o ni tee n baŋŋi yaseesi n sogi.

eng: Josephs feelings for his brother were so strong he was about to weep, so he rushed to another room and wept there.
local: Kumsi gbaag u yi o beya maa sunsuuniŋi, o nyisigi yoom yi n kpabira seela o n di n wa ti kumi. O kpe n gaari o dikpaari wa ti kumi.

eng: When they opened their sacks, each man found a pouch of his silver in his sack. When they and their father saw their pouches of silver, they were afraid.
local: Ba n daa n kpaagira ba chi yi ba bowatesi puwa, ba zaa nye ni ba ligiri borosi n ba niŋi. Jemanso ba ni ba ba n nye ligiri borosi maa, dabeem gbaa ba.

eng: Judah replied, What can we say to my master? What words can we use? How can we prove we are innocent? God has found your servants guilty. We are now your slaves, all of us, including the one found with the cup.
local: Niŋi n na, Juuda yeli, Pimparaŋa, bo n na ti lee n mari ti yeli i, ti yidaana? Wula ti lee n ya n yeli, a wusi maa ti bi naa kobee maa? Naawunni yuligi ti gbinni, ti yabee yi paaligu, ti zaa ni loobi e daaba, pa so saani e ni nye gbiti kobee maa ko.

eng: He washed his face, came back, pulled himself together, and said, Set out the dinner.
local: A nyaaŋa, o pegi o nufu, ka yi laanta, a gbaag o maŋŋa, ka yeli, Ya tori bindira la.

eng: Their father Jacob said to them, Youve taken my children from me. Josephs gone. Simeons gone. And you are taking Benjamin. All this cant really be happening to me.
local: Ba ba Yakubu yeli ba, Ya naa m biisi, ka che ma. Yisifu lee n ka, Simiyon gba ka, pimparaŋa, ya lee n ya n naa Benjamin gaari. Mani n na bunso zaa ziina.

eng: Joseph said, Id never do such a thing. Only the man found with the cup will be my slave. As for the rest of you, you are free to go back to your father.
local: Yisifu yeli ba, Akai! M ki niŋi lala. So saani ba n nye kobee la yi o ziiri niŋi maa ko n ni loobi m daabiri. Yaseesi n che la, ya pori n kunna ya ba saani, a ni niŋgbirimaasim.

eng: So they set out his food by himself, their food by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves because Egyptians dont allow themselves to eat with Hebrews; the Egyptians think it beneath their dignity.
local: Ba ti u o toori, o ziizii kuri, ka ti o beya la gba, ba ziizii kuri, ka Yijipiti dimseesi o ni ba n dira la gba, ziizii kuri, a kpali Yijipiti dima ni Hiburu dima bi lagimi n dira, Yijipiti dima chisirini lala.

eng: Reuben said to his father, You may put both of my sons to death if I dont bring him back to you. Make him my responsibility, and I will make sure he returns to you.
local: Rubin yeli o ba, Ka m si n bi mari u pori n ta, e ku m biduusi bayi ŋa. Naag u pobi m nuusi, m ni mari u pori n ta.

eng: Judah approached him and said, Please, my master, allow your servant to say something to my master without getting angry with your servant since you are like Pharaoh himself.
local: Niŋi n na, Juuda we o saani, a wa ti yeli u n ŋa, M yidaana, jaande, cheli e daabiri togsi yaluŋko ti i. Da ti e suuri nyisigi, m baŋŋi e ni Yijipiti naaba zaa n sagi.

eng: They were seated in front of him from the oldest to the youngest in their exact birth order, and the men looked at each other with amazement.
local: Ba daa n ti ni o beya maa zi n pa taaba zu, kpemba kpemba, o yiya, ba nyaasi taaba ni naŋgbaŋkuubu.

eng: But Jacob said to him, My son wont go down with you because his brothers dead and hes been left all alone. If anything were to happen to him on the trip you are taking, you would send me—old as I am—to my grave in grief.
local: Ama Yakubu yeli u, M biduwa ki pa ya zu gaa niŋi. O beeri kpiya, ka che o ko. Ka garidama ti gbaag u soori puwa, m zuaa peligini jaga, ya ni pi m ni sufusaaŋu.

eng: My master asked his servants, Do you have a father or brother.
local: Nyini n daa n boosi ti, ni ti mari ba ni sunzo?.

eng: Portions of food from Josephs table were brought to them, but Benjamins portion was five times as large as theirs. So they drank together and were at ease.
local: Ba ti ba bindira yi Yisifu teebul niŋi, Benjamin toori daa n gaa ba zaa toya tulum bunu zu. Ba nunu di, ka ŋu n kuuli.

eng: And we said to my master, Yes, we have an elderly father and a young brother, born when he was old. His brother is dead and hes his mothers only child. But his father loves him.
local: Ti sagi, Mm, ti mariya, ti ba kpe kpebilim niŋi, ka dogi biduwa o kurigirisim jemani. Bii maa beeri kpiya, o ko n che o ma biya, ka o ba kpabira u ga.

eng: You told your servants, Bring him down to me so I can see him.
local: Niŋi n na, e daa n yeli ti, ni ti mari u ta, e nye u.

eng: And we said to my master, The young man cant leave his father. If he leaves, his father will die.
local: Ti mi daa n yeli i, bii maa ki tee n larimi o ba. Ka o yi larimi o ba, o ba maa ni kpi.

eng: You said to your servants, If your youngest brother doesnt come down with you, youll never see my face again.
local: Ama, e daa n yeli ti, ni, ka ti bi mari ti sunzo maa ta e saani, ti nini nimbiisi ki lee n niŋi anaasi.

eng: When we went back to my father your servant, we told him what you said.
local: Ti n daa n pori n gaari ti ba, e daabiri, saani, ti yeli u seema zaa e n yeli.

eng: Our father told us, Go back and buy for us a little food.
local: Niŋi n na, ti ba yeli ti, ya pori n wa ti da bindira ta.

eng: But we said, We cant go down. We will go down only if our youngest brother is with us. We wont be able to gain an audience with the man without our youngest brother with us.
local: Ti yeli u, Ti ki tee n gaa. Ka e bi ti ti sunzobilaa pa ti zu, ti ki nye daba maa nufu see.

eng: Your servant my father said to us, You know that my wife gave birth to two sons for me.
local: Ti ba yeli ti, ya baŋiya maa, m poga Rachel dogini biduusi bayi ko.

eng: One disappeared and I said, He must have been torn up by a wild animal, and I havent seen him since.
local: Luŋko lee n ka m saani, a ko moo puu buŋ n gbaag u. O n yi zaa, m bi lee n nye u.

eng: And if you take this one from me too, something terrible will happen to him, and you will send me—old as I am—to my grave in despair.
local: Ka ya lee n nyisi ŋa la mi yi m saani, ka garidama wa ti paa soori, sufusaaŋu ni ku ma, m kurigirisim niŋi.

eng: When I now go back to your servant my father without the young man—whose life is so bound up with his—.
local: A zu, m yidaana, ka m ko pori n gaa m ba saani, ka biyaa bi pobi m nuusi, m ba ni kpi, a kpali bo, o nyevuri zaa bani bii maa niŋi. O n kurigi ga la zu, sufusaaŋu ni ku u.

eng: I, your servant, guaranteed the young mans safety to my father, telling him, If I dont bring him back to you, it will be my fault forever.
local: M yidaana, poin ka ti ta kpe, mani, e daabiri, daa n naani m nyevuri pa tarima, biyaa zu, a yi m ba saani, maa ka biyaa yi bi lee n pori n ta, m ni zi o tarijugu, m beebu niŋi zaa.

eng: Now, please let your servant stay as your slave instead of the young man so that he can go back with his brothers.
local: A zu, jaande, cheli m dee bii maa noba, a ba kpe, a loobi e daabiri, ka bii maa o paa o beya ŋa pori n kunna.

eng: How can I go back to my father without the young man? I couldnt bear to see how badly my father would be hurt.
local: A kpali bo? Wula ma ya n pori n gaa m ba saani, ka che biyaa? M ki n ti n ŋa sufusaaŋu kpe m ba niŋi.

eng: Israel packed up everything he owned and traveled to Beer-sheba. There he offered sacrifices to his father Isaacs God.
local: A zu, Yakubu nyisigi n naa o bunso zaa o n mari, a wara. O n wa ti paa Beesiiba, o nyisi sariga ti Naawunni, so o ba Yisaaka n daa n tumina la.

eng: God said to Israel in a vision at night, Jacob! Jacob! and he said, Im here.
local: O yiiŋu, Naawunni togsi Yakubu saani yi zaasim niŋi, a booli, Yakubu, Yakubu. O sagi, Mani n ŋa.

eng: He said, I am El, your fathers God. Dont be afraid to go down to Egypt because I will make a great nation of you there.
local: O yeli u, Mani n niŋi Naawunni, so e ba n daa n tumina. Da zora dabeem, a yi Yijipiti weebu. M ni loobi i balitintaa nisaaniŋi.

eng: I will go down to Egypt with you, and I promise to bring you out again. Joseph will close your eyes when you die.
local: M ni paag i gaa Yijipiti, ka lee n mari e yaŋsi pori n ta paaligaa. Yisifu nuusi niŋi n na e ni kpi.

eng: Joseph could no longer control himself in front of all his attendants, so he declared, Everyone, leave now! So no one stayed with him when he revealed his identity to his brothers.
local: A jemani maa, Yisifu bi lee n tee n mari o maŋŋa yi o nachiinsi maa saani, ka chaari kpeeŋutiri yeli ba, So zaag o yi m saani. A zu, nira zaa daa n lee n ka Yisifu saani, jemanso o naag o maŋŋa wusi o mabiisi maa.

eng: Then Jacob left Beer-sheba. Israels sons put their father Jacob, their children, and their wives on the wagons Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yi Beesiiba. O biisi naa ba ba Yakubu, a ni ba biisi, ba pogiba, a niŋi turukuseesi naaba maa n naa ti, ba naa n mari ba ta la.

eng: He wept so loudly that the Egyptians and Pharaohs household heard him.
local: Ka chaari kpeeŋutiri, haali Yijipiti dima wumi, ka Yijipiti naaba laanta dima wumi o baaru.

eng: They took their livestock and their possessions that they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and arrived in Egypt, Jacob and all of his children with him.
local: Ba naa ba buŋkobiri ni ba kapeteseesi zaa ba n nye, a yi Keenan paaligu puwa. Yakubu ni o biisi zaa gaari Yijipiti.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im Joseph! Is my father really still alive? His brothers couldnt respond because they were terrified before him.
local: Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa, Mani n niŋi Yisifu! M ba na ba o nyevuri niŋi? Ama o mabiisi maa daa n bi tee ba sagi, a kpali bo, dabeem daa n gbaa ba ni.

eng: His sons and grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters—all of his descendants he brought with him to Egypt.
local: O mari o biisi ni o yaŋsi zaa nyem gaari.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Come closer to me, and they moved closer. He said, Im your brother Joseph! The one you sold to Egypt.
local: Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa, Ya vuu n ta m saani. Ba n niŋi lala, o yeli ba, mani n niŋi ya sunzo Yisifu, so ya n koosi n ti Yijipiti dima la.

eng: These are the names of the Israelites who went to Egypt, including Jacob and his sons. Jacobs oldest son was Reuben.
local: blank.

eng: Now, dont be upset and dont be angry with yourselves that you sold me here. Actually, God sent me before you to save lives.
local: Ama pimparaŋa, ya da ti ya sufusi saami, koo, a yeli maa ya bi niŋi nene, ya n koosi ma kpe ŋa. A kpali, ya nyevuya moligirisim zu n na, Naawunni ti ma pa ya yii ta kpe.

eng: All of the persons going to Egypt with Jacob—his own children, excluding Jacobs sons wives—totaled persons.
local: Niseesi n daa pa Yakubu zu gaa Yijipiti, seesi n niŋi o biisi ni yaŋsi, ba pisiyoobu ni bayoobu. O biisi pogiba bi paasi.

eng: Weve already had two years of famine in the land, and there are five years left without planting or harvesting.
local: Yuma ayi n na la, kom n ba tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, ka yuma anu seesi n che, koobu ki ba niŋi, cheebu gba ki ba niŋi.

eng: Josephs sons born to him in Egypt were persons. Thus, all of the persons in Jacobs household going to Egypt totaled .
local: Yisifu daa n dogini biduusi bayi yi Yijipiti, a naa n ti paasi Yakubu biisi niŋi, ba loobi pisiyapoin.

eng: God sent me before you to make sure youd survive and to rescue your lives in this amazing way.
local: Naawunni mari ma ni pa ya yii ta, m ti nye bunso zaali ya tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, a moligi ya ni ya nyaaŋa dima, ya nye moligirisim tintaa.

eng: Israel had sent Judah ahead to Joseph so that Joseph could explain the way to Goshen. Then they arrived in the land of Goshen.
local: Yakubu tumi Juuda, o pa u yii gaa Yisifu saani, a wa ti yeli u, o ti wusi ma Goosin soori. Ba n paa Goosin paaligu.

eng: You didnt send me here; it was God who made me a father to Pharaoh, master of his entire household, and ruler of the whole land of Egypt.
local: Ama, pa yarima n mari ma ta kpe, wune Naawunni n daa n naa ma loobi Yijipiti naaba feretira, a ni o laanta zaa kpeema, ka nyaasira Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

eng: Joseph hitched up his chariot and went to meet his father Israel in Goshen. When he arrived, he threw his arms around his neck and wept, embracing him for a long time.
local: Yisifu niŋi o turuku seriya, a gaa Goosin wa ti paag o ba Yakubu. O n wa ti paag u, o naag o nuusi wa ti che n vili o ba nuŋgori, ka kumi jemanwogili.

eng: Hurry! Go back to your father. Tell him this is what your son Joseph says: God has made me master of all of Egypt. Come down to me. Dont delay.
local: Pimparaŋa, ya pori yomyom gaari m ba saani, wa ti yeli u, N ŋa n na, e bii Yisifu yeli. Naawunni naa ma ni loobi yidaana, a yi Yijipiti zaa. Ta m saani, ka da nagimi.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, I can die now after seeing your face. You are really still alive.
local: Yakubu yeli Yisifu, Pimparaŋa m niŋi siriya m kpi, seema m gbagba n si n nye i, ka baŋŋi maa e na ba nyevuri puwa.

eng: You may live in the land of Goshen, so you will be near me, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, your herds, and everyone with you.
local: Ya ti zi Goosin paaligu, a leeli ma, ya ni ya biisi, ya yaŋsi, peeri, naagi, a ni bunso zaa ya n mari.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers and to his fathers household, Let me go up and inform Pharaoh and tell him, My brothers and my fathers household who were in the land of Canaan have arrived.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa ni o ba laanta zaa, M ni gaa naaba maa saani wa ti yeli u, M mabiisi ni m ba ni o laanta zaa, seesi n daa n ba Keenan paaligu la ta m saani.

eng: I will support you there, so you, your household, and everyone with you wont starve, since the famine will still last five years.
local: M ni tira ya bindira nisaaniŋi, a kpali, yuma anu gba kom na ba niŋi. Ka pa lala, ya ni ya laanta zaa ni ya bunso zaa ni kpini kom.

eng: The men are shepherds, because they own livestock. Theyve brought with them their flocks and herds and everything they own.
local: Dabsi maa niŋini pekpagiriba, ba guunini buŋkobiri. Ba marini ba peeri ni buŋkobiseesi ni bunso zaa ba n mari ta.

eng: You and my brother Benjamin have seen with your own eyes that Im speaking to you.
local: Yisifu lee n yeli, Ya gbagba nyeya, lala n niŋi m sunzo Benjamin gba, a maa o niŋini yamonsi, ni mani so n togsira ya saani n niŋi Yisifu.

eng: When Pharaoh summons you and says, What do you do.
local: Ka naaba maa booli ya boosi, Bo tuma n na ya tumina.

eng: Tell my father about my power in Egypt and about everything youve seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.
local: Ya yeli m ba, m yela, jilimaso ni mari yi Yijipiti paaligu, a ni yaso zaa ya n nye. Ka ya mari m ba ta kpe yomyom.

eng: say, Your servants have owned livestock since we were young, both we and our ancestors, so that you will be able to settle in the land of Goshen, since Egyptians think all shepherds are beneath their dignity.
local: Ya yeli u, E gurunsi guunini buŋkobiri ti bibiilim niŋi zaa, seema ti ba dima n niŋina. Lala wune, o ni sagi ya zi Goosin paaligu, a kpali peeri kpagirisim niŋini tumdagiri ti Yijipiti dima zu.

eng: He threw his arms around his brother Benjamins neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his shoulder.
local: Niŋi n na, o naag o nuusi ŋaami o sunzo Benjamin, ka kumi, Benjamin gba ŋaami u, ka kumina.

eng: He kissed all of his brothers and wept, embracing them. After that, his brothers were finally able to talk to him.
local: Ka mugisira o mabiisi maa, ka kumina. A nyaaŋa, o ni ba togsi taaba saani.

eng: When Pharaohs household heard the message Josephs brothers have arrived, both Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.
local: Niŋi n na, baaru maa paa naaba maa yiri, maa Yisifu mabiisi tani. A daa n peri naaba maa ni o kpemba zaa.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Give your brothers these instructions: Load your pack animals and go back to the land of Canaan.
local: O yeli Yisifu, Yeli e mabiisi maa, Ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa ya bindira le n pa ya buŋsi niŋi, a pori n gaa Keenan paaligu.

eng: Get your father and your households and come back to me. Let me provide you with good things from the land of Egypt so that you may eat the lands best food.
local: a wa ti mari ya ba ni ya pogiba ni ya biisi pori n ta m saani. M ni ti ya tiŋgbanmaŋa yi Yijipiti, ya di o tono ni meesim.

eng: Give them these instructions too: Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your children and wives, and pick up your father and come back.
local: Ka e lee n yeli ba, Ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa turukusi yi Yijipiti, wa ti naa ya biisi, pogiba, a ni ya ba ta.

eng: Dont worry about your possessions because you will have good things from the entire land of Egypt.
local: Ya da ti ya bini yela tiri ya, tiŋgbaŋaa binsuma zaa ni niŋi ya toori.

eng: So Israels sons did that. Joseph gave them wagons as Pharaoh instructed, and he gave them provisions for the road.
local: Lala jemani maa, Yakubu biisi niŋi seema ba n yeli ba maa. Yisifu gba naa turukusi ti ba, a maa seema naaba maa n yeli la, ka lee n ti ba soori puu bindira.

eng: To all of them he gave a change of clothing, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of clothing.
local: O ti ba so zaa bimpinna luŋkoluŋko, ama Benjamin wune, o ti u gbitisi kobsita, a ni bimpinna tulumbunu zu.

eng: To his father he sent ten male donkeys carrying goods from Egypt, ten female donkeys carrying grain and bread, and rations for his father for the road.
local: Binseesi o n naa ti ba wa ti ti o ba n ŋa, buŋsi pii zi Yijipiti bimmansi, ka naa chi ni paanusi ziili buŋnyaana piiya, a ni o ba soori puu bindira.

eng: He sent his brothers off; and as they were leaving, he told them, Dont be worried about the trip.
local: Niŋi n na, o beeli o mabiisi maa soori. Ba n wara maa, o yeli ba, Ya wara, ka da zabira.

eng: So they left Egypt and returned to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan.
local: Lala jemani, ba yi Yijipiti ta ba ba Yakubu saani yi Keenan paaligu.

eng: They announced to him, Josephs still alive! Hes actually ruler of all the land of Egypt! Jacobs heart nearly failed, and he didnt believe them.
local: Ba yeli u, Yisifu na ba niŋi! Wune n niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa nyaasira. Yakubu fere jebi u, o bi yeride ba.

eng: When they told him everything Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the wagons Joseph had sent to carry him, Jacob recovered.
local: Ama, jemanso ba n yeli u bunso zaa Yisifu n yeli ba, ka o lee n nye Yisifu n naa turukusi ti ba ti mari u pori n gaari, ba ba Yakubu sii pori n ta.

eng: Then Israel said, This is too much! My son Joseph is still alive! Let me go and see him before I die.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli, M wumi o gbinni, m bii Yisifu na ba niŋi. M ni wa ti nye u, poin ka kpi.

eng: Joseph went to inform Pharaoh and said, My father and brothers with their flocks, herds, and everything they own have come from the land of Canaan and are now in the land of Goshen.
local: Lala jemani, Yisifu wa ti yeli naaba maa, M ba ni m mabiisi, ni ba peeri ni naagi, a ni ba bunso zaa, yi Keenan paaligu ba Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi pimparaŋa.

eng: From all of his brothers, he selected five men and presented them before Pharaoh.
local: O nyisi o mabiisi banu zaali naaba maa yiya.

eng: Pharaoh said to Josephs brothers, What do you do? They said to Pharaoh, Your servants are shepherds, both we and our ancestors.
local: Naaba maa boosi ba, Bo tuma n na ya tumina? Ba yeli u, E gurunsi niŋini pekpagiriba, a maa ti ba dima.

eng: They continued, Weve come to the land as immigrants because the famine is so severe in the land of Canaan that there are no more pastures for your servants flocks. Please allow your servants to settle in the land of Goshen.
local: Ka ba lee n yeli u, Ti ya n ti zinni paa jemani yinni, a kpali kom maa ba kpeeŋu ga yi Keenan, ka e gurunsi peeri ka bindira. A zu, jaande ti e gurunsi ba zi Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since your father and brothers have arrived.
local: Naaba maa yeli Yisifu, E ba ni e mabiisi ta e saani.

eng: the land of Egypt is available to you. Settle your father and brothers in the lands best location. Let them live in the land of Goshen. And if you know capable men among them, put them in charge of my own livestock.
local: Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu zaa bani e nuusi niŋi, ti e ba ni e mabiisi maa ba zi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zimaŋŋa ziya. Ti ba zi Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi. Ka e baŋŋi ba seesi n baŋŋi buŋkobiri yela nene, e ti ba nyaasira m gba buŋkobiri ŋa.

eng: Joseph brought his father Jacob and gave him an audience with Pharaoh. Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu mari o ba Yakubu ti wusi naaba maa. Ka Yakubu dibi n ti naaba maa.

eng: and Pharaoh said to Jacob, How old are you.
local: Naaba maa boosi u, E yuma a la.

eng: Jacob said to Pharaoh, Ive been a traveler for years. My years have been few and difficult. They dont come close to the years my ancestors lived during their travels.
local: Yakubu yeli naaba maa, Yumseesi ni ma n chaŋŋi n poli n jili ba koowa ni pisita. M yuma bi zoo, ka a ba toom. M ki tee n naag a bugisi m ba dima yuma yi ba cheni puwa.

eng: Jacob blessed Pharaoh and left Pharaohs presence.
local: Niŋi n na, Yakubu lee n maali n dibi n ti naaba maa, ka yi o saani.

eng: Joseph settled his father and brothers and gave them property in the land of Egypt, in the best location in the land of Rameses, just as Pharaoh had ordered.
local: A zu, Yisifu ziili o ba ni o mabiisi yi Yijipiti, a ti ba bindaalim yi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zimaŋŋa ziya, Ramasi paaligu, seema naaba maa n wusi.

eng: Joseph provided food for his father, his brothers, and his fathers entire household, in proportion to the number of children.
local: Yisifu lee n ti o ba ni o mabiisi maa ni o ba laanta dima zaa bindira, a maa seema so zaa biisi n sagi.

eng: There was no food in the land because the famine was so severe. The land of Egypt and the land of Canaan dried up from the famine.
local: Kom maa n daa ba niŋi ti niŋi aligaaya zu, bindirigu daa n lee ka seela zaa, Yijipiti ni Keenan niriba zaa daa n lee ka kpeeŋu, a kpali kom maa zu.

eng: Joseph collected all of the silver to be found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan for the grain, which people came to buy, and he deposited it in Pharaohs treasury.
local: Yisifu n koosira bindira maa, o dee Yijipiti ni Keenan dima ligiri zaa, a naag a wa ti ti naaba maa.

eng: The silver from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan had been spent, and all of the Egyptians came to Joseph and said, Give us food. Why should we die before your eyes, just because the silver is gone.
local: Jemanso Yijipiti ni Keenan dima ligiri n baasi, Yijipiti dima zaa ta Yisifu saani ti yeli u, Ti ti bindira, a daga ti n kpi e nufu a niŋi? Ti ligiri zaa baasiya.

eng: Joseph said, Give me your livestock, and I will give you food for your livestock if the silver is gone.
local: Yisifu yeli ba, Ya ligiri n baasi a, ya mari ya buŋkobiri ti tagira.

eng: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food for the horses, flocks, cattle, and donkeys. He got them through that year with food in exchange for all of their livestock.
local: A zu, ba mari ba buŋkobiri chena Yisifu saani, o naa bindira maa tagira ba binduurigusi, peeri ni buusi, naagi ni buŋsi. Lala yumini maa, ba naa ba buŋkobiri zaa tagi bindira.

eng: When that year was over, they came to him the next year and said to him, We cant hide from my master that the silver is spent and that weve given the livestock to my master. All thats left for my master is our corpses and our farmland.
local: Lala yumini maa n ti gaari, yumiso n lee n pa, ba ta o saani ti yeli u, Ti ki tee n naa yamonsi sogi ti yidaana niŋi, ti ligiri zaa baasiya, ka ti buŋkobiri zaa loobi e toori, ti bi lee n mari, ti ti tiyidaana, ama ti maŋsi, a ni ti tiŋgbana.

eng: Why should we die before your eyes, we and our farmland too? Buy us and our farms for food, and we and our farms will be under Pharaohs control. Give us seed so that we can stay alive and not die, and so that our farmland wont become unproductive.
local: Wula zu n na ti ya n kpi e nufu niŋi, ti ni ti tiŋgbana zaa, Ti ni naa ti maŋsi ni ti tiŋgbana tagi bindira, ka ti ni ti tiŋgbana zaa, a loobi naaba maa toori. Ti ti bindira, ka ti da kpi, ka che tiŋgbaŋŋu yoo digili.

eng: So Joseph bought all of Egypts farmland for Pharaoh because every Egyptian sold his field when the famine worsened. So the land became Pharaohs.
local: A la zu, Yisifu da Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa ti naaba maa. Yijipiti dima zaa nyem daa n koosi ba tiŋgbana, a ti Yisifu, a kpali, kom maa daa n ba kpeeŋu ga ti ba. Tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa loobi naaba maa toori.

eng: He moved the people to the cities from one end of Egypt to the other.
local: Yisifu naa niriba maa zaa loobi daaba, a za Yijipiti kigbunfu ŋa, a wa ti tuusi.

eng: However, he didnt buy the farmland of the priests because Pharaoh allowed the priests a subsidy, and they were able to eat from the subsidy Pharaoh gave them. Therefore, they didnt have to sell their farmland.
local: Ama, o daa n bi da tiŋgbana maaniba la tiŋgbana, a kpali naaba maa daa n yi yoora ba ni ligiri, a puu n na ba daa n nyaara ba bindira. Bunso n ti ba bi koosi ba tiŋgbaŋŋu n na la.

eng: Joseph said to the people, Since Ive now purchased you and your farmland for Pharaoh, heres seed for you. Plant the seed on the land.
local: Yisifu yeli niriba maa, Pimparaŋa ni da ya ni ya tiŋgbana ti naaba maa ŋa. Bumbura n na la, ya naa n wa ti buri.

eng: When the crop comes in, you must give one-fifth to Pharaoh. You may keep four-fifths for yourselves, for planting fields, and for feeding yourselves, those in your households, and your children.
local: Ama, ka a ti niŋi, ya che, ya digili a bunu zu, naaba maa n su buluŋko zu. Bunaasi seesi n che la, ya naag a zaali ya ni ya pogiba ni biisi bindira ni bumbura.

eng: The people said, Youve saved our lives. If you wish, we will be Pharaohs slaves.
local: Ba yeli u, Ti yidaana, e moligi ti nyevuya. Ka a peri i, ti ni loobi naaba maa daaba.

eng: So Joseph made a law that still exists today: Pharaoh receives one-fifth from Egypts farmland. Only the priests farmland didnt become Pharaohs.
local: A zu, Yisifu piili u maa zaaligu, a wara Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu pelli, a ti paa zinni, o na tuminini, a maa bindira bunu daana niŋini naaba maa toori. Tiŋgbana maaniba la ko tiŋgbaŋŋu n daa bi niŋi naaba maa toori.

eng: Israel lived in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. They settled in it, had many children, and became numerous.
local: A jemani, Izireel dima daa n zinni Yijipiti paaligu, Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi. Ba nye kapete bini niŋi, ka dogi bidamta.

eng: After Jacob had lived in the land of Egypt for seventeen years, and after he had lived a total of years.
local: Yakubu n daa n zi Yijipiti, o yuma pii ni ayapoin, o yuma zaa daa n ba ni koowa ni pisinaasi ni ayapoin.

eng: Israels death approached. He summoned his son Joseph and said to him, If you would be so kind, lay your hand under my thigh, and be loyal and true to me. Dont bury me in Egypt.
local: Yakubu kum jemani n daa n ti paagira, o booli o bii Yisifu ti yeli u, Ka e ni sagi n ti ma, e naag e nuu tabi m kpiiligu tiŋini, ka digili noori maa e ni wusi ma kpabirisim ni yamonsitiri. Da pa ti pi ma yi Yijipiti.

eng: When I lie down with my fathers, carry me from Egypt and bury me in their grave. Joseph said, I will do just as you say.
local: Ama ka m pa ti kpi, ya zi ma yi, a wa ti pi ma yi m ba dima noba see. O yeli u, M ni niŋi i seema e n yeli maa.

eng: Israel said, Give me your word! and Joseph gave his word. Then Israel slumped down at the head of the bed.
local: Yakubu yeli u, Pe n ti ma. Niŋi n na, Yisifu pe n ti u, ka Yakubu zulisi o zu o bindoori niŋi.

eng: Jacob summoned his sons and said, Gather around so that I can tell you what will happen to you in the coming days.
local: blank.

eng: Jacob ordered them, I am soon to join my people. Bury me with my ancestors in the cave thats in the field of Ephron the Hittite;.
local: Ŋama nyaaŋa, Yakubu yeli o biisi, M jemani paagira, m di n gaari m yaadima saani. Ka m pa si n ti kpi, ya pi ma yi m badima noba see, a yi zovoori la n ba Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira tiŋgbaŋŋu la niŋi.

eng: in the cave thats in the field of Machpelah near Mamre in the land of Canaan that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite as a burial property.
local: Zovooriso n ba Makipela tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a leeli Mamiri, Keenan paaligu, so Ibrahim n daa n da, a yi Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira la saani, o niŋi o niriba piibu ziya la.

eng: That is where Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried, and where Isaac and his wife Rebekah are buried, and where I buried Leah.
local: Niŋi n na, ba daa n pi Ibrahim ni o poga Saara, a pi Yisaaka ni o poga Rabeeka, ka m gba pi m poga Liya niŋi la.

eng: It is the field and the cave in it that belonged to the Hittites.
local: Tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori maa zaa ba daa n da n yi Hititi niriba saani.

eng: After he finished giving orders to his sons, he put his feet up on the bed, took his last breath, and joined his people.
local: Yakubu n togsi n ŋa ti o biisi baasi, o laasi o noba du bindoori niŋi, ka vuusi o baasigu vuusim, a gaari o yaadima saani.

eng: Joseph fell across his fathers body, wept over him, and kissed him.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu li n pa o ba niŋi, a kumi, ka mugisi u.

eng: Joseph then ordered the physicians in his service to embalm his father, and the physicians embalmed Israel.
local: Yisifu yeli o dokitesi, ni ba naa tiim niŋi o ba Yakubu, o da saami. Dokitesi maa niŋi lala.

eng: They mourned for him forty days because that is the period required for embalming. Then the Egyptians mourned him for seventy days.
local: ba naa dapisinaasi tumi lala tuma, seema n daga. Ka Yijipiti dima maa naa dapisiyapoin kumi o kuuri.

eng: After the period of mourning had passed, Joseph spoke to Pharaohs household: If you approve my request, give Pharaoh this message:.
local: Jemanso kuuri kuminisim n ti baasi, Yisifu yeli naaba maa kpemba, Ka ya n zo ma nimbaa, ya togsi naaba maa saani ti ma, ya yeli u.

eng: My father made me promise, telling me, Im about to die. You must bury me in the tomb I dug for myself in the land of Canaan. Now, let me leave and let me bury my father, and then I will return.
local: Poin ka m ba kpi, o ti ma digili noori maa, m pi u yi seela o n gbi o kaa, a yi Keenan paaligu. Jaande, ka m wa ti pi m ba, ka pori n chena.

eng: Pharaoh replied, Go, bury your father as you promised.
local: Naaba maa yeli u, Wa ti pi e ba, a maa seema o n ti e digili noori la.

eng: So Joseph left to bury his father. All of Pharaohs servants went with him, together with the elder statesmen in his household and all of the elder statesmen in the land of Egypt.
local: A zu, Yisifu gaa, o wa ti pi o ba. Nivudamta paag u gaa, Yijipiti naaba kpemba, a ni Yijipiti kpemba zaa.

eng: Josephs entire household, his brothers, and his fathers household. Only the children, flocks, and cattle remained in the land of Goshen.
local: a ni Yisifu laanta dima zaa, a ni o beya, a ni o ba laanta zaa. Ama, ba biisi ni peeri, buusi, a ni naagi ko n daa che Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

eng: Even chariots and horsemen went with him; it was a huge collection of people.
local: Turukusi ni binduurigusi dima zaa n paag u gaari. Zamutintaa n daa pa o zu gaari.

eng: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Atad on the other side of the Jordan River, they observed a solemn, deeply sorrowful period of mourning. He grieved seven days for his father.
local: Ba n paa Atadi chibuura gbantali, a leeli Joodan mogiri, ba chaari kpeeŋutiri, a ni sufusaaŋu. Nisaaniŋi n na, Yisifu naa dabaayapoin kumi o ba kuuri.

eng: After this happened, Joseph was told, Your father is getting weaker, so he took his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim with him.
local: blank.

eng: When the Canaanites who lived in the land saw the observance of grief on Atads threshing floor, they said, This is a solemn observance of grief by the Egyptians. Therefore, its name is Abel-mizraim. It is on the other side of the Jordan River.
local: Keenan nivuseesi n ba niŋi maa, ka nye ba n kumina kuuri maa, a yi Atadi gbantali niŋi, ba yeli, Yijipiti dima daankaari ni sufusaaŋu nimbirimoo kumsi n na la. A zu n na, ba boona nisaaniŋi Yijipiti dima kuuri kumsi.

eng: Israels sons did for him just as he had ordered.
local: Lala n na, Yakubu biisi niŋi seema ba ba n yeli ba.

eng: His sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, which Abraham had purchased as burial property from Ephron the Hittite.
local: Ba zi u gaa Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a wa ti pi u yi zovoori n ba Makipela tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a leeli Mamiri, so Ibrahim n daa n da, o niriba piibu ziya, a yi Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira saani, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu maa.

eng: Then Joseph returned to Egypt, he, his brothers, and everyone who left with him to bury his father.
local: Yisifu n pi o ba baasi, o ni o mabiisi ni seesi zaa n paa ba gaa la pori n gaa Yijipiti.

eng: When Josephs brothers realized that their father was now dead, they said, What if Joseph bears a grudge against us, and wants to pay us back seriously for all of the terrible things we did to him.
local: Ba ba kum nyaaŋa, Yisifu mabiisi la yeli taaba, Saŋa, Yisifu ni chisi ti, a yo ti yabeseesi ti n daa n niŋi u la samini.

eng: So they approached Joseph and said, Your father gave orders before he died, telling us.
local: A zu, ba tumi nira Yisifu saani, a wa ti yeli u. Seema e ba n yeli ti, ka kpi n ŋa.

eng: This is what you should say to Joseph. Please, forgive your brothers sins and misdeeds, for they did terrible things to you. Now, please forgive the sins of the servants of your fathers God. Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
local: Ya yeli Yisifu n ŋa: M suusira i, e che n paŋŋi e mabiisaa, yabeseesi ni namsigu seesi zaa ba n daa n niŋi i la. A zu, jaande, che n paŋŋi e ba Naawunni gurunsi ba yabeeri. Ba yatogiraa n paa Yisifu, o kumi.

eng: His brothers wept too, fell down in front of him, and said, Were here as your slaves.
local: Niŋi n na, o mabiisi maa ti li n do o yiya, ka yeli u, E daaba n niŋi ti.

eng: But Joseph said to them, Dont be afraid. Am I God?.
local: Yisifu yeli ba, Ya da zora dabeem, m bi niŋi Naawunni.

eng: You planned something bad for me, but God produced something good from it, in order to save the lives of many people, just as hes doing today.
local: Ya wune zani ya niŋi ma daaŋu, ama Naawunni wune naag u loobi binsuŋŋu, o ti niŋi bunso n niŋi moligirisim ti nivudamta nyevuya.

eng: Now, dont be afraid. I will take care of you and your children. So he put them at ease and spoke reassuringly to them.
local: Lala zu, ya da zora dabeem, m ni nyaasi ya ni ya biisi. O togsi yatogsuma ti ba, a maa ba sufusi.

eng: Thus Joseph lived in Egypt, he and his fathers household. Joseph lived years.
local: Yisifu daa n zinni Yijipiti, o ni o ba biisi zaa. O daa n dini yuma koowa ni piiya.

eng: and saw Ephraims grandchildren. The children of Machir, Manassehs son, were also born on Josephs knees.
local: O zi n nye Efirem biisi ni yaŋsi. Ka Manaasi bii Makiya gba daa n dogi biisi, ba naa ba pa Yisifu noba niŋi.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im about to die. God will certainly take care of you and bring you out of this land to the land he promised to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
local: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa, M kum jemani paaya. Ama, Naawunni ni paasi ya niŋi, a nyisi ya kpe gaa tiŋgbanso o n digili noori ti Ibrahim, Yisaaka ni Yakubu la niŋi.

eng: Joseph made Israels sons promise, When God takes care of you, you must bring up my bones out of here.
local: Yisifu ti Yakubu biisi pe, a yeli maa, Jemanso Naawunni n nyisi ya kpe, a wara tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ya naa m koba yi kpe paasi n wara.

eng: Joseph died when he was years old. They embalmed him and placed him in a coffin in Egypt.
local: Lala jemani maa, Yisifu daa n dini yuma koowa ni piiya, ka kpi. Ba niŋi tiim sariga u, o da saami zu, ka naag u niŋi daka puu yi Yijipiti.
